observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the
(a) Municipal Treasurer to exhaust all the possible measures to recover and collect the
dormant receivable accounts within the bounds of law prior to initiating any request for write-off
and/or disposition; and (b) Municipal Accountant to conduct a
(i) comprehensive evaluation of all dormant receivables, particularly those non-moving
for 10 years or more and effect proper disposition thereof, as warranted, in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, as amended by COA
Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and (ii) detailed collectability assessment
of dormant Other Receivable account prior to recognizing any allowance for
impairment in accordance with IPSAS 29.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the: (a) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all
Inventories and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI; (b) Acting Property
Officer to (i) prepare and maintain complete perpetual inventory records; and
(ii) analyze the semi-expendable inventory items included under the PPE accounts and
reclassify the PPE items that do not meet the P50,000.00 capitalization threshold
pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2022-004 and 2024-006; (c) Disposal Committee to fast
track the conduct inspection of all unserviceable properties, prepare and submit the
corresponding IIRUP to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items; and
(d) Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEV for the reclassification of PPE
accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the
Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-
002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation of
outstanding cash advances.

We also reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to (i) require immediate liquidation from employees with outstanding cash advances;
and (ii) strictly monitor the grant of cash advance by issuing certification that previous
cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books to prevent the grant
of additional cash advances to those officers and employees with outstanding cash
advance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the timely renewal
of fidelity bonds of AOs and that cash advances granted to AOs are within their
maximum cash accountability.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to conduct a
review and evaluation of long outstanding projects recorded under the CIP account to
determine their current status; (ii) reclassify completed and accepted projects to the
appropriate PPE accounts, supported by relevant documents, such as Certificates of
Completion and Inspection Reports, in accordance with Section 50 of the Manual on
NGAS; and (iii) prepare the necessary adjustments/Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
correct the actual balance of the CIP accounts in accordance with the definition of the
account set forth in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to: (i) review the composition of the Accounts payable and
ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required and necessary
documents to determine the validity and enforceability of claims; and (ii) revert to the
unappropriated surplus all accounts payable which have been outstanding for two
years or more and for which no valid claims have been established in compliance with
Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor to
require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) ensure the proper recording of affected
accounts upon collection and recognition of income; and (b) analyze the net effect of the
foregoing observations in the financial statements and draw the necessary adjusting
entries in the General and Special Education Fund books to reflect the correct and
reliable balance of the affected accounts, and submit to this Office the actions taken to
establish the correct balances of the subject receivable.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Treasurer to (i) exert more effort and undertake effective, efficient and
aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection by conducting massive tax
campaigns and consistent preparation and issuance of Notices of Delinquency for all
delinquent Real Property Units; (ii) monitor the payment of real property taxes due for
the current year and impose the available remedies and procedures as authorized under
the Local Government Code; (iii) for the delinquent real property taxes with NDs
already served, recommend to the Provincial Treasurer the imposition of the legal
remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or judicial action,
following the procedures authorized in the LGC, to enforce collection and to undertake
the same expeditiously on a yearly basis to reduce amounts accumulated as receivables
for delinquent taxes; and (iv) conduct dialogue with owners/operators different from
the records on file, as well as with the representatives of BFAR particularly for salt
farm and fishpond operators, to address concerns on the collection of taxes. If
warranted, request the presence of the Provincial Treasurer and Provincial Legal
Officer to assist in such undertaking.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Heads of the Municipal Offices to review the DTRs and accomplishment reports
of the contractual and/or job order personnel under their respective offices
before approving the same to ensure that pertinent supporting documents are
duly accomplished; and
b. Human Resource Management Officer to carefully monitor and check the actual
services rendered in the DTRs and reconcile the same with the payrolls, to avoid
overpayments.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments to
the contractual and/or job order personnel are supported with documents with all the
required information duly indicated in the prescribed form, in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payment, to establish the propriety,
regularity and validity of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Acting Chief of Police to
ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint
Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the entitlement, release, use, reporting
and audit of Confidential and/or Intelligence Funds.

We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor remind the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements are properly
and completely documented and submitted promptly in accordance with Sections
6.1.8.4, 6.3.1 (f), 6.1.7 (c-f) and 6.1.8.3 (a-d) of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint
Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the:

a. BAC and its TWG to: (i) revisit the updated guidelines and provisions of the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolutions to ensure that all
procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in
accordance with the pertinent provisions of the updated 2016 Revised IRR of
RA No. 9184; (ii) be cautious and exercise prudence in the examination of
eligibility and technical documents submitted by the prospective bidders in
faithful adherence to relevant provisions of the updated 2016 Revised IRR of
RA No. 9184 to ensure the award of contract to eligible
contractors/suppliers; (iii) immediately submit the lacking bidding
documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit; and (iv) strictly
comply with the prescribed period of furnishing the COA Auditor copies of
contracts including the documentary requirements to facilitate timely review
and evaluation as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February
12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
b. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting
documents noted and ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper
and complete supporting documents, as required under COA Circular No.
2012-001, before processing claims for payment; and
c. Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure compliance with DPWH Department
Order No. 197 s. 2016 on the revised guidelines in the preparation of ABC;
and (ii) ensure that photos taken as visual evidence for the actual
accomplishment of the projects were geo-tagged.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, through the
MSWMB and MPDC, to: (i) hasten the compliance with the latest comments and
recommendations of the EMB to secure an approved 10-year SWMP to ensure that
priority projects concerning Solid Waste Management are in accordance with the
National Solid Waste Management Framework; and (ii) convert the existing open
dumpsite into a controlled dumpsite to mitigate the health risks to proximate
communities and damages to the environment and enforce proper sorting, segregation,
composting, recycling and disposal of waste materials to efficiently address solid waste
concerns within the Municipality.

The Audit Team recognizes the efforts of the Municipality to comply with the
requirements of the law on Gender and Development, however, we reiterated our prior
year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor (i) require the Chairperson, GFPS
TWG and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure reconciliation between their
respective records/reports to reflect accurate and reliable data/information, mitigating
confusion for the users of these reports; and (ii) formally notify the concerned DILG
Office of any changes or revisions made to the DILG-endorsed GPB to warrant
consistency and verifiability on the related reports.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The accounting and the GSO ensure that inventory records such as the PPE Ledger Card and Property Card, whichever is applicable, are properly maintained and updated regularly for all items of PPE. Immediately record the receipts and issuances of PPE items in their respective inventory records to avoid accumulation of unrecorded transactions at year-end.
The Inventory Committee conducts the physical inventory count of PPE once a year, as at December 31 of each year and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE as follows:
Original copy		- Accounting Unit
Duplicate copy	- COA Auditor
Triplicate copy	- Supply and Property Unit
Quadruplicate copy	- Inventory Committee file
The Municipal Accountant provide sufficient disclosure on missing items still recorded in the books of the LGU and coordinate with the GSO on the status of these missing items for possible reversal of the same subject to applicable rules and regulation on the write-off of PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The accounting and the GSO ensure that inventory records such as the Supply Ledger Card, SPLC, Stock Card, SPC and RegSPI, whichever is applicable, are properly maintained and updated regularly for all items of inventory. Immediately record the receipts and issuances of inventory items in their respective inventory records to avoid accumulation of unrecorded transactions at year-end;
The GSO prepares and submits to the accounting office the SSMI/RSPI together with the original copy of the RIS on a weekly basis;
The accounting unit record the issuance of inventory items based on the SSMI/RSPI submitted by the general services office; and
The Inventory Committee conducts the physical inventory count of inventories twice a year, as at June 30 and as at December 31 of each year and submit the RPCI as follows:
Original copy		- Accounting Unit
Duplicate copy	- COA Auditor
Triplicate copy		- Supply and Property Unit
Quadruplicate copy	- Inventory Committee file

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the amounts granted under the Donations account to Due from Local Government Units;
Require the recipient barangays the submission of liquidation reports to ascertain that funds were utilized in accordance with the intended purpose.
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that the subsequent fund transfers to be granted by the Municipality to the barangays under the 20% DF, the documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 such as the Memorandum of Agreement, copy of OR from the implementing agency and copy of approved program of works/project expenditures be attached; and
To include in the MOA, the submission of a liquidation report for accountability and audit purposes.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to the following:
Reclassify the affected accounts to their appropriate receivable accounts in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher to the Audit Team; and
Strengthen the review and validation process of financial transactions to ensure accurate reporting, proper classification and correct recording in their designated accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to the following:
Ensure that recording of expenditures under the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account be made only if the transaction cannot be appropriately classified within any other designated category in the MOOE expense classification; and
Strengthen the review and validation process of financial transactions to ensure accurate reporting, proper classification and correct recording of expenditures in their designated accounts.

We recommended the following to Management:
Justify in writing the implementation of the reprogrammed projects despite absence of a Supplemental AIP approved by the SB and absence of a Supplemental Budget approved through a local ordinance;
MDC to prepare a supplemental investment program to be approved by the local sanggunian; and
Ensure that any changes in the budget like reprogramming of existing appropriation to fund a new project and/or augmenting any item in the approved annual budget from savings in other item of another expense class be supported by a Supplemental Budget approved through a Local Ordinance.

We recommend the following to management:
Submit a written justification as to the implementation of the “renovation/improvement of two units two-storey economic housing” despite absence of the necessary authority from the MDC and/or the Sangguniang Bayan, citing therein any legal basis for the same; and
The MPDC to secure an authority from the SB with recommendation from the MDC for any changes in the PPAs to be implemented under the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, MPDC, and MDC properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion. Ensure that necessary requirements existing during the planning phase that will deter the implementation of a particular project be acted or settled first before including the same in the list of projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The LSB ensure that charges to the SEF be among those authorized under the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC; and
The Municipal Budget Officer review the items in the Obligation Request to ensure that expenditure items therein are with available appropriation that is compliant to DepEd-DBM-DILG JC, particularly on the allowable expenditure items chargeable to SEF.
We recommended and the LSB agreed that moving forward, ECCD key program initiatives be included in the SEF Budget allocation.

We recommended and Management agreed that, on a regular basis:
Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004;
Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted in accordance with Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

We recommend and Management agreed to the following:
Ensure compliance with the required posting of tarpaulin signboards at the project site; and
Regularly update the information in the respective project signboards in accordance with item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to provide the public the essential project information.
We recommended that the MBO ensure to maintain RAAOs as prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs and keep abreast of issuances/circulars/laws relative to their functions.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Require all department heads to adhere to the proper procurement process and discontinue the practice of “procurement by office.”
Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to:
Maintain Supplies Stock Cards and issue Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) for all inventory issued to end-users; and
Prepare and submit the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to the Accounting Office to facilitate the proper recognition of expenses.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain Supply Ledger Cards (SLCs) and ensure their reconciliation with the GSO’s records.
Mandate the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct the semi-annual physical inventory count as required.
Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following office heads:
The Municipal Budget Officer
To ensure that the Obligation Request is completely filled up; and
To certify the Obligation Request if covered with appropriation.
The Municipal Accountant
Refrain from charging prior years’ expenses against the current year’s appropriation;
Issue a Memorandum Order at year-end setting the deadline for submitting required documents for the obligation of expenditures;
If the incurrence of expenses during year-ends cannot be dispensed due to the exigency of service, unpaid obligations shall be recorded as payables to validate their payments in the succeeding years; and
Consolidate all unobligated expenses with appropriate justifications and submit them to the Office of the Auditor for further recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review and correction of the affected entries and observe correct and proper account names or titles in the preparation of accounting entries.
Moreover, hire a Certified Public Accountant or implement additional training programs for accounting personnel, particularly in the proper application of the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the amount of P8,899,863.18 from Non-Current Loans Payable to the Current Portion of Loans Payable- Domestic and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the improper recording of interest expense amounting to P335,118.90.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements of all funds and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income, staled checks and unrecorded deposits to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance;
Refund the P4,800.00 service charges as these represent unnecessary expenses otherwise and a Notice of Disallowance will be issued; and
Close the affected accounts and return the remaining balances to the source agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough reconciliation of the Financial Statements and General Ledgers to ensure the accurate recording of payables and remittances and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct any discrepancies or abnormal balances.
Conduct a thorough audit and evaluation of the ECPAC system to identify and resolve technical issues. If the system is found to be persistently problematic and inefficient, consider transitioning to a more reliable and advanced accounting system that ensures accuracy, compliance, and seamless financial management.
Strictly comply with remittance schedules for GSIS, BIR, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid penalties and surcharges.
Require the concerned employee(s) to refund the P72,082.52 penalty incurred due to late GSIS remittances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the following actions:
a. Direct the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to:
Specify the details of identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund, including project locations, specific implementation schedules, and physical targets, to avoid the use of generic or lump-sum projects.
Discontinue the practice of implementing generic or lump-sum projects without further authorization from the Sanggunian, to prevent suspension or disallowance during audit.
b. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that all Programs/Projects/Activities in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget are included in the Annual Procurement Plan. Additionally, ensure that the Municipality complies with the national policy on the Full Disclosure of Local Budget and Finances, and Bids and Public Offerings, as mandated under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-141, dated August 9, 2012.
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Realign improperly charged expenses to the appropriate accounts, such as the General Fund, and ensure that public funds are properly allocated in accordance with their intended purposes.

We recommended to Local Chief Executive the following measures, in accordance with Sections 76 and 77, Title III of RA 7160, and CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017:
Expedite the hiring process for key department head positions such as the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Administrator;
Prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions by hiring new employees or expedite promotion of deserving employees to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the Municipality for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community; and
Prioritize the existing Contract of Service or Job Order workers in the appointment to the vacant positions provided that such workers meet the appropriate eligibility and other qualification requirements for the position subject to existing Civil Service law and rules.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GPB and GAD AR within the prescribed period to facilitate the timely evaluation and validation of the GAD PPAs; and
Create a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System through the issuance of an executive order to ensure that GAD activities are properly monitored and that gender-related initiatives truly benefit the community.

We recommended, and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed, to immediately submit the perfected contracts along with the complete supporting documents for the aforementioned projects/activities. Furthermore, strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, will be observed.

1.8 We recommended and it was agreed that both the Accounting and Property Divisions exert extra efforts to reconcile their records and avail and/or begin the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.
1.9 We also recommended and for which was also agreed by management to provide timeline to complete this process.

2.8 We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the completed projects, and regularly monitor the CIP accounts to ensure that these are transferred to their appropriate asset accounts when these become completed and readily available.

3.13 We recommended and it was agreed that the LGU of Magsaysay strictly comply with the provisions of the aforementioned DSWD Memorandum Circular mandating the engagement with the National Food Authority (NFA) for rice procurement.
3.14  Furthermore, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should thoroughly verify the legal documents of suppliers to confirm that their line of business, as stated in their Mayor’s Permit and BIR Certificate of Registration, aligns with the requirements of the procurement project.

5.8 We recommended and that management agreed, through its Municipal Accountant, should implement the required employee’s net take home pay of not less than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000) per month as required under Section 56 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriation Act (GAA).

6.9 We recommended and it was agreed by Management that (a) claims of RATA be supported with the required documents in order to have a basis in determining whether the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations were observed; and (b) the Municipal Accountant see to it that the claims for RATA are supported with complete documentation before certifying and effecting payment to prevent any audit suspension/disallowance.
Submit the schedule of dormant accounts quarterly on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month; and
Consider the preparation of a request for write-off of the receivable accounts which were considered dormant pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-08.

For Cash Advances for Confidential Funds which were already liquidated, we recommended management to make the necessary follow-up to COA-ICFAU duly supported with a copy of the transmittal letters for the issuance of the Credit Notice.

In addition, for the unsettled disallowance of Ms. Dumoloy, management to make the necessary follow-up and/or effect deductions from any claim against the LGU accruing to the benefit of the former to settle the disallowance.

We recommended that Management direct its Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and for the latter to prepare the necessary adjusting entries where appropriate, for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor, Municipal General Services Officer, and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records on the land owned by the LGU as against those recorded in the books.

We recommended that Management conduct inspection, appraisal and disposition of unserviceable properties in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386 so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management, in observance of the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 pertaining to missing/non-existing property, require the Inventory Committee to identify with finality the officers/personnel who are accountable for these properties, and demand the production of the same. Further, the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs be strictly implemented in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to facilitate the proper disposal of and dropping from the books of accounts of the agency.

direct the Municipal General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS and submit the PIF to the GSIS.

Management provide justification as to why funds were transferred despite clear provision of the law; and

Locate the OR/CRs of the motor vehicles that have no ORs/CRs on file. If warranted, Management coordinate with the LTO for the issuance of a duplicate OR/CR of the vehicle;

We recommended the BAC Chairman to submit the lacking supporting documents and adhere to the provisions cited pursuant to Section 4.6 of P.D 1445, Section 5.1 of Budget Circular No. 20014-5A dated October 7, 2005 and COA No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: As of reporting date, lacking supporting documents remained not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Auditor all the unsubmitted DVs for CY 2022 with complete supporting documents including the DVs for cash advances for post audit purposes; and submit written justification explaining therein why cash advances were not covered with DVs, and specific legal purpose was not reflected in the RCIs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to discontinue processing of claims/payments of a cash advance without specific legal purpose;
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management the following courses of actions: 

Stop the practice of shouldering taxes for procurements made through reimbursements and/or special cash advances;
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Minimize transactions pertaining to reimbursements through proper procurement planning; and
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: Strictly avoid incurring irregular and unnecessary expenses as provided under COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: 3. Institutionalize the GAD Database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the basis for gender analysis, planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation;
4. Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificate of Title of the land owned by the Municipality;
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor’s Office is still in the process of preparing and gathering the necessary documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents for the purchase of land and ensure that future procurement of land should not be made through cash advance;
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Land accounts recorded in the books, which were not supported with TCT and other legal documents of ownership, should be properly disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: For CY 2023, no disclosures were made in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the concerned officers and employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances immediately;
observations.reason: Partial liquidations were made to the outstanding cash advances noted.

observations.recommendation: Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the liquidation and refund of cash advances.
observations.reason: Partial liquidations were made to the outstanding cash advances noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to the following courses of action: 

Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of GPPB Resolution No.09-2009 and IRR of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of goods if adopting shopping/small value procurement as a mode of procurement.
Submit complete documentary requirements for all transactions as prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking supporting documents were already submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to the following courses of action: 

Require the submission of lacking documentary requirements for travels by all personnel indicated in Appendix H in accordance with COA Circular No. 2021-001, otherwise, direct refund of travels claimed.
observations.reason: Some of the lacking documentary requirements for travels were already submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Liquidating Officer/Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all her collections as well as those remitted to her will be deposited intact and daily or in the frequency prescribed by regulation;
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Collector/Cashier to turn over her collections intact and daily to the Liquidating Officer at the frequency required under existing regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following corrective measures to address existing weak points in collection such as but not limited to:

Direct all Accountable Officers/Revenue Collectors and Liquidating Officer to prepare and submit the monthly Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and the Consolidated RAAF on a monthly basis;
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector and check the same against new balances on hand column.  Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Adopt a regular monitoring system and/or closer supervision by a responsible officer such that the issuance of official receipts/cash tickets is consistently made; the possibility of non-issuance, recycling or misuse is minimized if not eradicated.
observations.managementAction: The Management is already planning to avail the services of the ETRACS System.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Acting Municipal Treasurer the following:

Stop the practice of using cash advances to pay for the Municipality’s operating expenses and activities;
observations.managementAction: No management action was taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that payments for suppliers are made thru checks and consider establishing a petty cash fund to address petty expenditures of the LGU;
observations.managementAction: No management action was taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that the purposes of cash advances are on the face of the disbursement vouchers and that are duly supported with complete documents at the time of grant of cash advances;
observations.managementAction: The purposes of the cash advances were already reflected in the face of the Disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: Still, with lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Discontinue the practice of granting multiple cash advances to the Acting Municipal Treasurer and ensure that her previous cash advance has been liquidated before granting additional cash advance; and
observations.managementAction: No management action was taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Direct each Department Head of the LGU to ensure PPMPS is not limited to common office supplies but that all programs, projects and activities of their respective offices are included therein.
observations.reason: There are still programs and activities which were not included in the PPMP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the BAC to prioritize and submit all lacking supporting documents of the subject contracts immediately and to submit prospective contracts within 5 days upon their perfection in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001;
observations.reason: Some of the lacking supporting documents were already submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: Direct the BAC Secretariat to publish in the Phil-GEPS website and in the agency website the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed for the procurement done through public bidding and all instances of alternative modes of procurement, except for contracts with ABC of ₱50,000.00 and below;
observations.reason: Still, the Notices To Proceed and Notices Of Award were not posted in the Phil-GEPS website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive directs the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and all concerned officials to properly and completely accomplish relevant procurement documents such as PR, RFQ and PO and provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement projects to serve as a reference during inspection and acceptance of deliveries by management.
observations.reason: There are still Purchase Requests, Request for Quotations and Purchase Orders with incomplete information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive requires the Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the COA Audit Team copies of AIRs within twenty-four (24) hours after the acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the personnel concerned on other items so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.
observations.reason: Acceptance and Inspection Reports were submitted in bulk to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Inventory and Inspection Report, photographs if any, Waste Material Report and to ensure that strict adherence to disposal of assets outlined in COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 shall be observed.
observations.reason: Some of the required documents were already submitted to audit team.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC Secretariat to transmit all contracts pending for submission immediately and to ensure that prospective contracts will be submitted within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract;
observations.reason: Some of the contracts were already submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Certificate of Project Completion and Certificate of Acceptance for completed projects and to request for its technical inspection from COA.
observations.reason: Some of the required documents were already submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented for the year will be submitted every beginning of the year; to inform COA within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and to accomplish properly the quarterly report on projects, programs and activities.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer still failed to inform COA the list of infrastructure projects awarded and to be started for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit SAOB on a monthly basis and to ensure that the Obligation Requests are properly accomplished.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer already submitted in bulk the monthly SAAOB to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the designated MDDRMO and Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports, post the same to the appropriate website, bulletin board or conspicuous places, and submit the Annex C of DBM LBC Circular No. 125 to COA.
observations.reason: Management still failed to comply with the posting requirements of the BGCM Fund.

observations.managementAction: No concrete action was taken by the management to address this matter.
observations.reason: Management failed to submit the Reports required.

observations.recommendation: Increase the number of MRFs pursuant to Section 33 of RA 9003 in coordination with the barangays of the Municipality to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage.
observations.reason: Additional MRFs were already established in some of the barangays of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to secure the Program of Work of the subject disbursements and submit to COA.
observations.reason: Program of Work of the subject disbursements was still not submitted to COA

observations.recommendation: Require the Human Resource Management Officer to fill up vacant positions and adopt innovative ways to ensure there is a wider publication and posting;
observations.reason: There are still positions unfilled to date.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Local Chief Executive to ensure that employees who are designated to other positions do not hold incompatible duties/functions.
observations.reason: There are still positions unfilled to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management avail and/or begin the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.reason:
Training requirements and procedural know-how.

Further, for creation of committee and assignments of personnel.
Project is still on-going having an accomplishment of 81.06% as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to exhaust all possible means in the completion of the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE to prevent recurring issues of unreliable account balances.
observations.reason:
Training requirements and procedural know-how.

Further, for creation of committee and assignments of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to secure the required NTC permits and licenses of the Radio Station to operate pursuant to Sec 2 of RA 3846 for proper and legal discharge of communication during emergency situations.
observations.reason: On the process of securing NTC permit(s) and licenses of the Radio Station.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that the management instruct the accountant to provide depreciation in the books of accounts and adjust previous balances not provided with depreciation.  Facilitate the creation of an inventory team to conduct inventory of properties owned by the LGU for the purpose of having better accuracy in presenting items and balances in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Depreciation recognized were based on estimate due to difficulty in determining the specific item in the books and the details of its acquisition including the costs and exact date of acquisition. And one-time cleansing of PPE items are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that management instruct both offices to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards for properties owned and conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of the PPE accounts.  The differences per physical count and book balances must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze transactions including those pertaining to prior years relative to unidentified discrepancy and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts and furnish COA the corrections made therein.
observations.reason: The year end balances of reciprocal accounts, Due from other Funds and Due to other Funds, for all funds in the amount of ₱15,673,130.61 and ₱15,552,530.61, respectively, showed a total difference of ₱120,600.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
•	Prepare and submit on a monthly basis the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued duly supported with Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) to the Accounting Office for the preparation of the JEV to record in the books all the issuances made during the period.

•	Instruct the Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for each inventory stocks.

•	Prepare and submit the RPCI on an annual basis to the auditor.

We recommended that management strictly adhere with existing laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of LDRRMF and submit justification for the utilization of the fund in implementation of PPAs not included in the LDRRMFIP, AIP, and supplemental AIP.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department make the necessary adjusting entries to record the accrued liability pertaining to earned leave benefits of the employees for the current and previous years after ensuring the correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances as of December 31, 2019. Review at each reporting date and adjust to reflect the current best estimate to ensure that employee’s benefits are properly accounted for and disclosed in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management to make necessary adjustments to record the accrued liability.
observations.reason: Management have financial constraint in funding the liability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Accountant to recognize and transfer the Local Road Networks from the Public Infrastructure Registry to the books of accounts.  Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to revisit the map of the municipality’s local road networks and determine the proper cost for each local road through thorough review of records.
observations.managementAction: Management to determine serviceability of the remaining Road Networks and determine value of serviceable local roads.
observations.reason: Difficulty in determining local roads owned by the Municipality as road projects often overlapped with road projects of the National Government and Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct both offices to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards for properties owned and conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of the PPE accounts.  The differences per physical count and book balances must be investigated cleared and reconciled immediately.
observations.managementAction: Management to reconcile balances between the two funds through conduct of physical count and determination of serviceability of recorded inventory items
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report. Management was unable to conduct physical count and determine serviceability of recorded items.  Instead, ownership records (Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment) was updated but item status was not updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the corresponding elected officials to secure from the Philippines Councilor’s League, Incorporated, a government issued official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management to correspond with League of Councilors to demand Official Receipts for the registration thereon.
observations.reason: Ongoing deliberation with courts regarding status of the League and the requirement to issue Official Receipts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to prepare the LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness in the DRRM Investment Plan pursuant to 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommend that Management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and Utilization Report to the auditors’ office.
observations.reason: Monthly utilization report not submitted to auditor’s office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management closely monitor the receivables and intensify its collection, otherwise, file the appropriate request for authority to write-off for the dormant accounts duly supported with the relevant documents pursuant to Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand liquidation from LGUs.
observations.reason: Changes in barangay personnel and difficulty in obtaining records on the barangay level.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management strictly require the recipient NGOs/POs to submit the duly certified final Utilization Report to support its liquidation pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001. Issue demand letter to the NGOs/POs. If no settlement is made, institute legal sanctions pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations applicable for violation of the provisions of the above COA Circular.

We also recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers releasing fund transfers to the same NGOs/POs unless the previous ones are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand liquidation from NGOs/PO.  Pursue writeoff of long outstanding accounts.
observations.reason: Difficulty in serving demand letters to NGOs/POs involved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the Schedule of Dormant Accounts quarterly on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month; and
Consider the preparation of a request for write-off of the Receivable accounts which were considered dormant pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008.
For Cash Advances for Confidential Funds which were already liquidated, we recommended management to make the necessary follow-up to COA-ICFAU duly supported with a copy of the transmittal letters for the issuance of the Credit Notice.
In addition, for the unsettled disallowance of Ms. Dumoloy, management to make the necessary follow-up and/or effect deductions from any claim against the LGU accruing to the benefit of the former to settle the disallowance.
We would like to emphasize that the Circular shall not be interpreted to condone the accounts written-off nor to extinguish the obligations. The Management shall continue to exert effort to collect the accounts appearing in the Registry of Accounts Written-Off (Annex 10) or Registry of Derecognized Dormant Accounts (Annex 11) when circumstances warrant. Further, this shall not be construed as a ground to exonerate from liability the officers/employees for infidelity in the custody of documents.

We recommended that Management direct its Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and for the latter to prepare the necessary adjusting entries where appropriate, for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
Furthermore, instruct the custodians to maintain Stock Card for each type of supply to record all receipts and issuances, taking into account the correct quantity of receipts from the approved Purchase Order/contracts and quantity received per Deliver Receipt and Acceptance and Inspection Report. Likewise, regularly update the stock card of each item for the issuances made based on a summary of issuances made on a daily basis, and each time items are received to properly monitor against under/over stocking of supplies, and to establish control against theft or abuse of the inventory items.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor, Municipal General Services Officer, and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records on the land owned by the LGU as against those recorded in the books. Furthermore, fast track the transfer of ownership of the land in the name of the LGU to avoid the risk to possible squatters, unlawful occupants, reclaim by heirs and losing all of the improvements made on the properties to those who can prove rightful ownership thereto.

We recommended that Management conduct inspection, appraisal and disposition of unserviceable properties in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386 so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management, in observance of the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 pertaining to missing/non-existing property, require the Inventory Committee to identify with finality the officers/personnel who are accountable for these properties, and demand the production of the same. Further, the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs be strictly implemented in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to facilitate the proper disposal of and dropping from the books of accounts of the agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the required Certificate of Completion of the completed projects to the Municipal Accountant for the adjustment/closing of the Construction in Progress accounts to its appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS and submit the PIF to the GSIS; and,
direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to set up financial assistance to NGOs/POs/CSOs as a receivable account under the “Due from NGOs/POs” account requiring liquidation documents to settle the fund transfer.

We recommended that management direct:
The Municipal Accountant, before effecting payment on the procurement of drugs and medicines, to require the Suppliers to submit the certifications/ documents as listed in RA 9502 and COA Circular No. 2012-001;
During post-qualification, the BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) to require the Responsive Bidder to submit the Certificate of Product Registration of each drug/medicine being procured to ensure that these are duly registered with the DOH-FDA; and,
The Municipal Health Officer to submit a certification that the drugs and medicines being procured are based on the PNDF Vol. I, current edition in full compliance with RA 9502 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
direct the LDRRMO to prepare a revised LDRRMP to support the release and use of LDRRMF and as basis by the MPDO for inclusion in the AIP; and,
the Municipal Accountant ensure that all LDRRMF utilizations are supported with LDRRMFIP and AIP/Supplemental AIP and with appropriate funding source.

We recommended the following:
Management provide justification as to why funds were transferred despite clear provision of the law; and
Management stop the transfer of funds out of the STF unless the five-year period has lapsed.

We recommended the following to the Management:
The Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer provide written justification, noted by the Municipal Mayor, as to why Management did not ensure the availability of the appropriation in the General Fund prior to making the disbursements or to let the Special Trust Fund pay to the Provincial Government instead, the failure to consider those resulted in an illegal transaction;
The Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer provide written justification, noted by the Municipal Mayor, as to why there was no disbursement vouchers covering the two transactions which resulted in an irregular transaction; and
Management stop the practice of transferring funds from one fund to another without a covering disbursement voucher.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant enter in the books of account the receivable of the LGU share from RAMEMCO;
The Municipal Treasurer demand the collection of the LGU share from RAMEMCO; and
RAMEMCO to submit the Financial Statements of the Joint Venture from CY 2013 to CY 2020 together with the Notes to the Financial Statements. Likewise, RAMEMCO pay the LGU the differential as a result of the under-remittance.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Appropriate a budget and consider the procurement of a computerized system or Enhance Tax Revenue and Collection System (ETRACS) for the collection and assessment of real property and business activities.
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
Prepare immediately the Disbursement Vouchers for the release of the prior year’s payable on RPT shares to the twenty-four (24) Barangays and ensure that subsequent releases of the same are made within five days after the end of each quarter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman/Secretariat to furnish the COA Office with copies of the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) working days after approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee establish and adopt appropriate measures and controls, not solely to rely on the availability of appropriations/allotments but the cash availability as well. Emergency austerity measures should also be applied to cover up existing cash overdraft at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
BAC and the Municipal Engineer to submit a plausible explanation to the Audit Team on the necessity of renting heavy equipment despite the ownership of the same unit by the Municipality;
BAC to submit the following to the Audit Team:
Validation report from the Department of Public Works and Highways
Evaluation and Equipment Utilization Report
Approved contracts with complete supporting documents on the procurement of materials and heavy equipment rentals of the above-mentioned infrastructure projects
Acting Municipal Accountant to check the adequacy, propriety and completeness of supporting documents prescribed under Sections 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payments to ensure compliance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements;
Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking supporting documents on the liquidation of cash advances and stop the practice of charging project-related expenses to cash advances. These payments should be made directly to the concerned contractor/supplier.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to maintain special account with complete set of subsidiary ledgers and prepare Financial Statements at year-end for each LEEs as prescribed in Section 313 of RA 7160 and to the pertinent provisions of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs so that the management would be informed of the financial position and condition of the special accounts that would assist them in making sound economic decisions.

We recommended that Management direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:
Submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers and observe the prescribed period for the submission of DVs to COA;
Refrain from processing claims that are incomplete of supporting documents; and
Keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balances and other financial reports as required under Sections 122 and 127 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended that Management require the responsible personnel to reconcile the unpaid GSIS deductions and remit all withheld premium contributions and loan payments to the GSIS within the timeline set to avoid penalties/interests, and to enable employees to enjoy the privileges and benefits accruing to members and impose sanctions on employees responsible for the non-remittance thereof.
Also, we recommend a refund in the amount of P3,940.32 by the employees directly responsible for the cause of the delay in the GSIS remittance improperly charged to the government funds.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant implement the one-time cleansing of PPE, record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, and submit the required reports to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer comply with the monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as required under COA Circular No. 2012–002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry to record the consumed fuel, oil and lubricant consumed and/or withdrawn from the various gasoline stations during the year 2023; and,
To stop the practice of making advance payment for procurement of fuel in compliance with the aforementioned rules and regulations which prohibit advance payments for government contracts.

We have recommended that the Municipality discontinue the aforementioned practice as this is susceptible to fraudulent and/or anomaly in transaction(s). Otherwise, repetitive acts or any future discrepancies in payroll transaction(s) will be disallowed in audit.
The Disbursing Officer or responsible officials/employees to require the representative or “other than those authorized payees” to present/submit authorization letter prior to the release/claim of payroll, if any.
Further, submit valid justification why the aforesaid deficiency occurred for auditor’s review and evaluation.

We have recommended that Management effect payment of meals and snacks through a check payable to the supplier/creditor except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent and cannot be conveniently paid by check and discourage the practice of disbursing such items through cash advance for payroll unless it is petty in nature.
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to conduct competitive public bidding or any alternative mode on the procurement of meals and snacks for various activities of the Municipality as prescribed in RA 9184 and its IRR.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office maintain a repair and maintenance record/history of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipality to avoid unnecessary repair and maintenance expenses. We suggest that a repair history form be devised, indicating the type of repair undertaken, the date when it was made, and other pertinent data for the information and guidance of the COA, the agency, and others concerned.
We further recommended that the repair and maintenance history/record be attached to the disbursement vouchers for repairs and maintenance expenses as supporting documents.

We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal/Project/ Resident Engineers to strengthen the monitoring and supervision of the overall project implementation, formulate strategies/measures, and exert the best efforts to timely implement and complete the projects.
Compute and impose liquidated damages of the aforementioned projects, copy furnish both the Accounting Department and COA.
We further recommended that management closely monitor strict compliance with the provision of the Construction Safety and Health Program and adhere to existing laws and regulations relative thereto to prevent potential construction risk to avoid disallowance of the amount.

We recommended that Management conduct periodic and continuous monitoring and review of the implementation of strategies of its special accounts, Waterworks System and Motorpool, with the end view that the desired results are achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income with the List of Delinquent Taxpayers prepared by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Position of the Municipality are fairly presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:

a) The Municipal GSO submit the Report on Local Road Network using the template provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and complete the necessary information in every field; 

b) The Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, provide the cost segregation of every road network owned by the Municipality per road component for the prior years and submit the data to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO; and,

c) The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal GSO, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the required disclosures on the Road Network System.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to (a) immediately prepare the necessary adjusting JEVs to reclassify the accounts; and (b) to establish mechanism to ensure that transactions are recorded in an appropriate account classification.
We recommended that Management direct the MEEDO Manager to demand the immediate payment of the delinquent accounts for IBJT stalls and water rental.

We recommended that management strictly comply with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, by taking into consideration the following:

a. Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the PPA, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; and,

b. Adopt strategies to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objectives of a desired quality of life for all.

We recommended the following:

1. Ensure that the MPDC regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time;

2. Conduct intensive review of the continuing projects funded under the 20 percent DF to determine if the PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality, otherwise, reprogram the identified PPAs to priority PPAs; and,

3. Realign or reprogram the savings from completed projects and unimplemented PPAs identified to Priority Development PPAs in accordance with the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that Management utilize and fully operationalize its recruitment process through its Human Resource Management Division (HRMD) to ensure that personnel who possess the qualification standards of funded plantilla positions are filled up to achieve the purpose/s for which funds are appropriated.  The Municipal HRMO to uphold the priority needs, desired service requirement and financial capacity of the Local Government consistent with the principles of efficiency, economy and effectiveness.

We recommended the following:

1. The BAC and the BAC Secretariat submit justification on the noted deficiencies as a result of the mode of procurement chosen;

2. The BAC be more observant with the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR most especially with regards to Direct Contracting; and

3. The Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents are complete before proceeding for payment.

We recommended the Management to:

a. Use the required format of a sequentially numbered trip ticket duly approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete information being filled out in every detail needed to support the claims for payment of fuel expenses.

b. Direct the authorized personnel to reconcile every month the driver’s trip tickets with the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report and Summary of Monthly Report of Official Travels as mandated under COA Circular No. 77-61 D.6 to determine whether the said fuel usage is valid and is being used appropriately.

c. Install controls on the monitoring of issuance of the fuel and strictly adhere to the guidelines on the use of government vehicles.

We recommended the following corrective measures for the aforesaid deficiencies:

a. Strict compliance with DBM BC No. 2016-2 in the computation of monetization of leave credits; and

b. Strictly comply with Section 22 of the Civil Service Memorandum Circular No. 41, series of 1998 and the Civil Service Resolution No. 000034.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal Department Heads refund the overtime pay given which is contrary to CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 01 
s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that the MPDC and the Focal Person - Children establish properly the disaggregated database which is monitored and updated yearly to come up with more responsive child-centric PPAs that will help attain the issues and concerns.

We recommended the following:

1. The Committee submits proof of the following:

a. That LGU-Ramon Magsaysay has achieved at least an average of 70 percent of all the targets under their PPAs as budgeted in CY 2021;

b. Proof of review of the agency’s financial records; and,

c. The written resolution indicating the MOOE Allotments that had become available as a result of cost-cutting and systems improvement measures and the internal guidelines governing the grant of CNA.

2. Municipal Mayor to stop the practice of granting CNA Incentive to its Municipal Officials and Employees until the policy and procedural guidelines on the granting of CNA Incentives for LGU RM are already complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1. Create a special committee, headed by the Municipal Accountant, to craft the internal rules on the use of digital signature or other types of electronic signature on electronic documents. The internal rules shall include provisions with regards to the sanctions for unauthorized and illegal use of digital certificates or electronic signatures; and

2. Submit the Management Representation or Policy Statement on the use of signature on electronic documents in their operations to their respective Auditors, together with the approved internal rules.

We recommended that the municipality immediately comply with the existing regulations for the creation and fill up the plantilla positions under the office of the MDRRM required to perform the functions of administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning in accordance with the RA 10121 to ensure that disaster risk reduction and management programs, projects, and activities will be implemented in an effective, efficient, and economical manner.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Issue an Executive Order creating an Inventory and Appraisal Committee of the Municipality;
observations.managementAction: The LCE has already issued an Executive Order creating a new Inventory and Appraisal Team

observations.recommendation: Prepare the Report on the Local Road Network and Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks in the prescribed format.

Further, we requested the Municipal Mayor to assign, designate or appoint qualified personnel to be the General Service Officer, instead of the Municipal Budget Officer, so that the preparation and maintenance of essential reports such as property/stock cards pertaining to the Municipality’s properties will be well taken cared of.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account and properly disclose the said accounts in the Notes to the Financial Statements to provide information to the users and for a fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements.
Prepare immediately the Disbursement Vouchers for the release of the prior year’s payable on RPT shares to the twenty-four (24) Barangays; and

Further, we requested that the Municipal Mayor designate or appoint another qualified person to be the General Service Officer, instead of the Municipal Budget Officer, so that the preparation and maintenance of essential reports such as property/stock cards will be well taken care of.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting entries to reclassify expenses that were erroneously recorded to EME account.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to prepare the duly approved Procurement Monitoring Report, and to submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to conduct an assessment together with other concerned personnel, the condition/causes/lapses on the delayed submission and/or non-submission of reports and documents and adopt effective and efficient measures to fasten submission thereof; and

Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure the withholding of taxes on all its payments to suppliers/sellers who are subject to 1% or 2% EWT, 5% GVAT and 3% GPT and remit the same to the BIR on time;
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to constitute its GFPS in conformity with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-JMC 2013-01 in its creation or establishment.

We recommended that the Management implement the following measures in compliance with Section 36 (C), Chapter VI of the IRR of RA No. 9710 and Section 4.1.B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01:

Gather and process sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information from its barangays and from various sources as identified in its GAD Code;
2. Consolidate the same for data processing, data analysis and data updating and maintenance of their GAD databases;

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive shall direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to stop recording/recognizing cash advances as an outright expense; to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each cash advance to ensure accuracy and correctness; to prepare adjusting journal entries to establish cash advance previously recognized as expense amounting to P1,231,842.05.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Accountant has already prepared the correcting entries to the subject cash advances which were erroneously recorded as expenses.

observations.recommendation: Require revision of IT by those who claimed for actual costs of gasoline and actual meals and recompute for allowable traveling expenses using the current rate to establish if traveling expenses claimed were not excessive, otherwise, direct refund of the excess.
Collecting Officers/ other employees handling cash should be provided with a safety deposit box and the combination code should be filed with the authorized official.
Advise management headed by the Local Chief Executive to revisit the internal control policies over LGU operations to identify and address these recurring lapses over the submission of contracts;

Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to reconcile these dormant accounts to its related projects to establish whether the remaining balance represents LGU equity or that from the source/funding agency so that appropriate actions will be undertaken;
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to secure an SB Resolution authorizing the closing of dormant accounts.
Ensure that programs, projects and activities are responsive to the needs for integration in the local development plan;
observations.managementAction: No concrete action was taken by the management to address this matter.
