observations.recommendation: Direct the Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the monitoring of the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan, closure of the open dumpsite, establishment of a sanitary landfill, and strict compliance of segregation of wastes under all its jurisdiction starting on LGU premises; and

Direct the Local School Board to prepare and submit the Annual SEF Budget with the prescribed form and timeline pursuant to the new Joint Circular Dep Ed-DBM and DILG No. 1 s. 2017 and to ensure that SEF funds are released and utilized pursuant to Section 272 of RA 7160; and

We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of granting monthly travel allowance to barangay captain officials since there is no existing rules and regulations allowing such. Else, such transaction will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended to Management that all perfected contracts and purchase orders are submitted to COA within five days after the perfection thereof for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, the conduct of inspection, and other necessary audit procedures. The copies to be furnished to the Audit Team should be separate from the ones being appended to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of bypassing deliveries/inspections and require the members of the Inspectorate Team to inspect and verify delivered items as to quantity and conformity with specifications based on the Delivery Receipt and approved Purchase Order (PO) prior to issuance to end-user/requesting party.

Further, require also the Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the IARs within twenty-four (24) hours after the acceptance of all deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.

We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of bloating project’s work accomplishment for progress billing purposes and approving payment on those projects with an actual work accomplishment is less than the progress billing claimed by contractor; and,
The Municipal Engineer to strictly supervise and monitor infrastructure projects by contract in order to attain target completion date as stipulated in the contract agreement, thus, likewise provide ample time in the processing of final payment and meet the liquidation deadline set by source funding agency.

We have recommended that Management adhere to the provision under Section 62.1 of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184. Hence, direct the Municipal Accountant not to process a claim/payment on the purchase of heavy equipment without securing the warranty security either retention money or a special bank guarantee, and shall only be released after the lapse of the warranty period.

Hereafter, we highly encourage the Management to procure only brand-new motor vehicles and/or heavy equipment to avoid early repair(s), damage, and/or engine failure.

We recommended that management strictly adhere Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and prepare PPMP and consolidated APP. Require the BAC to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated PPMP and APP yearly for us to validate and ensure that all procurement undertaken by the LGU were based on the approved annual procurement plan.

observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record the necessary adjustments for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; (ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (iii) conduct a thorough review of the long outstanding receivables of P4,214,550.08. If these receivables are found to be uncollectible and worthless, the Municipality should derecognize the asset account through writing-off of dormant accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005. Also, set up an appropriate Allowance for Impairment for the possible losses; and (iv) verify the validity and existence of the Accounts Payable by informing the concerned payees of their outstanding claims and determine whether these are still valid especially those that have been dormant for two years or more, if found to be not valid, proper reversion should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund; and

Municipal Treasurer to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances by withholding the payment of salaries of concerned AOs, if necessary.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause for the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof and institute appropriate legal actions to enforce collection/settlement thereof, to recover the amount due thereon and cause the return of the unexpended balances of the Due to NGAs totaling P548,396.32 to the respective Source Agencies for proper presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the Financial Statements and/or make representations with the source agency for the possible reprogramming of the unexpended balances to other PPAs.
observations.reason: Outstanding receivables pertaining to retired Municipal Treasurer remained unsettled due to supporting documents which were burned during the fire incident in the Provincial Auditor’s Office in 2010.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance. The inventory accounts shall be properly monitored using the Supplies Ledgers Card (SLCs) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower who will monitor the said inventories, the Municipal Accountant opted to record the supplies and materials as outright expense, since they were also directly issued to the end-users. This finding is reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive shall direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to stop recording/recognizing cash advances as outright expense; to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each cash advance to ensure accuracy and correctness; to prepare adjusting journal entries to establish cash advance previously recognized as expense amounting to P1,231,842.05.

We recommended and management agreed to the following courses of actions:
Strictly follow the perpetual inventory method in recording and accounting of inventories for distribution.
Conduct physical count of unused inventories for the recognition of inventories accounts reported without balances in the books.
Prepare and maintain the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports in relation to receipt and issuance of inventories for distribution.
Submit complete AIR’s and RIS’s (if necessary) as required to support pertinent disbursement vouchers.

We recommended and management agreed to the following courses of actions:
Make use of the correct and appropriate accounts in recording payments for meals, snacks and drinks relative to official functions and all expenses in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular 2015-009.
Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of GPPB Resolution No.09-2009 and IRR of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of goods if adopting shopping/small value procurement as a mode of procurement.
Submit complete documentary requirements for all transactions as prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended and management agreed to the following courses of actions:
Require the submission of lacking documentary requirements for travels by all personnel indicated in Appendix H in accordance with COA Circular No. 2021-001, otherwise direct refund of travels claimed.
Adjust prior year’s transactions and reclassify other erroneous debits to the account into the appropriate account for proper accounting in conformity with Volume III of the GAM for LGUs.
Stop payment of gasoline and fuel cost as transportation expense and cost of actual meals for travel claims by employees. Adhere strictly to EO No. 77 for computation of allowable travel claims.
Require revision of IT’s by those who claimed for actual costs of gasoline and actual meals and recompute for allowable traveling expense using the current rate to establish if traveling expenses claimed were not excessive, otherwise, direct refund of the excess.

We recommended the following corrective measure to address existing weak points in collection such as but not limited to:
Direct all Accountable Officers/Revenue Collectors and Liquidating Officer to prepare and to submit the monthly Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and the Consolidated RAAF on a monthly basis.
Direct all Accountable Officers to prepare Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and to record their collections in the Cashbooks on a daily basis.
Instruct the Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of collector and check the same against new balances on hand column.  Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections.
Collecting Officers/other employees handling cash should be provided with a safety deposit box and the combination code should be filed with the authorized official.
Adopt a regular monitoring system and/or closer supervision by a responsible officer such that the issuance of official receipts/cash tickets is consistently made; possibility of non-issuance, recycling or misuse is minimized if not eradicated.
Instruct the Accountable Officers to stop using the collections for payment of any expenses as these should be covered appropriately by issuance of checks or through the agency’s established petty cash fund.
We recommended and management agreed to discontinue payment of RATA to employees who are not entitled to it in compliance to Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that PPMPs are properly evaluated in a manner that procurement activities can be synchronized and that PPMPs of all offices/departments/schools were consolidated in the APP taking into consideration their budget framework, priorities and objectives; and
Direct each Department Heads of the LGU to ensure PPMPS were not limited to common office supplies but that all programs, projects and activities of their respective offices are included therein.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit Certification that the project is completed and inspected and the Certificate of Final Acceptance and to request for its technical inspection from COA.
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the documentary requirements under Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payment of final billing and retention money shall be done.
Strictly impose the mandatory posting of warranty security for infrastructure projects to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3 of the IRR of R.A. 9184 and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive directs the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and all concerned officials to properly and completely accomplish relevant procurement documents such as PR, RFQ and PO and provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement projects to serve as reference during inspection and acceptance of deliveries by management.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive requires the Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the COA Audit Team copies of AIRs within twenty-four (24) hours after the acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the personnel concerned on other items so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to –
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to close the account Trust Liability – DRRMF amounting to ₱894,237.10 under general fund books;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that available funds for calamity are based on the regular sources of the LGU.
Also, require the Acting Municipal Accountant with the assistance of the MDRRMO to prepare necessary adjusting entries in order to reclassify in the Disaster and Rescue Equipment account for all those procured items or equipment relative to calamity fund.
Direct the designated MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that only appropriate expenses will be charged against the development fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to submit the result of the assessment for the GAD Plan and Budget, to conduct gender mainstreaming and to establish and maintain a GAD Database.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the inclusion of Undelivered Checks among its Outstanding Checks in preparing its Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to arrive at the correct adjusted Book and Bank balances of the Cash in Bank account, and so as to attain fairness of presentation of the Cash and Cash Equivalent line item in the face of the financial statement.

Recommendation:
We recommended management to exhaust all possible means in the completion of the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE to prevent recurring issues of unreliable account balances.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management initiate the following actions:
Explanation for the advance preparation of Contract Agreement before Pre-Bid and Opening of Bids of the said Infrastructure Projects and Execution of another Contract Agreement with new date; and
We recommended Management to enforce adherence to the guidelines and procedures enumerated in IRR RA 9184 for proper bidding process to avoid further audit disallowances.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management strictly adhere Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and prepare PPMP and consolidated APP. Require the BAC to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated PPMP and APP yearly for us to validate and ensure that all procurement undertaken by the LGU were based on the approved annual procurement plan.

Recommendation:
We recommended the management to secure the required NTC permits and licenses of the Radio Station to operate pursuant to Sec 2 of RA 3846 for proper and legal discharge of communication during emergency situations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management submit the lacking documents to support the transactions as required in Sections 4 and 61 of D 1445. Non-submission of these documents shall constitute a valid ground for the suspension and/or disallowance of the subject transactions, as the case may warrant.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management should adhere to the regulation requiring checks drawn to be made payable directly to the creditor and to stop the practice of purchasing supplies on reimbursement basis to officials and employees and ensure the withholding of the required amount of taxes under existing regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to procure consultancy services pursuant to the provisions in RA 9184.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure sufficient and complete documentation of consultants as required in

We recommended Management to:
Immediately submit the LDRRM Report of Utilization for CY 2021;
Provide justification for the following:
Charges to DRRMF that were not incorporated in the LDRRMFIP/AIP
Overspent items and expenditures without budget
Ensure that all charges to the DRRMF are made within and/or duly supported with an approved Budget.

Recommendation:
We recommended Management for the submission of the BOT Resolution and CHED Memorandum Order as basis for the additional compensation and supporting documents to support the payments.

We recommended Management to:

        1. For the MGSO: to prepare, maintain and submit  to  the  Municipal
           Accountant  local  road  network  ledger  cards   to   facilitate
           subsequent reconciliation with the Report on  Physical  Count  of
           Road Network System;

        2. For  the  MGSO,  Municipal  Engineer  and  Municipal  Accountant:
          Evaluate the existing processes  and  identify  those  roads  that
          should be part of PPE; to reconcile inventory records of both; and
          perform their duties and responsibilities in accordance  with  COA
          Circular  2015-008  so  that  records  for  local  roads  will  be
          completely accounted for; and

        3.  For  Accounting  Office:  to  make  necessary  adjustments,   if
          necessary, to correct the  initially  recorded  balances  of  Road
          Networks  and  its  corresponding  Accumulated  Depreciation   and
          provide the Audit Team with copies of the adjustments made.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

        1.  Request the  accounting  software  provider  to  facilitate  the
           modification of the inventory valuation  to  the  moving  average
           method; and

        2.  Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the  usage  of  the
           moving average method in the  valuation  of  inventories  and  to
           facilitate identifying interventions, like manual computation  of
           proper inventory valuation, to accurately present  the  inventory
           balance in the operational  and  financial  reports  pending  the
           modification of the accounting software.

We recommended the Municipal Management to:


        1. Enhance the preparation of the AIP by way of proper planning  and
           specific identification of development projects;


        2. Instruct the Municipal Development Council  to  come  up  with  a
           detailed  AIP  with  consultation  and  deliberation   with   the
           officials concerned; and


        3. Ensure that identified development projects will actually address
           environmental  management  and  socio-economic   needs   of   the
           constituents,  as  required  by  the  DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum
           Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended the Municipal Management to:

        1.  Consider  recalling  all  employees  holding   treasury–related
           positions detailed to other offices to discharge the  collection
           functions as part of their duties and responsibilities  inherent
           to their positions; and

        2.  Ensure  strict  adherence  to  existing  regulations  that  all
           accountable officers/employees including those employed  as  job
           order, contractual and casual  employees  whose  duties  require
           possession or custody of government  funds  and  properties  are
           properly bonded as required by law.

We recommended the Municipal Management to:

        1. Adopt the ATM payroll system for its disbursements  of  salaries
           and allowances for its job orders and other employees instead of
           manual distribution through pay envelopes; and


        2. Require all employees to open an ATM payroll account  with  Land
           Bank of the Philippines to facilitate efficient distribution  of
           salaries and allowances.

We recommended the Municipality to:

        1. Find alternatives like other near companies  which  caters  this
           kind of services with regard to the disposal of its stacked non-
           biodegradable wastes;


        2. Continue  to  comply  with  the  mandatory  provisions  for  the
           collection of non-recyclable materials and special wastes;

        3. Conduct regular orientation to  continually  educate  the  waste
           generators about the importance of proper waste segregation;


        4. Conduct necessary training for all collectors and  personnel  to
           ensure that solid wastes are handled properly and in  accordance
           with the guidelines pursuant to RA 9003; and

        5. Continue to properly maintain the established Materials Recovery
           Facility (MRF) within the Municipality in  accordance  with  the
           prescribed guidelines.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:


      1. Direct the MDRRMO to observe the minimum standards  established  in
        the  NDPP   2011-2028   specifically   on   the   availability   and
        serviceability of aforementioned equipment and tools to be used for
        preparedness, response and rescue activities; and


     3. Incorporate on the Municipality’s plan and budget the appropriation
        for the deficiencies noted to ensure the preparedness  of  the  LGU
        and to strengthen its capacity to respond to man-made  and  natural
        calamities that may strike its locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that all procurements for
COVID-19 related PPAs are made strictly in accordance with the provisions
of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB guidelines and
resolutions; and
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims are duly supported with
complete and proper documentation, to establish the validity and propriety
of transactions, before processing claims for payment; and ensure that
accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with essential
information.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the: (a) Acting Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct
periodic reconciliation of their records in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume I; (b) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory
of its PPE annually as prescribed by the existing rules and regulations to validate the
existence of PPE and correctness of property records and the integrity of property
custodianship; (c) Acting Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE
Ledger Cards and complete set of Property Cards for all PPE items, respectively, to
facilitate reconciliation of both records; (d) Acting Property Officer to conduct
inspection of unserviceable properties, prepare and submit the corresponding IIRUP to
facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items; (e) accountable officer to pay the
corresponding book value of the lost laptop to settle his accountability; and
(f) Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation, to prepare the necessary adjustment/s
on the PPE accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) create a Disposal
Committee to facilitate the preparation of the Appraisal Report for the eventual
disposal of the unserviceable properties and to avoid accumulation therefrom; and
(ii) require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the BAC Chairman for the
two parcels of lot to facilitate its transfer to the Barangays and to effect the necessary
adjusting/correcting entries in the books of accounts of the LGU.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to
record the necessary adjustments particularly in the advances to SDOs,
Advances to Officers and Employees and Accounts Payable for fair and
accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements;
(ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that
no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the
previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting
thereof is made; (iii) conduct a thorough review of the long outstanding
receivables of P4,214,550.08. If these receivables are found to be uncollectible
and worthless, the Municipality should derecognize the asset account
through writing-off dormant accounts as provided under COA Circular No.
2016-005. Also, set up an appropriate Allowance for Impairment for the
possible losses; (iv) verify the validity and existence of the Accounts Payable
by informing the concerned payees of their outstanding claims and determine
whether these are still valid and enforceable especially those that have been
dormant for two years or more, if found to be not valid, proper reversion
should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund; and
(v) remit promptly the taxes collected within the prescribed period of
remittance pursuant to BIR Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to withhold the payment of salaries of concerned AOs,
if necessary, to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the immediate
settlement of long outstanding cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof and
institute appropriate legal actions to enforce collection/settlement thereof, to recover
the amount due thereon and cause the return of the unexpended balances of the Due to
NGAs to the respective Source Agencies/Bureau of Treasury for and accurate
presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the Financial Statements and/or make
representations with the source agency for the possible reprogramming to other PPAs
of the unexpended balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all procurement
activities undertaken by the Municipality is strictly in accordance with the provisions of
the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly, during the bid evaluation and post-
qualification phase for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal,
technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. BAC and its Technical Working Group (TWG) to: (i) be cautious and
exercise care and prudence in the examination of various eligibility and
technical documents submitted by the prospective bidders in faithful
adherence to relevant provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to
prevent the award of contract to ineligible contractors/suppliers; and
(ii) immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at
a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are
properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the
Audit Team;
b. BAC to: (i) submit immediately the unsubmitted contracts and its supporting
documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit; (ii) revisit the
updated guidelines of the GPPB on the procurement of goods, services and
infrastructure project particularly the forms applicable thereat; (iii) invite,
in addition to the representative of the COA, at least two (2) qualified
observers with knowledge, experience and expertise on the procurement at
hand, in all stages of the procurement process, five (5) days prior to the
activity; (iv) post in the PhilGEPS website and in any conspicuous places
within the premises of the LGU copies of the Notices of Award, Approved
Contracts and Notices to Proceed within the prescribed period; and (v)
ensure that bidding documents available for sale to prospective bidders
are complete as to details to ensure that requirements/specifications of the
projects are properly addressed and clear to the prospective bidders;
c. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting
documents noted and ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper
and complete supporting documents, as required under Section 9.1.3.1 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before processing for
payment; and
d. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that (i) claimants other than the authorized
representative of contractor are duly supported with SPA from the
supplier/contractor and with proofs of identification; and (ii) the contractor
pay the subsequent annual premium as it falls due until the project’s final
acceptance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MSWMB to:
(i) implement priority projects concerning solid waste Management to efficiently
address solid wastes concerns within the Municipality; (ii) make representations and
coordination with the PENRO and NSWMC to fast track the review and approval of the
SWMP and thereafter, coordinate with the Engineering Office and Bids and
Awards Committee for the immediate implementation of the PPAs embodied in the 10-
year SWMP; and (iii) convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dumpsite to
mitigate the health risks to proximate communities and damages to the environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) augment the work force in the Accounting Office to help the Municipal Accountant in the preparation and regular updating of accounting journals and ledgers; (b) issue a memorandum order requiring the Municipal Accountant to update the GLs and SLs for CYs 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020 and 2021 pursuant to Section 114 of PD No. 1445, within a set deadline and make the same available to the Audit Team for audit purposes; and (c) consider initiating the proceedings for administrative disciplinary action against the Municipal Accountant pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445 and Section 444 of RA No. 7160 in accordance with the provisions of the 2017 Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant adjust the affected account balances, otherwise submit a valid justification for disregarding the pertinent accounting rules and standards in the presentation of the Financial Statements for CY 2021, particularly those regarding the distinction between current and non-current assets and liabilities and the proper category of liability accounts for further evaluation and proper action, as appropriate; and
Municipal Mayor, consider initiating the proceedings for administrative disciplinary action against the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the provisions of the 2017 Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant submit a satisfactory written explanation/justification for non-compliance with the prescribed rules and standards in the presentation of revenue and expense accounts in the Statement of Financial Performance and in the Notes to FS for CY 2021; and
Municipal Mayor, consider initiating the proceedings for administrative disciplinary action against the Municipal Accountant pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445 and Section 444 of RA No. 7160 for repeated unjustified failure to comply with reasonable rules and standards in the preparation of financial reports and statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) designate additional support staff for the Municipal Accountant to assist her in the preparation of monthly reports and statements, including the BRS; and (b) pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445, issue a memorandum order requiring the Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2021 within a set deadline and submit them to the Audit Team together with the bank statements, paid checks and debit/credit memoranda in accordance with Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) prepare the general and subsidiary ledgers for all accounts pertaining to cash advances and submit a summary schedule of subsidiary ledger balances and the SUCA as of December 31, 2021 and June 30, 2022; (b) impose strictly the provision requiring all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances fully and promptly as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served or at the end of each year, whichever comes first; and (c) cause the issuance of demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances which are due and demandable as of the current date, copy furnish the Audit Team for monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor issue a memorandum order to the accountable officers concerned to submit complete documentation on the consumption and/or distribution of supplies and materials purchased in CY 2021 totaling ₱7,306,812.46;
Municipal Accountant strictly apply the prescribed recording standards on receipts and consumption/distribution of inventories including the adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided under Paragraphs 9, 44 and 47 of IPSAS No. 12; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of a duly appointed GSO, take charge in setting up and updating of stock cards for proper monitoring of purchases and issuances of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant take the initiative in securing the distribution report and supporting documents for the fishing gears amounting to ₱1,499,250.00 and record the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts while strictly adhering to the prescribed recording standards on receipts and distribution of inventories; and
Municipal Mayor issue a memorandum order for officers involved in the property and supply management of the Municipality to strictly observe the proper use of prescribed accounting forms on the acceptance, inspection, requisition and issuance or distribution of supplies and materials in accordance with Sections 116, 119 and 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the:
Inventory Committee Chairperson prepare a revised RPCPPE for CY 2021 such that all PPE accounts other than real properties are duly included thereon, all the necessary information are correctly presented, and all discrepancies with the accounting records and the individual departments’ reports are duly reconciled; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the other department heads, exert joint efforts to setup the complete set of property records, property cards and ledger cards for each type of PPE, and henceforth ensure that they are complete and regularly updated.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer take the necessary actions to address the errors and deficiencies noted by the Municipal Accountant on the unsubmitted check disbursements and cause the immediate cancellation and replacement of the said checks, as appropriate.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant give due priority in finding the unsubmitted voucher which is still in her custody and provide clear written instructions to the Acting Treasurer by which her requirements may be satisfactorily complied with and then facilitate the complete submission of the vouchers and supporting documents to the Audit Team at the soonest time possible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) and the Municipal Accountant, submit the work accomplishment reports, time records, job orders and contract of service for 255 wage payrolls totaling ₱3,885,603.07.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth ensure that all wage payrolls are duly supported with complete documentation prior to its processing for approval and payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the LDC, (a) impose a policy on strict adherence to Section 109 of RA No. 7160 in carrying out their function as mandated thereon; and (b) henceforth, ensure that priority development programs and projects are well-planned, procurement-and-implementation ready, and properly coordinated with the implementing offices and other agencies concerned.

We recommended that the MDRRMO prepare the required investment plan for prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF accompanied with a valid justification on why it was not formulated prior to actual disbursement of fund, and henceforth, ensure that all proposed programs, projects, and activities to be charged against the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF are duly incorporated in the annual LDRRMFIP and integrated in the approved AIP of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the MPDC, in coordination with the Local Development Council and upon preparation of the required investment plan by the MDRRMO, prepare a Supplemental AIP therefor and submit it along with the MDRRMO’s justification to the Sangguniang Bayan for their consideration and approval.

We recommended that the MPDC, in coordination with the GFPS-TWG, initiate the establishment of a GAD database containing the gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data of the Municipality in accordance with Section 37-D, Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9710 and henceforth keep it updated such that it may serve as a reliable basis for addressing gender issues and concerns in the GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing years.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, upon careful review and reconciliation of their records, determine the correct amount of taxes due for remittance and remit the full amount to the BIR at the soonest time possible, otherwise they shall be personally liable for the surcharge and interest that may be imposed thereon; and henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld from payments to contractors and suppliers are remitted intact within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) review the accounting records to determine the cause/s of abnormal balance of Due to BIR account under the General Fund and make the necessary adjustment therefor; and (b) regularly review and monitor the information process flow in the Accounting Unit to ensure the integrity of accounting records and reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned department heads to observe timely submission of reports and documents, especially those requested by the Audit Team pursuant to existing accounting and auditing regulations, to allow timely review of municipal accounts and transactions including the preparation and transmittal of the annual audit report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2020 using the prescribed AAPSI Form, and henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA

Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to review previous financial statements, schedules and Annual Audit Reports as reference to determine the value of the municipal hall and consequently to prepare adjusting entries to derecognize the assets from the books;

Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Inventory and Inspection Report, photographs if any, Waste Material Report and to ensure that strict adherence on disposal of assets set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 shall be observed.

observations.recommendation: Direct all Revenue Collectors to prepare RAAF while the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF and submit to COA the said monthly reports in the prescribed form provided in the NGAS Manual not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.

observations.reason: Monthly RAAFs were only submitted during cash examinations.

observations.managementAction: Notice of Suspension was issued, and the management has fully submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
Require the BAC Secretary –

to submit to COA the    approved contracts together with its supporting papers for the said perfected projects for COA’s review.  

to submit prospective contracts within 5 days upon its perfection in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.

Advise management headed by the Local Chief Executive to revisit the internal control policies over LGU procurement operations to identify and address these recurring lapses over submission of contracts.
observations.managementAction: Contracts were partially submitted with lacking supporting documents.
observations.reason: The contracts submitted were returned to the management due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that the management instruct the accountant to provide depreciation in the books of accounts and adjust previous balances not provided with depreciation.  Facilitate the creation of an inventory team to conduct inventory of properties owned by the LGU for the purpose of having better accuracy in presenting items and balances in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Depreciation recognized were based on estimate due to difficulty in determining the specific item in the books and the details of its acquisition including the costs and exact date of acquisition. And one-time cleansing of PPE items are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the proper recording of financial transactions in accordance with the IPSAS 12 and GAM for LGUs and effect corresponding adjustments.

We recommended that management:
•	Prepare and submit on a monthly basis the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued duly supported with Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) to the Accounting Office for the preparation of the JEV to record in the books all the issuances made during the period.
•	Instruct the Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for each inventory stocks.
•	Prepare and submit the RPCI on an annual basis to the auditor.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee should post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month. Also, inform the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance of the status report.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of using cash advances in the procurement of various supplies/materials to avoid non-withholding of creditable income taxes and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR in all government procurement and Revenue Regulations No. 2-2006.

We recommended that management strictly adhere with existing laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of LDRRMF and submit justification for the utilization of the fund in implementation of PPAs not included in the LDRRMFIP, AIP, and supplemental AIP.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department make the necessary adjusting entries to record the accrued liability pertaining to earned leave benefits of the employees for the current and previous years after ensuring the correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances as of December 31, 2019. Review at each reporting date and adjust to reflect the current best estimate to ensure that employee’s benefits are properly accounted for and disclosed in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management to make necessary adjustments to record the accrued liability.
observations.reason: Management have financial constraint in funding the liability.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct both offices to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards for properties owned and conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of the PPE accounts.  The differences per physical count and book balances must be investigated cleared and reconciled immediately.
observations.managementAction: Management to reconcile balances between the two funds through conduct of physical count and determination of serviceability of recorded inventory items
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report. Management was unable to conduct physical count and determine serviceability of recorded items.  Instead, ownership records (Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment) was updated but item status was not updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 
• Formulate a disaster recovery plan to secure the agency’s data and information systems.
• Set policies in securing data which includes determining the frequency in which data and records are backed-up and the location to which these back-ups are stored.
observations.managementAction: Management to formulate policies regarding safeguard to backup agency data in cases of emergency.
observations.reason: Back-up of agency data done at employee level with no policies provided by top management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the contracts including all the necessary supporting documents for review and evaluation.  Implement strictly the provisions of Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. With strict adherence, immediate corrective measures can be done for all deficiencies noted in the course review, evaluation, and inspection.
observations.managementAction: Management to submit contracts and Purchase Orders with the deadline set.
observations.reason: Several procurement of goods were not submitted to COA within time period with the contracts determined to be submitted thereon with the related voucher.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the corresponding elected officials to secure from the Philippines Councilor’s League, Incorporated, a government issued official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management to correspond with League of Councilors to demand Official Receipts for the registration thereon.
observations.reason: Ongoing deliberation with courts regarding status of the League and the requirement to issue Official Receipts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to prepare the LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness in the DRRM Investment Plan pursuant to 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommend that Management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and Utilization Report to the auditors’ office.`
observations.reason: Monthly utilization report not submitted to auditor’s office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management enforce strictly the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, aggressively follow-up long outstanding cash advances and compel them to settle their accounts. And after due notice/demand, cause the withholding of the amount due the concerned officials. 

We also recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers giving additional cash advances unless the previous ones are settled.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand to liquidation of advances prior to release of new cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the affected accounts in conformity with Section 04 (p), Chapter 2 of the New Government Accounting system (NGAS) for LGUs.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of the Section 79 of P.D 1445 on the disposal of the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to make an entry based on prepared IIRUP
observations.reason: The IIRUP has already been prepared but still no adjustment made on the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to insure all new buildings including its equipment and other attachments thereon pursuant to the provisions of Section5 of R.A. 656 and Section489 of GAAM, Volume I.

Provide an appropriation for the insurance premium for the insurance of all its properties having insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).
observations.managementAction: For budget deliberation
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to adopt the most appropriate accounting policy on recognition of impairment losses considering the availability of data and nature of the impairment and to: Direct the Municipal Accountant to - 1. Record the impairment loss for minor, moderate and severely damaged buildings and other structures; 2. Compute and record the impairment loss of the totally damaged buildings and other structures using the residual value as the recoverable service amount.

We recommended Management to: 3. Adopt an ATM payroll system instead of a manual distribution of salaries and allowances; 4. Require all employees to open an ATM payroll account with Land Bank and provide the account numbers to facilitate the crediting of salaries and allowances; 5. Refrain from charging recurring expenses related to travel from the petty cash fund; 6. Maintain petty cash vouchers and prepare the petty cash replenishment report; 7. Record the following adjusting entries to correct the accounting treatment of petty cash advances: (Dr) Petty Cash P100,000.00 (Cr) Advances to OPEX P100,000.00

We recommended Management to: 9. Effect results to close supervision on the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. 10. At once deposit to the depository bank the amount of P19,900.00; 11. Check the accuracy of the reports submitted by other collecting officers; 12. Revisit and comply with the procedures on proper handling of dishonored checks; 13. Submit a revised consolidated RAAF from August 2019 to date in accordance with the prescribed format taking into account the differences noted and inclusion of accountability for checks; 14. Indicate receipt dates in the record book for checks and check series in the inspection and acceptance report; 15. Perform piece by piece count of all accountable forms upon receipt from the provider; 16. Refrain from delegating the actual custodianship of accountable forms and preparation of the related reports; 17. Thereafter, submit canceled accountable forms simultaneous with the Report of Collections; 18. Include accountabilities for accountable forms with face value in the next renewal of fidelity bond; 19. Adopt a filing method that will protect collection reports the most; 20. Direct the other collecting officers to thereafter, submit daily RCD and monthly RAAF for consolidation by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended Management to: 21. Effect payment of goods and services through checks payable directly to supplier or dealer; 22. Discourage the practice of procuring goods and services instead of directly paying to suppliers/service providers, contrary to Section 93 of Presidential Decree No. 1445; 23. Consider the establishment and maintenance of Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an estimate of expenses, that shall be strictly for petty operating expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to: 24. Immediate submission of above-lacking documents; 25. Ensure the completeness of the supporting documents in the disbursement vouchers before submission to COA Audit Team. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee – 26. Acquaint themselves on the latest government issuances to ensure all documents are submitted by the winning suppliers and that are attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended Management to: 27. Properly control and account the gasoline consumption and use of motor vehicles as provided in COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977; 28. Ensure that the driver’s trip tickets are properly accomplished (i.e. departure time, arrival time, speedometer reading, lubricants and fuel consumption); 29. Provide adequate information on the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption of Motor Vehicles and Monthly Reports of Official Travel and to include significant information like the specific government vehicle used (with plate number indicated on the report), total distance travelled and total fuel used; 30. For the Municipal Accountant - Properly review the trip tickets submitted by end-users; and 31. For the MGSO and issuing office - Revisit relevant guidelines on how to properly fill-out a driver’s trip ticket and provide only accurate and truthful information on the said form.

We recommended Management to: 32. Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing special disbursements like SRAs and other future kind of disbursements to avoid charging them to an inappropriate classification of expense and to avoid inconvenience as to the benefits of some social and economic services that may be funded out of it.

We recommended Management to: 33. Coordinate with the Punong Barangays to verify the inclusion of those unqualified barangay functionaries as recipients of SAP cash subsidy, and require the immediate refund of the total cash aid of P12,000.00; 34. Establish accuracy of its current database by conducting revalidation and verification of information for constituents already profiled and the addition of constituents not yet included in the database. This requires better coordination between barangay officials and the Municipality, to affirm immediate identification of targeted beneficiaries for future relief programs.

We recommended Management to: 36. Develop or adopt a monitoring and evaluation tool for the SWM Program that is suitable to the needs of the Municipality; 37. Thereafter, maintain all necessary documentations for all activities related to SWM Program; 38. Reconstitute the SWM Board including the following members: Chairperson of the Sangguniang Kabataan Federation; Representative from NGOs whose principal purpose is to promote recycling and the protection of air and water quality; Representative from the recycling industry; and Representative from the manufacturing or packaging industry. However, if some cannot be included after exhausting all efforts, the Management can submit their justifications in writing; 39. Conduct or sponsor formal trainings on proper solid waste management for the garbage collectors and other relevant personnel.

observations.managementAction:
The Management have already established a distribution system where claim stubs are now being issued and collected from each recipient during the distribution. The stubs are issued and collected to ensure that every recipient has been accounted for and claimed their share of goods, and to have the right to claim their goods even after the scheduled date of distribution in their respective barangays. 

Some of the lacking documents as well as the justifications for non-compliance with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances were submitted to the Audit Team on June 23, 2021.
observations.reason:
Based on the management reply, the preparation of RIS and SSMI were no longer complied since it is long overdue and they believe that it would only cast additional doubt since they should be attached along with the DVs.  
Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office opted to record the inventories as outright expense as it is going to be difficult to monitor the in-and-out of supplies and materials during the peak of COVID-19 operations where such inventories are immediately distributed to key personnel, frontliners and other recipients.  They further added that there is no existing GSO or any particular personnel trained for GSO who can handle and manage the inventories of the LGU and there is no municipal warehouse that can serve as storage for such inventories.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of its PPE annually as prescribed by the existing rules and regulations to validate the existence of PPE, correctness of property records and the integrity of property custodianship;        (b) Acting Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE Ledger Cards and complete set of Property Cards for all PPE items, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of both records; (c) Acting Property Officer to conduct inspection of unserviceable properties, prepare and submit the corresponding IIRUP to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items; (d) the accountable officer to locate the missing government property otherwise pay its corresponding money value under his custody to settle his account; and (e) Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation, to prepare the necessary adjustment/s on the PPE accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) create a Disposal Committee to facilitate the preparation of the Appraisal Report for the eventual disposal of the unserviceable properties and to avoid accumulation therefrom; and (ii) cause the titling of lots in the name of the Municipality to ensure ownership and control over the properties and to avoid any legal impediments due to possible third-party claims.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Property Officer already started maintaining Property Cards since CY 2020 and already completed in CY 2021 the PCs of the three PPE accounts (Office Equipment, Furniture and Fixture and ICT Equipment) in all the offices of the Municipality.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant already maintains the PPE Ledger Cards for PPEs procured from July 2021 onwards which includes ICT Equipment, Office Equipment, Furniture and Fixture and Road Network Accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of initiative and consistent efforts of the concerned officials to implement the audit recommendations thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the BAC/TWG ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly, during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

BAC and the TWG, thru the Municipal Accountant, to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team, and henceforth ensure that all perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team; and

Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking/missing supporting documents to the Audit Team to be able to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity and propriety of transactions; and ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with essential information.
observations.managementAction: Several lacking documents were already submitted to the Audit Team on May 19 and June 6, 2021.
observations.reason: Procurements of goods and infrastructure projects were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the audit recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the: 

MSWMB (i) make representations and coordination with the CENRO and NSWMC to fast track the review and approval of the SWMP and thereafter, coordinate with the Engineering Office and Bids and Awards Committee for the immediate implementation of the PPAs embodied in the 10-year SWMP; (ii) ensure that the existing MRFs are fully operational; and (iii) convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dumpsite to efficiently address solid waste concerns within the Municipality; and 

Local Sanggunian enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment.
observations.managementAction:
The Waste Analysis Characterization Study (WACS) which is one of the required documents for the final approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) has already been submitted to the National Solid Waste Management Board (NSWMB) along with the LGU’s SWMP. Management Plan (SWMP).

The MRFs are now being utilized by the Barangays starting CY 2021.

The Local Ordinance restricting the practice of open burning of waste was already approved as of CY 2021.
observations.reason: The collection of garbage is still limited to Barangay Poblacion due to limited heavy equipment used for garbage collection.  Meanwhile, the Municipality is still using the existing open dumpsite due to non-implementation of the PPAs in CYs 2021 which includes the acquisition of lot for the controlled sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Local Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-001 dated February 22, 2017 particularly, on the priority projects and disbursements charged against the Fund.
observations.managementAction: Job Order Personnel whose wages had been charged against the 20% Development Fund were already addressed and corrected by the LGU starting July 2020.
observations.reason: The Management has yet to fully implement some of its targeted PPAs for the 20 percent Development Fund, hence, fund were still not optimally utilized by the Management hence, similar audit observations had been observed in CY 2021 transactions as discussed in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:

(b) consider issuing a memorandum order requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare the GLs and SLs for CYs 2017, 2018, 2019 and 2020 pursuant to Section 114 of PD No. 1445, within a set deadline and make the same available to the Audit Team for audit purposes.

None

observations.recommendation:

(b) pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445, issue a memorandum order requiring the Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2020 within a set deadline and submit them to the Audit Team together with the bank statements, paid checks and debit/credit memoranda in accordance with Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction:

None

We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant take the initiative in securing the distribution report and supporting documents for the fishing gears amounting to ₱1,499,250.00 and record the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts while strictly adhering to the prescribed recording standards on receipts and distribution of inventories; and

Municipal Mayor, (i) establish a sound property and supply management system that imposes the proper use of prescribed accounting forms to enable timely and effective monitoring of supplies and materials; and 

(ii) designate a Supply Officer who shall be responsible for overseeing the property and supply management system of the Municipality and observing the proper use of forms such as IAR, RIS, SSMI and Stock Cards.

None

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Mayor (i) create an Inventory Committee who shall take charge of the annual physical count of all properties and equipment of the Municipality;



(ii) require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report therefor to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; 


(iii) designate a specific officer who shall take charge of property and supply management, application of property tags and reference numbers, consolidation of reports on physical count and maintaining a centralized record for properties and equipment; and


(iv) consider the creation of a plantilla position for and hiring of a General Services Officer pursuant to Section 490, Article 20 of RA No. 7160 to ensure efficient administration of all property and equipment of the Municipality; and

Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the other department heads, exert efforts to reconstruct the property records and setup property cards and ledger cards for each type of PPE, and

henceforth ensure that such property and accounting records are complete and regularly updated.

observations.recommendation:

MDRRMO, in coordination with the MDRRMC, (i) prepare the required investment plan for the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF amounting to ₱7,912,862.00; the investment plan should correspond to the actual expenditures incurred in CY 2020 totaling ₱3,514,724.80 and should be accompanied by a written justification on why it was not formulated prior to actual disbursement of the fund; and

Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the LDRRMF and initiate the transfer of any unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF to the STF pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular 2012-002.

(b) see that the GPB reviewed and endorsed by the DILG is duly incorporated in the annual budget.

observations.recommendation:

(a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2019 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and 

(b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor issue a memorandum order enjoining the persons responsible for six audit suspensions issued on October 29, 2018 and August 5, 2019 to immediately settle them within a reasonable time, otherwise the transactions covered shall be disallowed in audit pursuant to Section 9.4 of the RRSA; and 

(b) Municipal Mayor and the Acting Municipal Treasurer enforce the COEs issued on January 22, 2013 and January 5, 2018 by withholding the salaries and other compensation due to the persons liable in satisfaction of the audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.3 of the RRSA. Payment of salaries or any money due them in violation of the said COEs will be disallowed in audit and the Municipal Mayor and the Acting Municipal Treasurer shall be held liable therefor.
observations.managementAction: The Management settled four audit suspension totaling ₱6,080,181.54 in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The Audit Team has not yet received any response regarding this matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) Inventory Committee  to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding duly accomplished RPCPPE, and henceforth ensure that said physical count are strictly and timely conducted every year;                (b) Storekeeper/Acting Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPCPPE with  the corresponding ledger balances and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; (c) Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation, to prepare the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts and determine the persons accountable, for any losses therein; and (d) Acting Property Officer to fast track the preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable items. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Disposal Committee to facilitate the preparation of the Appraisal Report for the eventual disposal of the unserviceable properties. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor is advised to fast track the processing of the Deed of Donation for the transfer of ownership of the land to Barangay Caguray for the eventual derecognition in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is in the process of creating the Disposal Committee, pending the issuance of the Executive Order.
observations.reason: The Management still failed to conduct complete physical count and create a disposal committee, as discussed in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare the GLs and SLs for CYs 2017, 2018 and 2019 pursuant to Section 114 of PD No. 1445; (b) consider augmentation of personnel in the Accounting Office to assist the Municipal Accountant in preparing the journals and maintaining the ledgers; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the municipal books of accounts maintained by the Accountant are complete and in accordance with the formats prescribed under the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant failed to prepare the GLs and SLs for CYs 2017 to  2019 due to voluminous workload, limited manpower and health care concerns as well as lack of an automated accounting system.

The deficiencies were still observed in CY 2020 and thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 1 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete set of Financial Statements for CY 2019 with adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 and IPSAS 1, 2 and 24. Otherwise, cause the suspension of her salaries until such reports have been submitted to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant did not submit the lacking components of the CY 2019 Financial Statements due to voluminous workload, limited manpower and health care concerns as well as lack of an automated accounting system.

Other specific actions taken by the Management, if any, could not be validated due to lack of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Mayor through the Inventory Committee, require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report thereon to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with property and accounting records; and 

ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant prepare the Property Cards and PPELC, respectively, per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Complete physical count of all PPE in CY 2020 was not conducted due to the failure to  create an Inventory Committee and thus reiterated in Audit Observation No. 7 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2019 and promptly submit the same to the Audit Team along with duly certified copies of bank statements and debit/credit memoranda for verification, pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant reasoned limited personnel, time constraints and health care concerns and that she prioritized the preparation of financial reports and the signing of payrolls and disbursement vouchers over the preparation of BRS. 

The deficiency was still observed in CY 2020 and thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 3 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

prepare the general and subsidiary ledgers for all accounts pertaining to cash advances and submit a summary schedule of subsidiary ledger balances and the SUCA as of December 31, 2019;

impose strictly the provision requiring all accountable officers to fully and promptly liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served or at the end of each year, whichever comes first; and

cause the issuance of demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2019.
observations.managementAction: The unliquidated cash advances slightly reduced by ₱345,661.45 during the year 2020.
observations.reason: No justification was submitted on their failure to enforce liquidation of cash advances and thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 4 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, cause the immediate issuance of a demand letter to the Provincial Government requiring the return of unexpended fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
Fund transfer to the Provincial Government of Palawan for Inter-LGU Alliance Project remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2020.

Other specific actions taken by the Management, if any, could not be validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports on the same with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments;

Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects; and

Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to achieve the  socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Specific actions taken by the Management, if any, could not be validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairperson (a) submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2019 either in printed or electronic form, whichever is more convenient or cost-efficient; and (b) henceforth observe regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in such manner and timing as may be deemed reasonable in view of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
The BAC still failed to submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CYs 2019 and 2020.

Other specific actions taken by the Management, if any, could not be validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) propose to the Sangguniang Bayan the adoption of the LEE Manual as recommended under LBC No. 111 dated June 10, 2016; (b) assign or designate the relevant local officials who will be responsible in planning for and implementing the new LEEs; and (c) enjoin the said officials to conduct an evaluation on the operations of the municipal public market in accordance with Section 4 of the LEE Manual.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Specific actions taken by the Management, if any, could not be validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer (1) submit a written explanation on why taxes withheld were not remitted intact to the BIR; (2) cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible interests, penalties and surcharges for late payments; and (3) henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld are remitted intact and on the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The deficiency was still observed in CY 2020 and thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 11 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the unremitted balance of the Due to GSIS account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate; and

OIC-Municipal Treasurer, (a) based on the analysis prepared by the Municipal Accountant, remit to the GSIS all contributions and/or loan amortizations from prior periods so that only those from the preceding month shall remain as the balance of Due to GSIS account; and (b) henceforth, ensure that GSIS contributions and loan amortization are remitted intact within the prescribed period under RA No. 8291 for the welfare of the employees and to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: GSIS contributions pertaining to previous years (CY 2019 and below) were still not remitted as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the unremitted balance of the Due to PhilHealth account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate;

OIC-Municipal Treasurer, (a) based on the analysis prepared by the Municipal Accountant, remit to the PhilHealth all contributions from prior periods so that only those from the preceding month shall remain as the balance of Due to PhilHealth account; and (b) henceforth, ensure the prompt remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed under PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s. 2014 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties; and

Municipal Mayor cause the refund of ₱4,895.71 by concerned local officials who failed to remit PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed time.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The deficiency was still observed in CY 2020 and thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 12 in Part II of this AAR.
