observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2018 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to submit the AAPSI relative to the audit observation and recommendations included in the AAR for CY 2018.

The deficiency was still observed in CY 2020 and thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 16 in Part II of this AAR.
Reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the monitoring of the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan, closure of the open dumpsite, establishment of sanitary landfill, and strict compliance of segregation of wastes under all its jurisdiction starting on LGU premises; and

Increase the number of MRF pursuant to Section 33 of RA 9003 in coordination with the barangays of the Municipality in order to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage.

Direct the Local School Board to prepare and submit Annual SEF Budget with the prescribed form and timeline pursuant to the new Joint Circular Dep Ed-DBM and DILG No. 1 s. 2017 and to ensure that SEF funds are released and utilized pursuant to Section 272 of RA 7160; and
Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to secure the Program of Work of the subject disbursements and submit to COA.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the above-mentioned lacking documents within fifteen days upon receipt of this AOM and to ensure that prospective payments are supported with complete documents to establish the propriety of the transactions. 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a document checklist in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 to help ensure that claims are completely supported

We recommended that the management instruct the accountant to provide depreciation in the books of accounts and adjust previous balances not provided with depreciation.  Facilitate the creation of an inventory team to conduct inventory of properties owned by the LGU for the purpose of having better accuracy in presenting items and balances in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Depreciations and other adjustments are posted and inventory team are created
observations.reason: Difficulty in determining the specific item in the books and the details of its acquisition including the costs and exact date of acquisition

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department make the necessary adjusting entries to record the accrued liability pertaining to earned leave benefits of the employees for the current and previous years after ensuring the correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances as of December 31, 2019. Review at each reporting date and adjust to reflect the current best estimate to ensure that employee’s benefits are properly accounted for and disclosed in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management to make necessary adjustments to record the accrued liability.
observations.reason: Management have financial constraint in funding the liability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Accountant to recognize and transfer the Local Road Networks from the Public Infrastructure Registry to the books of accounts.  Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to revisit the map of the municipality’s local road networks and determine the proper cost for each local road through thorough review of records.
observations.managementAction: Management to determine serviceability of the remaining Road Networks and determine value of serviceable local roads.
observations.reason: Difficulty in determining local roads owned by the Municipality as road projects often overlapped with road projects of the National Government and Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management closely monitor the receivables and intensify its collection, otherwise, file the appropriate request for authority to write-off for the dormant accounts duly supported with the relevant documents pursuant to Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand liquidation from LGUs.
observations.reason: Changes in barangay personnel and difficulty in obtaining records on the barangay level.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to insure all new buildings including its equipment and other attachments thereon pursuant to the provisions of Section5 of R.A. 656 and Section489 of GAAM, Volume I.

Provide an appropriation for the insurance premium for the insurance of all its properties having insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).
observations.managementAction: For budget deliberation
observations.reason: Not yet implemented in CY 2020 as there were no funds appropriated during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of its PPE annually as prescribed by the existing rules and regulations to validate the existence of PPE, correctness of property records and the integrity of property custodianship;        (b) Acting Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE Ledger Cards and complete set of Property Cards for all PPE items, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of both records; (c) Acting Property Officer to conduct inspection of unserviceable properties, prepare and submit the corresponding IIRUP to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items; (d) the accountable officer to locate the missing government property otherwise pay its corresponding money value under his custody to settle his account; and (e) Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation, to prepare the necessary adjustment/s on the PPE accounts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) create a Disposal Committee to facilitate the preparation of the Appraisal Report for the eventual disposal of the unserviceable properties and to avoid accumulation therefrom; and      (ii) cause the titling of lots in the name of the Municipality to ensure ownership and control over the properties and to avoid any legal impediments due to possible third party claims.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record the necessary adjustments for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; (ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (iii) conduct a thorough review of the long outstanding receivables of P4,214,550.08. If these receivables are found to be uncollectible and worthless, the Municipality should derecognize the asset account through writing-off of dormant accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005. Also, set up an appropriate Allowance for Impairment for the possible losses; and (iv) verify the validity and existence of the Accounts Payable by informing the concerned payees of their outstanding claims and determine whether these are still valid especially those that have been dormant for two years or more, if found to be not valid, proper reversion should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund; and
Municipal Treasurer to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances by withholding the payment of salaries of concerned AOs, if necessary.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause for the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof and institute appropriate legal actions to enforce collection/settlement thereof, to recover the amount due thereon and cause the return of the unexpended balances of the Due to NGAs totaling P548,396.32 to the respective Source Agencies for proper presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the Financial Statements and/or make representations with the source agency for the possible reprogramming of the unexpended balances to other PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the BAC/TWG ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly, during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
BAC and the TWG, thru the Municipal Accountant, to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team, and henceforth ensure that all perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team; and
Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking/missing supporting documents to the Audit Team to be able to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity and propriety of transactions; and ensure that accounting forms are properly accomplished and complete with essential information.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance. The inventory accounts shall be properly monitored using the Supplies Ledgers Card (SLCs) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the:
MSWMB (i) make representations and coordination with the CENRO and NSWMC to fast track the review and approval of the SWMP and thereafter, coordinate with the Engineering Office and Bids and Awards Committee for the immediate implementation of the PPAs embodied in the 10-year SWMP;           (ii) ensure that the existing MRFs are fully operational; and (iii) convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dumpsite to efficiently address solid waste concerns within the Municipality; and
Local Sanggunian enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Local Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-001 dated February 22, 2017 particularly, on the priority projects and disbursements charged against the Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) augment the work force in the Accounting Office to aid the Municipal Accountant in the preparation and regular updating of accounting journals and ledgers; and (b) consider issuing a memorandum order requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare the GLs and SLs for CYs 2017, 2018, 2019 and 2020 pursuant to Section 114 of PD No. 1445, within a set deadline and make the same available to the Audit Team for audit purposes.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant submit a satisfactory written explanation/justification for non-compliance with the prescribed rules and standards in the presentation of revenue and expense accounts in the Statement of Financial Performance and in the Notes to FS for CY 2020; and
Municipal Mayor, consider initiating the proceedings for administrative disciplinary action against the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the provisions of the 2017 Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) designate additional support staff for the Municipal Accountant to aid her in the preparation of monthly reports and statements, including the BRS; and (b) pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445, issue a memorandum order requiring the Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2020 within a set deadline and submit them to the Audit Team together with the bank statements, paid checks and debit/credit memoranda in accordance with Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the general and subsidiary ledgers for all accounts pertaining to cash advances and submit a summary schedule of subsidiary ledger balances and the SUCA as of December 31, 2020;
impose strictly the provision requiring all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances fully and promptly as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served or at the end of each year, whichever comes first; and
cause the issuance of demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2020.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant strictly apply the prescribed recording standards on receipts and consumption/distribution of inventories including the adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided under Paragraphs 9, 44 and 47 of IPSAS No. 12;
Acting Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of a duly appointed GSO, take charge in setting up and updating of stock cards for proper monitoring of purchases and issuances of supplies and materials; and
Municipal Mayor initiate the creation of an Inventory Committee who shall be primarily responsible for the annual physical count of supplies and materials inventory and the preparation of the corresponding reports.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant take the initiative in securing the distribution report and supporting documents for the fishing gears amounting to ₱1,499,250.00 and record the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts while strictly adhering to the prescribed recording standards on receipts and distribution of inventories; and
Municipal Mayor, (i) establish a sound property and supply management system that imposes the proper use of prescribed accounting forms to enable timely and effective monitoring of supplies and materials; and (ii) designate a Supply Officer who shall be responsible for overseeing the property and supply management system of the Municipality and observing the proper use of forms such as IAR, RIS, SSMI and Stock Cards.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor (i) create an Inventory Committee who shall take charge of the annual physical count of all properties and equipment of the Municipality; (ii) require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report therefor to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; (iii) designate a specific officer who shall take charge of property and supply management, application of property tags and reference numbers, consolidation of reports on physical count and maintaining a centralized record for properties and equipment; and (iv) consider the creation of a plantilla position for and hiring of a General Services Officer pursuant to Section 490, Article 20 of RA No. 7160 to ensure efficient administration of all property and equipment of the Municipality; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the other department heads, exert efforts to reconstruct the property records and setup property cards and ledger cards for each type of PPE, and henceforth ensure that such property and accounting records are complete and regularly updated.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant submit a valid justification for disregarding pertinent accounting rules and standards in recording financial transactions and in the preparation of financial statements that resulted in material misstatements on the Statement of Financial Position and the Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2020; and
Municipal Mayor, consider initiating the proceedings for administrative disciplinary action against the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the provisions of the 2017 Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service.

We recommended that the:
MDRRMO, in coordination with the MDRRMC, (i) prepare the required investment plan for the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF amounting to ₱7,912,862.00; the investment plan should correspond to the actual expenditures incurred in CY 2020 totaling ₱3,514,724.80 and should be accompanied by a written justification on why it was not formulated prior to actual disbursement of the fund; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that all the proposed programs, projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF are duly incorporated in the annual LDRRMFIP and integrated in the approved AIP of the Municipality;
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), in coordination with the LDC and upon preparation of the required investment plan by the MDRRMO, prepare a Supplemental AIP therefor and submit it along with the MDRRMO’s justification to the Sangguniang Bayan for their consideration and approval should they find it reasonable based on the current circumstances; and
Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the LDRRMF and initiate the transfer of any unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF to the STF pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer: (a) submit a written explanation on why taxes withheld were not regularly remitted intact to the BIR; (b) upon careful review and reconciliation of their records, determine the correct amount of taxes due for remittance and remit the full amount thereof to the BIR at the soonest time possible; and (c) henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld on contractors and suppliers and compensation of municipal officials and employees are remitted intact within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor determine the local officials/employees responsible for the unnecessary delays in the remittance of PhilHealth premium contributions and cause the refund of interest and surcharges totaling ₱2,149.31;
Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of unremitted PhilHealth premium contributions including the unremitted PhilHealth contributions in the Trust Fund books, reconcile billings and remittances, and effect necessary adjustment and corrections, as appropriate; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer, henceforth, ensure prompt remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed by PhilHealth to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.

We recommended that the GFPS and the LFC (a) exert due diligence in the formulation and integration of budget proposals to ensure that the identified GAD programs, projects and activities in the GPB are adequately provided with a budget allocation which is at least 5% of the total annual budget; and (b) see that the GPB reviewed and endorsed by the DILG is duly incorporated in the annual budget.

We recommended that the MPDC, in coordination with the GFPS-TWG, initiate the setting up of a GAD database containing the gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data of the Municipality in accordance with Section 37-D, Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9710 and henceforth keep it updated to serve as a reliable basis in addressing gender issues and concern in GAD planning and budgeting for the ensuing years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned department heads to observe timely submission of reports and documents, especially those requested by the Audit Team, to allow timely review of municipal accounts and transactions including the preparation and transmittal of the annual audit report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2019 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Appropriate fund for the titling of all land;
Direct the Municipal Assessor̕ s Office to exert effort in titling the land owned by the Municipality; and
Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents for the procurement of land worth P1.5 million.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, as follows:
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to remit the tax deficiency immediately.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the designated General Supply Officer, Acting Municipal Accountant and designated MDRR Officer to install controls over handling of relief goods; and
Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that items received during calamity whether in cash or in kind should be appropriately recorded in the books and disclosed in the calamity reports.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all her collections as well as those remitted to her will be deposited intact and daily or in the frequency prescribed by regulation;
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to delegate the liquidating function to other Revenue Collectors or Cashier to reduce her workload; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to set a specific date on a monthly basis when to reconcile their records without delay so that any discrepancy noted in the process could be corrected immediately.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare an Inventory of Obsolete Accountable Forms in four copies, submit the same to the authorized officials for approval and finally to the COA Auditor so that the obsolete accountable forms be disposed of pursuant to Chapter IV.B of COA Revised Cash Examination Manual of 2013.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to review the variance arising from remittance and withheld amount from employees so that appropriate actions will be undertaken; to ensure that the amount withheld are computed correctly and remitted in full; and to initiate a reconciliation of records with Philhealth to settle the unpaid premiums from previous years.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the BAC Secretariat to transmit all contracts pending for submission immediately  and to ensure that prospective contracts will be submitted within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract;
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that ABC and Inspection Report are attached in the disbursement vouchers and that retention money are deducted in every progress billings; and
Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare Certificate of Project Completion and Certificate of Acceptance for completed projects and to request for its technical inspection from COA.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented for the year will be submitted every beginning of the year; to inform COA within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and to accomplish properly the quarterly report on projects, programs and activities.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct all Department heads to prepare and to submit their individual PPMPs showing the detailed list of goods or infrastructure projects to be procured during the coming year  to the BAC Secretariat and to the Municipal Budget Officer who will ensure that amount involved are within budget;
Require all members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the Head of the Procuring Entity to attend seminars/trainings that will improve their knowledge in the procurement; and
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPBB and Commission on Audit (COA) within fourteen (14) calendar days after end of each semester.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit SAOB on a monthly basis and to ensure that the Obligation Requests are properly accomplished.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain special account in the General Fund for the water works system; and
Create linkages of records, among the Accounting, Treasury, General Services Office and Budget Offices for recording, collection, procurement, disbursement and reporting processes that will facilitate systematic recording and generation of reports.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to recognize liquidation of cash advances only when supported with complete documents; and
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Cashier to prepare Report of Disbursements for each cash advances liquidated on CY 2020 and to ensure that future liquidation of cash advances will be supported with ROD and complete documents.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that only appropriate expenses will be charged against the development fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Program of Works and Detailed Estimates of the projects funded out of the 20% development fund and to ensure that this will be adhered to prospectively.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the designated MDRRMO and Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a revised Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF enumerating therein all sources of funds and disbursements expended for calamity in CY 2020;
Direct the designated MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that available funds for calamity are based on the regular sources of the LGU.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the designated MDDRMO and Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports, post the same to appropriate website, bulletin board or conspicuous places, and to submit the Annex C of DBM LBC Circular No. 125 to COA.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents as well as the list of recipients for the rice distribution.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to submit the result of the assessment for the GAD Plan and Budget; to conduct gender mainstreaming to establish and maintain a GAD Database; and prepare and submit Annual GAD Accomplishment Report of CY 2020.

Recommendation:
We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that the management instruct the accountant to provide depreciation in the books of accounts and adjust previous balances not provided with depreciation.  Facilitate the creation of an inventory team to conduct inventory of properties owned by the LGU for the purpose of having better accuracy in presenting items and balances in the books of accounts.

We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that management instruct both offices to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards for properties owned and conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of the PPE accounts.  The differences per physical count and book balances must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze transactions including those pertaining to prior years relative to unidentified discrepancy and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts and furnish COA the corrections made therein.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the proper recording of financial transactions in accordance with the IPSAS 12 and GAM for LGUs and effect corresponding adjustments.

We recommended that management:
Prepare and submit on a monthly basis the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued duly supported with Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) to the Accounting Office for the preparation of the JEV to record in the books all the issuances made during the period.
Instruct the Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for each inventory stocks.
Prepare and submit the RPCI on an annual basis to the auditor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Finance Committee should post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month. Also, inform the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance of the status report.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management stop the practice of using cash advances in the procurement of various supplies/materials to avoid non-withholding of creditable income taxes and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR in all government procurement and Revenue Regulations No. 2-2006.

We recommended that management strictly adhere with existing laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of LDRRMF and submit justification for the utilization of the fund in implementation of PPAs not included in the LDRRMFIP, AIP, and supplemental AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record the necessary adjustments to the affected accounts for fair and accurate presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements; (ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (iii) cause the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof; and (iv) verify the validity and existence of the Accounts Payable by informing the concerned payees of their outstanding claims and determine whether these are still valid and enforceable especially those that have been dormant for two (2) years or more, if found to be not valid, proper reversion should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund; and

Municipal Treasurer to: (i) send written notices to the payees one (1) month before the check becomes stale as required in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual. Cancelled stale checks shall be reported and attached to the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) at the period of cancellation; and (ii) withhold the payment of salaries of concerned AOs, if necessary, to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the return of the unexpended balances of the Due to NGAs totaling P608,929.86 to the respective Source Agencies for proper presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the Financial Statements and/or make representations with the source agency for the possible reprogramming of the unexpended balances to other PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed to adhere in providing full disclosure on valuation of acquired properties and will ensure to maintain PPE ledger and Property Cards and to prepare necessary adjustments for fair and accurate presentation on the affected accounts and determine the persons accountable for any losses. 

The balance in the Cash-Local Treasury account was already reclassified to the Other Receivable account.

The Municipal Accountant already drew JEVs for the 66 stale checks.

Due from and Due to Other Funds were already reconciled as at year-end.

The Municipal Treasurer already started sending written notices to the payees of stale checks and withheld payment of salaries of concerned individuals to enforce liquidation of their outstanding Cash Advance.

The Accountant already processed the return of the unexpended balances.
observations.reason:
The Accounts Payable account still have long outstanding balances which were not yet adjusted in the books as reiterated in Part II of this Report

Suspension of salaries of erring Accountable Officers was still not withheld.

There were still unremitted balances from 13 completed projects as of year-end, as reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and the TWG to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

BAC and its TWG to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team; and

Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents in the claims for payment, and ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, as required under Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing for payment.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted the lacking documents and will ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documents before processing claims for payments.

Some of the lacking supporting documents were already submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Procurements of goods were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the audit recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and TWG to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly, during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

BAC and its Technical Working Group (TWG) to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team;

Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that all the requirements of the Detailed Engineering Design for infrastructure projects to be implemented are complete prior to the procurement proceedings so that information and/or specifications are duly incorporated in the bidding documents; and (ii) see to it that the computations of ABC are in accordance with the latest guidelines issued by the DPWH to prevent excessive project cost; and

Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit the lacking supporting documents, and henceforth ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documents before processing claims for payment; and (ii) ensure that retention money equivalent to 10% of the total amount due are deducted from progress payments of projects with delays, even after completion of 50% of the value of works.
observations.managementAction: Some of the lacking documents were already submitted.
observations.reason: Procurements of infrastructure projects were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the audit recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the LDRRM Council, ensure that LDRRM Funds are used solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities pursuant to the provisions of R.A. No. 10121.

We further recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:

MDRRMO to (i) ensure that distribution lists for the distribution of relief goods are duly received and signed by the intended beneficiaries; and (ii) ensure that Invoice Receipt of equipment for the donated generator set transferred to various Barangays are properly and duly acknowledged by the concerned Punong Barangay for proper recording and monitoring;   

Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing for payment; (ii) refrain from recording purchases of goods and supplies as outright expense instead they shall be accounted for under the related inventory accounts and recorded as expense only upon consumption and/or distribution thereof for proper monitoring; (iii) coordinate with the MDRRMO for the proper inventory and reconciliation of the relief goods on hand as at December 31, 2019 and prepare the necessary adjustments for the correct presentation of the related inventory and expense accounts; and (iv) record the equipment and supplies received as donation in-kind from other local sources pursuant to Item IV.C of COA Circular No. 2014-002 for proper accounting and monitoring thereof; and 

BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction:
Allocation for wages of job personnel in DRRM fund have already been extinguished effective CY 2020 by the Management.

The donations might not have been booked up yet but the MDRRMO has proofs that the items have been transferred to the recipients.

The 3 units of generator set were already transferred under the accountability of the MDRRM Officer, while the other three sets were already transferred to the 3 barangays (Brgy. Calawag, Lourdes and Poblacion) submitted to the Audit Team.

Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) for 1 unit of a single door-refrigerator was submitted;

14 acknowledgment receipts of family packs (5), blankets (10) and shelter kits (30) each recipient were submitted in compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
Payment of wages for job personnel were still observed during the first semester of CY 2020 as discussed in AOM No. 2021-006. Audit recommendation was implemented in the second semester of CY 2020.

Some disbursements were still recorded as outright expense instead of the appropriate inventory account as reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the: (i) SWMB to make representations and coordination with the CENRO and NSWMC to fast track the review of the SWMP; and (ii) Municipal Budget Officer to provide sufficient appropriation for the implementation of programs/projects/activities embodied in the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian, enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems acquired through inhalation of fine particles produced during incineration and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already appropriated P1,000,000.00 in CY 2020 to be used for the purchase of lot for the Sanitary Landfill of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian still failed to enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning of wastes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Local Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-001 dated February 22, 2017 particularly, on the priority projects and disbursements charged against the Fund. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001, before processing claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: Some of the lacking documents were already submitted.
observations.reason: There were still disbursements in CY 2020 which do not directly contribute to capital development of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Local School Board: (i) observe the timely preparation, submission and approval of the Annual SEF Budget as prescribed by the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017; and (ii) ensure that PPAs incorporated in the SEF Budget are only those expenses allowed to be charged against the fund as enumerated in the same Joint Circular to eliminate expenditures that are not among the primary purpose for which the fund was established.  

We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pursuant to Item 6.3 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: Annual SEF Budget and Utilization Report of SEF were submitted to the Audit Team on February 21, 2021.  PPAs in CY 2020 included in the SEF budget were in compliance with DepEd-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the former Municipal Mayor submit a satisfactory written explanation on the reason/s for their failure to certify/approve each of the said disbursement vouchers; and 

Municipal Mayor, former Mayor and the former ICO-Municipal Treasurer (i) submit a reasonable explanation for signing the checks and releasing payment for DVs totaling ₱9,335,790.98 despite the incomplete certifications and/or approval thereon; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that no disbursement of government funds is made without the complete certification and approval of proper officials pursuant to Section 4(5) of PD No. 1445 and Section 344 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer submitted a written explanation pertaining to 37 disbursement vouchers without her certification.
observations.reason:
Other concerned municipal officials failed to submit the written explanations stated in the audit recommendation.

Other specific actions taken by the Management, if any, could not be validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed staled checks and submit the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents to the Accounting Office; and

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to bring back the cash in bank account in its correct balance and to secure snapshots of the bank statements if the bank statements are not yet available.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to stop recording/recognizing cash advances as outright expense; to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each cash advance to ensure accuracy and correctness; to prepare adjusting journal entries to establish cash advance previously recognized as expense amounting to P997,114.29; and to refrain granting of cash advances for special purpose activity to employees not bonded.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers for each Cash Advances of Accountable Officers have been prepared and maintained but other recommendations were still not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to observe appropriate accounting entries as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to avoid misstatements and fairly present the balances of each individual accounts in the financial statements and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

Require the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to update the Leave Cards (LC) on a timely basis and to furnish the Accounting office with a copy of Schedule of Earned Leave Credits with money for all LGU personnel as basis by the Accounting Office to record the leave accruals and in the meantime that budget is unavailable, it is advise that this information be disclosed as unrecorded liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.managementAction: The submission of disbursement vouchers has improved.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure strict adherence with the required time frame for the submission of accounts; and

Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to approve and process only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentations and approvals.
observations.managementAction: The submission of trial balance and quarterly financial statements has improved.

observations.recommendation:
Require the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to submit to COA the perfected contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof for COA’s review immediately; to submit prospective contracts within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract; and to ensure that all information required in the contracts and its supporting documents are properly filled up; and

Revisit the internal control policies over LGU procurement operation to identify and address these recurring lapses and delay over submission of contracts.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the various BIR Revenue Memorandum Circulars by withholding the correct tax rates applicable to the transactions and to remit the P198,922.78 immediately.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the purpose of cash advances are on the face of the disbursement vouchers and that are duly supported with complete Documents at the time of grant of cash advance including his/her certification that the previous cash advance of the employee/officer has been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare demand letter for each employee requiring them to liquidate or refund unused cash advances and cause the withholding of salaries for those who failed to liquidate within the time frame, if warranted; 

Advise the Local Chief Executive to impose a “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy and to impose administrative sanctions against accountable officers/employees who continue to neglect their obligations in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular 2012-001.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments for suppliers are made thru checks and consider establishing a petty cash fund to address petty expenditures of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Submission of liquidations papers were done partially.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the GSO to initiate the immediate disposal proceedings by preparing Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) showing the itemized list and complete description of the assets and Appraisal Report showing the appraised value of the assets.

Require the GSO to transfer the four unserviceable motor vehicles in one designated area pending the disposal of the items.
observations.managementAction: The designated GSO already submitted an IIRUP but was duly returned to incorrect use of report/form. Likewise, not all unserviceable properties were included in the report.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure strict compliance with the transfer of all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund books.

Require the Municipal Accountant with the assistance of the MDRRMO to prepare necessary adjusting entries in order to reclassify in the Disaster and Rescue Equipment account all those procured items or equipment relative to calamity fund.

Require the Human Resource Management Officer to fill up vacant positions and adopt innovative ways to ensure there is a wider publication and posting;

Advise the Local Chief Executive to ensure that employees who are designated to other positions do not hold incompatible duties/function.

observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Cashbook’s balance and the accounting books were not done regularly.

observations.recommendation: Direct all Revenue Collectors to prepare RAAF while the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF and submit to COA the said monthly reports in the prescribed form provided in the NGAS Manual not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: Monthly RAAFs were only submitted during cash examinations.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Local Chief Executive (LCE) assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2017 and to strictly comply with the submission of AAPSI in the ensuing annual audit reports.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the report and that the LDRRMO to submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to COA Auditor and to furnish the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) and the Department of the Interior and Local Government-Local Government Operations Office (DILG-LGOO).
observations.managementAction: Quarterly reports were submitted instead of monthly calamity reports.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LCE to create a Municipal Council for Protection of Children (MCPC) and to ensure that programs, projects and activities are responsive to the needs of the children and pursuant to those identified in the Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC).

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to ensure the timely completion of the programs, projects and activities.
Advise the Municipal Engineer to consider extending assistance to the barangays in preparing program of works.
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that funds for 20% development funds are transferred without delay to its bank account on a monthly basis.

Establish and maintain the Petty Cash Fund under the imprest system. 

Establish PCF equivalent to an amount sufficient to cover only expenses that are difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by check, pursuant to COA-Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  

The Annual Procurement Plan (APP) should include requirements for common-use office supplies to avoid purchases of the same through cash advances. 

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission of official receipts and other supporting documents to support the liquidation of cash advances/PCF as proof of payments and refrain from using Reimbursement Expenses Receipts (RERs).

Maintain a PCF cashbook and record therein transactions daily.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash disbursements were made thru a reimbursement basis.
Notice of Suspension was issued, and the management has partially submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
Require the BAC Secretary –

to submit to COA the approved contracts together with its supporting papers for the said perfected projects for COA’s review.  
to submit prospective contracts within 5 days upon its perfection in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.
Advise management headed by the Local Chief Executive to revisit the internal control policies over LGU procurement operations to identify and address these recurring lapses over submission of contracts.
observations.managementAction: Contracts were partially submitted with lacking supporting documents.
observations.reason: The contracts submitted were returned to the management due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required valid supporting documents for various transactions of disbursement to ensure proper internal control and to readily determine validity of the accounting entries made.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit liquidations or supporting documents of all unliquidated cash advances to COA, and to strictly observe the COA rules and regulations in the disbursement of public funds.  Otherwise, appropriate sanctions will be enforced by this Commission.

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SLs by year for the Development Fund, Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and the collections and remittances of the collectors to facilitate verification and monitoring of the said accounts’ balances
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office was still deficient in complying the appropriate form of SLs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: closely monitor the daily collections and remittances from all collectors; verify and count, not only the cash remitted but also the initial and ending balance of accountable forms on hand, to ensure that all collections are properly accounted and remitted intact by all collectors concerned, to prevent loss or misuse of government funds; and ensure that the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms be prepared properly and correctly in order to monitor the movement of accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: To conduct regular remittance, daily remittance of office collector and weekly or twice a week remittance of Cash Tickets from collection of Bus Terminal. Prepare monthly RAAF

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management enjoin strict compliance with the provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management to observe awarding of contract to the LCRB rather than among the bidders based on the unit price. Notices to be posted in PhilGEPs website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in its procurement activities, management should strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  Revise the awarded contract amount as necessary.

We recommended that Management require the contractor to rectify the damaged intake portions of the water supply system.  Should the contractor be unable to repair the damaged portion, undertake the repair itself and charge the costs for repair against the contractor as provided in R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommend that management instruct both offices to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards for properties owned and conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of the PPE accounts.  The differences per physical count and book balances must be investigated cleared and reconciled immediately.
observations.managementAction: Management to reconcile balances between the two funds through conduct of physical count and determination of serviceability of recorded inventory items
observations.reason: Management was unable to conduct physical count and determine serviceability of recorded items.  Instead, ownership records (Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment) was updated but item status was not updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management notify the Office of the Auditor of any deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours through submission of the aforementioned report in accordance with the said Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management to provide notices of delivery to COA.
observations.reason: Personnel concerned not able to submit the notice of delivery to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 
• Formulate a disaster recovery plan to secure the agency’s data and information systems.
• Set policies in securing data which includes determining the frequency in which data and records are backed-up and the location to which these back-ups are stored.
observations.managementAction: Management to formulate policies regarding safeguard to backup agency data in cases of emergency.
observations.reason: Back-up of agency data done at employee level with no policies provided by top management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the contracts including all the necessary supporting documents for review and evaluation.  Implement strictly the provisions of Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. With strict adherence, immediate corrective measures can be done for all deficiencies noted in the course review, evaluation, and inspection.
observations.managementAction: Management to submit contracts and Purchase Orders with the deadline set.
observations.reason: Several procurement of goods were not submitted to COA within time period with the contracts determined to be submitted thereon with the related voucher.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned departments and personnel to fast track the implementation of the project to avoid incurring additional cost due to delay.
observations.managementAction: Management to fast track implementation of project.
observations.reason: Project on-going as of report date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to prepare the LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness in the DRRM Investment Plan pursuant to 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommend that Management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and Utilization Report to the auditors’ office.`
observations.reason: Monthly utilization report not submitted to auditor’s office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management enforce strictly the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, aggressively follow-up long outstanding cash advances and compel them to settle their accounts. And after due notice/demand, cause the withholding of the amount due the concerned officials. 

We also recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers giving additional cash advances unless the previous ones are settled.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand liquidation of advances prior to release of new cash advances.
observations.reason: Balances from several transactions from prior periods still unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management strictly require the recipient NGOs/POs to submit the duly certified final Utilization Report to support its liquidation pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001. Issue demand letter to the NGOs/POs. If no settlement is made, institute legal sanctions pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations applicable for violation of the provisions of the above COA Circular. 
  	
We also recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers releasing fund transfers to the same NGOs/POs unless the previous ones are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand liquidation from NGOs/PO.  Pursue writeoff of long outstanding accounts.
observations.reason:
Balances from several transactions from prior periods still unliquidated.
Difficulty in serving demand letters to NGOs/POs involved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management discontinue the deduction from the salaries of the loans from thrift banks/lending institutions for those who have net take home pay of below P4,000.00.

We also recommend that the MOA entered into between the banks and the LGU for the collection arrangement should not be renewed.
observations.managementAction: Management to refrain deducting other than the mandatory contributions from the payroll.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties of the LGU and submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the books with the inventory report and property cards.
observations.managementAction: Create an Inventory Committee, Conduct Physical Count and Reconcile Records.
observations.reason: The Management committee has conducted the physical count of all properties and submitted the RPCPPE, however, the same were not reconciled with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the affected accounts in conformity with Section 04 (p), Chapter 2 of the New Government Accounting system (NGAS) for LGUs.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of the Section 79 of P.D 1445 on the disposal of the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to make an entry based on prepared IIRUP
observations.reason: The IIRUP has already been prepared but still no adjustment made on the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) coordinate with the Punong Barangay of Barangay Caguray to identify the rightful owner of the Land costing P300,000.00. If it is proven that it will remain in the possession of the Municipality, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the actual acquisition cost of the land and fast track the transfer of the certificate of title under the name of the Municipality to avoid any third party claims and to recognized expense on the inventory items amounting to P262,550.00 that were already distributed to various Barangays; and (ii) require the concerned officials to locate the service vehicles amounting to P250,000.00 which was not found during the ocular inspection.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

Municipal Accountant to provide full disclosure on the valuation of land acquired through non-exchanged transactions and other important information in the Notes to Financial Statement as required by PPSAS 17. Prepare the necessary adjustments on the error made in the recording of service vehicle amounting to P720,000.00 for fair and accurate presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position;

Inventory Committee to ensure that the RPCPPE are based on the results of actual physical count so that any unrecorded PPE found on location and/or losses discovered during the physical count could be immediately investigated and adjusted in the books of accounts; and

ICO Municipal Treasurer to prepare the IIRUP, together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items. While waiting for the proper disposal of PPE, the concerned officials may start the test for impairment of PPE totaling P23,141,507.95 for the proper valuation in the Statement of Financial Position. If the carrying amount exceeded the recoverable service amount, the Municipal Accountant shall recognize an impairment loss in the books of accounts.
observations.reason:
Parcel of land amounting to P300,000.00 was still not covered with a Certificate of Title under the name of the Municipality.

Inventory Committee had overlooked the conduct of complete physical count on all properties of the Municipality and to reconcile the balances of the RPCPPE with the corresponding ledger balances hence this finding I reiterated in Part II of this Report.

Preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for the unserviceable properties was still not given priority by the concerned offices hence, this finding is still included in Part II of this Report..

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) initiate the preparation and submission of the request for write-off, together with the required documents, for the unliquidated cash advances of the deceased Municipal Mayor totaling P1,539,241.54 to the Commission on Audit for proper evaluation and disposition pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 for fair presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements; (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of salaries and other emoluments due to Ms. Elizabeth Itumay to enforce the liquidation/settlement of her long outstanding cash accountabilities; and (c) henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: (i) issue demand letters requiring the immediate settlement of the remaining outstanding cash advances especially those who are no longer connected with the agency but are still alive; (ii) refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance given is first settled and proper accounting thereof is made in accordance with Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular N. 97-002; (iii) require full settlement of cash advances upon submission of liquidation reports. Any excess from the amount granted should be simultaneously refunded; and (iv) make the necessary adjustments on the reclassification of the Due from Officers and Employees account totaling P2,545,583.04 to a proper Receivable account depending on the nature of the cash advance for fair and accurate presentation of accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.reason:
The Management still failed to comply with COA Circular 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances resulting to further accumulation of outstanding cash advances.

Suspension of salaries of erring Accountable Officers was still not withheld.

Preparation of demand letters were overlooked hence, no demand letters were issued to the current and previous employees with remaining outstanding cash advances.

Additional cash advances were still granted even previous cash advances were still unsettled. These observations are again reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board to observe the timely preparation, submission and approval of the Annual SEF Budget as prescribed by the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017. We also recommended that the LSB ensure that PPAs incorporated in the SEF Budget are only those expenses allowed to be charged against the fund as enumerated in the same Joint Circular to eliminate expenditures that are not among the primary purpose for which the fund was established.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR RA No. 9184 pursuant to Item 6.3 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. Requisitions for procurements with the same purpose and needed at or about the same time be consolidated into a single requisition and undergo competitive bidding, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR.
observations.reason:
Budget was still prepared and approved beyond the prescribed period.

SEF budget was still utilized for PPAs which are not among the PPAs allowed to be charged against the said fund.

BAC still failed to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Development Council, ensure that only expenditures/projects that directly contribute to capital development be included in the local development plan and refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures in accordance with Items 3 and 4 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.
observations.reason: Payment for wages of Job Order personnel, fuel and other expenditures totaling P1,800,765.90 do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and were still charged against the appropriation of 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare the GL and SLs for CYs 2017 and 2018 in accordance with Sections 9 and 10 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

observations.reason: The deficiencies were still observed in CY 2019 and thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 1 in Part II of this Annual Audit Report (AAR).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete set of Financial Statements for CY 2018, including the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to Section 76 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004. Otherwise, cause the suspension of her salaries until such reports have been submitted to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.

observations.reason:

Moreover, similar deficiencies were observed in CY 2019 and thus included under Audit Observation No. 2 in Part II of this Annual Audit Report (AAR).
