observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record the necessary adjustments to the affected accounts for fair and accurate presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements; (ii) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (iii) cause the immediate settlement of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof; and (iv) verify the validity and existence of the Accounts Payable by informing the concerned payees of their outstanding claims and determine whether these are still valid and enforceable especially those that have been dormant for two (2) years or more, if found to be not valid, proper reversion should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund; and
Municipal Treasurer to: (i) send written notices to the payees one (1) month before the check becomes stale as required in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual. Cancelled stale checks shall be reported and attached to the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) at the period of cancellation; and (ii) withhold the payment of salaries of concerned AOs, if necessary, to enforce liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the return of the unexpended balances of the Due to NGAs totaling P608,929.86 to the respective Source Agencies for proper presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the Financial Statements and/or make representations with the source agency for the possible reprogramming of the unexpended balances to other PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) Inventory Committee  to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding duly accomplished RPCPPE, and henceforth ensure that said physical count are strictly and timely conducted every year;                (b) Storekeeper/Acting Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPCPPE with  the corresponding ledger balances and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; (c) Municipal Accountant, upon reconciliation, to prepare the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts and determine the persons accountable, for any losses therein; and (d) Acting Property Officer to fast track the preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable items.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Disposal Committee to facilitate the preparation of the Appraisal Report for the eventual disposal of the unserviceable properties. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor is advised to fast track the processing of the Deed of Donation for the transfer of ownership of the land to Barangay Caguray for the eventual derecognition in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and the TWG to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
BAC and its TWG to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team; and
Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents in the claims for payment, and ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, as required under Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and TWG to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality are strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly, during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to, legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
BAC and its Technical Working Group (TWG) to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team;
Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that all the requirements of the Detailed Engineering Design for infrastructure projects to be implemented are complete prior to the procurement proceedings so that information and/or specifications are duly incorporated in the bidding documents; and (ii) see to it that the computation of ABC are in accordance with the latest guidelines issued by the DPWH to prevent excessive project cost; and
Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit the lacking supporting documents, and henceforth ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documents before processing claims for payment; and (ii) ensure that retention money equivalent to 10% of the total amount due are deducted from progress payments of projects with delays, even after completion of 50% of the value of works.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the: (i) SWMB to make representations and coordination with the CENRO and NSWMC to fast track the review of the SWMP; and (ii) Municipal Budget Officer to provide sufficient appropriation for the implementation of programs/projects/activities embodied in the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian, enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems acquired through inhalation of fine particles produced during incineration and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Local Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-001 dated February 22, 2017 particularly, on the priority projects and disbursements charged against the Fund.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001, before processing claims for payment.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Local School Board: (i) observe the timely preparation, submission and approval of the Annual SEF Budget as prescribed by the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017; and (ii) ensure that PPAs incorporated in the SEF Budget are only those expenses allowed to be charged against the fund as enumerated in the same Joint Circular to eliminate expenditures that are not among the primary purpose for which the fund was established.
We also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pursuant to Item 6.3 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare the GLs and SLs for CYs 2017, 2018 and 2019 pursuant to Section 114 of PD No. 1445, (b) consider augmentation of personnel in the Accounting Office to assist the Municipal Accountant in preparing the journals and maintaining the ledgers; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the municipal books of accounts maintained by the Accountant are complete and in accordance with the formats prescribed under the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the (a) Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts, (b) Cash Flow Statements for General Fund and Trust Funds, and (c) disclosures for prior year’s comparative balances of asset and liability accounts in the Notes to the Financial Statements, contrary to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 and thus deviated from the pertinent paragraphs of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) Nos. 1, 2 and 24, thereby limiting the scope of the audit.
With the issuance of the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units in connection with the adoption of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS), the composition of Financial Statements has been updated to include the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) as prescribed under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016. The updated list of components of the Financial Statements is as follows:
Statement of Financial Position
Statement of Financial Performance
Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity
Cash Flow Statement
Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual Amounts
Notes to Financial Statements
Meanwhile, Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 requires the Accountants to submit to the Auditor the year-end financial statements and schedules on or before February 14 of each year.
Contrary to the aforementioned provision of COA Circular No. 2016-004, the Municipal Accountant failed to submit the following financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2019:
SCBAA for the General Fund (GF);
SCBAA for the Special Education Fund (SEF);
Cash Flow Statements for GF; and
Cash Flow Statements for Trust Fund (TF).
We have also observed that the Notes to the Financial Statements did not disclose the comparative balances of asset and liability accounts for CY 2018.
The Accountant submitted separate cash flow statements for the quarters ended March 31, June 30, September 30 and December 31, 2019, but those are not enough to satisfy the requirements for annual financial reporting. It is necessary for the Accountant to consolidate the quarterly statements and submit a duly signed Cash Flow Statement for the whole year. We have noted that the Accountant has done that for SEF, but not for GF and TF.
The lack of SCBAA in the Financial Statements is a deviation from Paragraph 24 of IPSAS 1 and Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 24, to wit:
IPSAS 1 - Presentation of Financial Statements
Public sector entities are typically subject to budgetary limits in the form of appropriations or budget authorizations (or equivalent), which may be given effect through authorizing legislation. General purpose financial reporting by public sector entities may provide information on whether resources were obtained and used in accordance with the legally adopted budget. Entities that make publicly available their approved budget(s) are required to comply with the requirements of IPSAS 24, Presentation of Budget Information in Financial Statements. XXX
IPSAS 24 - Presentation of Budget Information in Financial Statements
Subject to the requirements of paragraph 21, an entity shall present a comparison of the budget amounts for which it is held publicly accountable and actual amounts, either as a separate additional financial statement or as additional budget columns in the financial statements currently presented in accordance with IPSASs. The comparison of budget and actual amounts shall present separately for each level of legislative oversight:
The original and final budget amounts;
The actual amounts on a comparable basis; and
By way of note disclosure, an explanation of material differences between the budget for which the entity is held publicly accountable and actual amounts, unless such explanation is included in other public documents issued in conjunction with the financial statements, and a cross reference to those documents is made in the notes.
Non-submission of the Cash Flow Statements for the year ended December 31, 2019 is contrary to Paragraphs 1 and 3 of IPSAS 2 which provide:
IPSAS 2 – Cash Flow Statements
An entity that prepares and presents financial statements under the accrual basis of accounting shall prepare a cash flow statement in accordance with the requirements of this Standard, and shall present it as an integral part of its financial statements for each period for which financial statements are presented.
This Standard applies to all public sector entities other than Government Business Enterprises.
Non-disclosure of comparative balances of asset and liability accounts for the previous year in the Notes to the Financial Statements is not in accord with Paragraph 53 of IPSAS 1 which states that:
Except when an IPSAS permits or requires otherwise, comparative information shall be disclosed in respect of the previous period for all amounts reported in the financial statements. Comparative information shall be included for narrative and descriptive information when it is relevant to an understanding of the current period’s financial statements.
The aforementioned deficiencies in the CY 2019 Financial Statements have significantly limited the scope of our audit. We were not able to conduct a review on the cash flows and the comparison of budget and actual amounts of revenue and expenditure items due to lack of the corresponding Financial Statements therefor. Furthermore, we were precluded from rendering a complete validation of the reported account balances of asset and liability accounts due to inadequate disclosure of information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
Attention is invited to Sections 122 and 127 of PD No. 1445 which provide:
Section 122. Submission of Reports – Whenever deemed necessary in the exigencies of the service, the Commission may under regulations issued by it require the agency heads, chief accountants, budget officers, cashiers, disbursing officers, administrative or personnel officers, and other responsible officials of the various agencies to submit trial balances, physical inventory reports, current plantilla of personnel, and such other reports as may be necessary for the exercise of its functions.
Failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.
Section 127. Administrative disciplinary action. Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the President (Prime Minister), any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against the said public officer who, upon being found guilty thereof after hearing, shall be meted out such penalty as is commensurate with the degree of his guilt in accordance with the Civil Service Law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements imposed in this Code shall be conclusive proof that the public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete set of Financial Statements for CY 2019 with adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 and IPSAS 1, 2 and 24. Otherwise, cause the suspension of her salaries until such reports have been submitted to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor through the Inventory Committee, require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report thereon to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with property and accounting records; and
ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant prepare the Property Cards and PPELC, respectively, per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2019 and promptly submit the same to the Audit Team along with duly certified copies of bank statements and debit/credit memoranda for verification, pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and the former Municipal Mayor submit a satisfactory written explanation on the reason/s for their failure to certify/approve each of the said disbursement vouchers; and
Municipal Mayor, former Mayor and the former ICO-Municipal Treasurer (i) submit a reasonable explanation for signing the checks and releasing payment for DVs totaling ₱9,335,790.98 despite the incomplete certifications and/or approval thereon; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that no disbursement of government funds is made without the complete certification and approval of proper officials pursuant to Section 4(5) of PD No. 1445 and Section 344 of RA No. 7160.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the general and subsidiary ledgers for all accounts pertaining to cash advances and submit a summary schedule of subsidiary ledger balances and the SUCA as of December 31, 2019;
impose strictly the provision requiring all accountable officers to fully and promptly liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served or at the end of each year, whichever comes first; and
cause the issuance of demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2019.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, cause the immediate issuance of a demand letter to the Provincial Government requiring the return of unexpended fund transfers.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the:
MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports on the same with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments;
Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects; and
Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to achieve the  socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson (a) submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2019 either in printed or electronic form, whichever is more convenient or cost-efficient; and (b) henceforth observe regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in such manner and timing as may be deemed reasonable in view of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) propose to the Sangguniang Bayan the adoption of the LEE Manual as recommended under LBC No. 111 dated June 10, 2016; (b) assign or designate the relevant local officials who will be responsible in planning for and implementing the new LEEs; and (c) enjoin the said officials to conduct an evaluation on the operations of the municipal public market in accordance with Section 4 of the LEE Manual.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer (1) submit a written explanation on why taxes withheld were not remitted intact to the BIR; (2) cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible interests, penalties and surcharges for late payments; and (3) henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld are remitted intact and on the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.

We recommended that the
Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the unremitted balance of the Due to PhilHealth account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate;
OIC-Municipal Treasurer, (a) based on the analysis prepared by the Municipal Accountant, remit to the PhilHealth all contributions from prior periods so that only those from the preceding month shall remain as the balance of Due to PhilHealth account; and (b) henceforth, ensure the prompt remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed under PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s. 2014 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties; and
Municipal Mayor cause the refund of ₱4,895.71 by concerned local officials who failed to remit PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed time.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2018 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (b) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.
The Management has not yet provided an official reply to this audit observation as of this writing.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed staled checks and submit the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents to the Accounting Office; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to bring back the cash in bank account in its correct balance; to secure snapshots of the bank statements if the bank statements are not yet available.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to stop recording/recognizing cash advances as outright expense; to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each cash advance to ensure accuracy and correctness; to prepare adjusting journal entries to establish cash advance previously recognized as expense amounting to P997,114.29; and to refrain granting of cash advances for special purpose activity to employees not bonded.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Create inventory committee; to prepare a timetable for the conduct of Physical Inventory including Local Road Network; to direct all Department Heads to coordinate with the Inventory Committee for a cohesive planning and to augment the workforce in the GSO specially during the conduct of physical inventory taking;
Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant and Acting General Services Officer to maintain complete property ledger cards and property cards respectively for all PPE owned by the agency; and
Direct the Acting General Services Officer to maintain Subsidiary Records and Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment (ARE) on all equipment as well as Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for small items.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to observe appropriate accounting entries as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to avoid misstatements and fairly present the balances of each individual accounts in the financial statements and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the designated Head of MEEDO or MAO to submit the financial statements of the waterworks;
Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the assets, liabilities and equity of the waterworks in the LGU and to maintain subsidiary ledgers and financial statements necessary in planning and evaluating the performance of the waterworks; and
Install or designate a new Head of Office of the MEEDO to oversee the operations of the waterworks and other economic ventures of the LGU.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to update the Leave Cards (LC) on a timely basis and to furnish the Accounting office with a copy of Schedule of Earned Leave Credits with money for all LGU personnel as basis by the Accounting Office to record the leave accruals and in the meantime that budget is unavailable, it is advise that this information be disclosed as unrecorded liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure strict adherence with the required time frame for the submission of accounts; and
Instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to approve and process only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentations and approvals.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) to provide an explanation and identify the difficulties in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan;
Direct all Department Heads of LGU and Focal Persons to strictly observe the time frame prescribed in the preparation of annual budget; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to review and ensure that forms of the annual budget are in accordance with the latest prescribed forms and that computations in the report are all accurate.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to submit to COA the perfected contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof for COA’s review immediately; to submit prospective contracts within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract; and to ensure that all information required in the contracts and its supporting documents are properly filled up; and
Revisit the internal control policies over LGU procurement operation to identify and address these recurring lapses and delay over submission of contracts.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain records and subsidiary ledger for each barangay to facilitate the release of RPT and CTC within the prescribed period;
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to release the P239,642.70 shares from RPT and CTC to the barangays immediately; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain the records and disbursement of RPT and CTC shares of the barangay under General Fund books.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the various BIR Revenue Memorandum Circulars by withholding the correct tax rates applicable to the transactions and to remit the P198,922.78 immediately.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the concerned employees to submit the lacking documents;
Instruct the Municipal HRMO to make necessary adjustment in the leave cards of officers  or employees  whose monetization were deducted from the sick leave instead of vacation leave and to prepare Summary of Leave credit balances of all employees at the end of the year for employees’ information and ready reference;  and
Refrain from utilizing the budget for Terminal Leave Pay for payment of monetization as the two accounts have different purpose.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the monitoring of the approval of the Solid Waste Management Plan, closure of the open dumpsite, establishment of sanitary landfill, and strict compliance of segregation of wastes under all its jurisdiction starting on LGU premises; and
Increase the number of MRF pursuant to Section 33 of RA 9003 in coordination with the barangays of the Municipality in order to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Local School Board to prepare and submit Annual SEF Budget with the prescribed form and timeline pursuant to the new Joint Circular Dep Ed-DBM and DILG No. 1 s. 2017 and to ensure that SEF funds are released and utilized pursuant to Section 272 of RA 7160; and
Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to secure the Program of Work of the subject disbursements and submit to COA.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Ensure timely implementation of all 20% development projects;
Require the MPDC to provide specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs and the related target schedule for each procurement activities in order to ensure timely implementation of projects; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to provide sufficient allocation in the General Fund for operating requirements of the LGU-Magsaysay Managed Water System (LMMWS) and to ascertain proper charging of expenses to the 20% DF.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Impose strict compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and to ensure that GAD Plan and Budget are prepared and submitted to DILG for their review and approval.;
Require the GAD Focal Person and MPDC to conduct gender mainstreaming to identify the appropriate activities responsive to the needs of the LGU constituents; establish and maintain a GAD Database in implementing the Magna Carta for Women which helps in formulating GAD-related activities based on information gathered; and prepare and submit Annual GAD Accomplishment Report including all its supporting documents as prescribed under DILG-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the GAD Plan and Budget are in accordance with the JMC.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the accountant to provide depreciation in the books of accounts and adjust previous balances not provided with depreciation.  Facilitate the creation of an inventory team to conduct inventory of properties owned by the LGU for the purpose of having better accuracy in presenting items and balances in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department make the necessary adjusting entries to record the accrued liability pertaining to earned leave benefits of the employees for the current and previous years after ensuring the correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances as of December 31, 2019. Review at each reporting date and adjust to reflect the current best estimate to ensure that employee’s benefits are properly accounted for and disclosed in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: closely monitor the daily collections and remittances from all collectors; verify and count, not only the cash remitted but also the initial and ending balance of accountable forms on hand, to ensure that all collections are properly accounted and remitted intact by all collectors concerned, to prevent loss or misuse of government funds; and ensure that the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms be prepared properly and correctly in order to monitor the movement of accountable forms.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management enjoin strict compliance with the provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that in its procurement activities, management should strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  Revise the awarded contract amount as necessary.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the contractor to rectify the damaged intake portions of the water supply system.  Should the contractor be unable to repair the damaged portion, undertake the repair itself and charge the costs for repair against the contractor as provided in R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the accountant to provide depreciation in the books of accounts and adjust previous balances not provided with depreciation.  Facilitate the creation of an inventory team to conduct inventory of properties owned by the LGU for the purpose of having better accuracy in presenting items and balances in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department make the necessary adjusting entries to record the accrued liability pertaining to earned leave benefits of the employees for the current and previous years after ensuring the correctness of the accumulated leave credit balances as of December 31, 2019. Review at each reporting date and adjust to reflect the current best estimate to ensure that employee’s benefits are properly accounted for and disclosed in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: closely monitor the daily collections and remittances from all collectors; verify and count, not only the cash remitted but also the initial and ending balance of accountable forms on hand, to ensure that all collections are properly accounted and remitted intact by all collectors concerned, to prevent loss or misuse of government funds; and ensure that the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms be prepared properly and correctly in order to monitor the movement of accountable forms.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management enjoin strict compliance with the provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that in its procurement activities, management should strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  Revise the awarded contract amount as necessary.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the contractor to rectify the damaged intake portions of the water supply system.  Should the contractor be unable to repair the damaged portion, undertake the repair itself and charge the costs for repair against the contractor as provided in R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

Recommendations:

      We recommended Management to:


      Direct the Municipal Accountant to -


       a. Record the adjusting entries to restore the amount  of  unreleased
          checks to appropriate cash accounts;


    b. Thereafter, record undeposited collections to proper cash account;


       c. Review the records  of  the  Municipal  Treasurer  for  unreleased
          checks at year-end;


       d. Submit immediately the  bank  reconciliation  statements  for  DBP
          Account Nos.  0920-027027-160  and  00003559-205  from  August  to
          December 2019 after the receipt of the  bank  statements/snapshots
          from the depository bank;


      Direct the Municipal Treasurer to -


       e. Request reversal of all service charges from the  depository  bank
          due to account balances falling below the required  average  daily
          balance; and


       f. Pending the retrieval of MOA, transfer the  remaining  balance  of
          the accounts that have incurred avoidable service charges  to  the
          General Fund to prevent further reduction of government funds.

Recommendations:

      We recommended Management to:

     1. Strictly adhere to the pertinent  provisions  of  applicable  laws,
        rules and regulations governing SEF disbursements to avoid charging
        expenses that are administrative in nature to the fund; and
     2. Maximize the utilization  of SEF  to  attain  its  objectives  and
        consider allocating some amounts  to  projects  and  programs  that
        could address the needs of every school in Magsaysay.

Recommendation:

      We recommended Management to  assess  and  consider  prioritizing  the
procurement for the supply and delivery of the  Nail  Art  Starter  Kit  and
ensure that  necessary  trainings  are  provided  to  the  beneficiaries  as
provided in the approved project proposal and Memorandum  of  Agreement  for
the implementation  of  KABUHAYAN  program  thru  proponent  accredited  co-
partner (ACP)/proponent beneficiary dated July 9, 2019.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

     1.  Require  the  Municipal  Engineering  Office  for  the   immediate
        preparation of the documentary  requirements  and   the  submission
        thereof to the concerned office as required  in the  6.5.4  of  the
        DILG Memorandum Circular; and


     2. Ensure strict compliance with the relevant provisions of  the  DILG
        Memorandum Circular No. 2019-71 dated May 20, 2019 relative to  the
        implementation of Local Government Support  Fund  -  Assistance  to
        Municipalities Program.

Recommendations:

      We recommended Management to:


     1. Stop utilization of  20%  Development  Fund  for  expenses  not  in
        adherence with the set  guidelines  of  DILG-DBM  Joint  Memorandum
        Circular 2017-1; and


     2.  Ensure  that  20%  Development  Fund  is  allocated   to   finance
        developmental projects that shall contribute to the  attainment  of
        desirable socio-economic development and  environmental  management
        outcomes and see to it that charges against this fund shall partake
        only nature of investment and non-capital expenditures.

Recommendation:


      We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  strictly  implement  the
deduction of retention money amounting to ten percent (10%)  of  the  amount
due the contractor to cover the cost of uncorrected discovered  defects  and
third party liabilities pursuant to Section 6 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:


     1. Require the LDRRM  Officer  to  fully  implement  on  CY  2020  the
        unimplemented projects,  programs  and  activities  of  the  Annual
        Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan and Budget for CY
        2019; and


     2.   Maximize   the   utilization    of    LDRRMF    by    formulating
        programs/projects/activities     particularly     for      disaster
        preparedness, prevention and mitigation so that the LGU’s  ultimate
        objective  of  protecting  the  lives   and   properties   of   its
        constituents will be realized.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

     1. Require the Human Resource Development  Officer  (HRMO)  to  ensure
        that the provisions prescribed for a minimum monthly net  take  pay
        of P5,000.00 under the GAA FY 2019 and onwards be adhered to.

     2. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions
        stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take-home pay to promote an
        efficient and effective public service by ensuring that  government
        employees have enough resources to support their  daily  sustenance
        until the next payday.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Account prepared Journal Entry Voucher to correct the errors in recording in the Cash – Local Treasury account amounting to P1,040,501.15.

Loans payable were already paid by Municipality thus.
observations.reason:
Accountabilities of each former Municipal Treasurer on the collections that remained undeposited for more than two (2) decades amounting to P3,743,812.74 were still not identified thus, reclassification of the undeposited balance to proper Receivable account was not made.

Accounts Payable still includes long outstanding cash advances of officers and employees totaling P2,020,174.45.

The nature and origin of the net book reconciling items totaling P436,275.80 still could not be properly identified thus, necessary adjustments in CIB-Local Current Accounts were not made.

Accounts Payable still includes obligations to various employees and officials and suppliers which still remained unpaid for several years ranging from three (3) to almost 13 years amounting to P353,469.59 which were not supported with adequate documents to substantiate whether these are valid and/or legal claims.

The Municipal Accountant had overlooked the reconciliation of the Due from and Due to Other Funds account hence the accounts were not reconciled as of year-end of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer all funds received from NGAs from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books and all disbursements and transactions pertaining thereto shall be duly recorded therein for proper accounting and monitoring of project implementation and reporting to the respective source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred funds received from NGAs from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books.
observations.reason: Funds received from various NGAs amounting to P4,110.92 and P17,776.90 were still accounted in the books of the 20% Development Fund and Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide sufficient appropriation for the completion of the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan so that the programs/projects/activities embodied therein can be implemented accordingly pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian, enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning of wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems acquired through inhalation of fine particles produced during incineration and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment.
observations.managementAction: Municipality has already implemented a garbage collection schedule once a week within the Poblacion area.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still failed to appropriate funds for the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

The Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian still failed to enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning of wastes.

observations.managementAction: The management committed to the audit team that noted deficiencies will be addressed and all transactions are duly supported with proper and complete documentations.
observations.reason: Procurement of goods and infrastructure projects were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the recommendations thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that funds are utilized for the specific purpose for which they have been appropriated. In the event that appropriation for PPAs in the budget is determined to be deficient, augmentation thereof may be made within the same class of their respective appropriation pursuant to Section 336 of RA No. 7160. We further recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the:

LDRRM Officer to utilize the LDRRM Fund solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities such as early warning device, search, rescue and retrieval equipment and other useful equipage for disaster preparedness and disaster response pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 10121;

Municipal Accountant to: (i) transfer the CY 2012 unutilized LDRRM Fund which remained unutilized for more than five years in the Trust Fund to General Fund in compliance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121; (ii) transfer the CY 2018 unexpended appropriation for Capital Outlay which was erroneously transferred to the Trust Fund to the General Fund and henceforth ensure that unappropriated balances for CO shall remain in the General Fund until the project funded therefore is completed; (iii) refrain from recording purchases of goods and supplies as outright expense instead such shall be accounted under the inventory accounts which shall be properly monitored; and (iv) coordinate with the LDRRM Office for the proper inventory and reconciliation of the relief goods on hand as of December 31, 2018 and prepare the necessary adjustments for the correct presentation of the related inventory and expense accounts; and

BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects; and

We also recommended that the Management ensure that only the information required by COA Circular No. 2013-004 is displayed on signages, tarpaulins and other printed materials to avoid unnecessary expenditures that are disallowable in audit.
observations.managementAction:
The Management transferred the unutilized DRRM Fund that exceeds 5 years to the General Fund to be used for other social services to be determined by the Local Sanggunian at the end of the current year.

The Management committed that unappropriated balances for CO of succeeding projects will remain in the General Fund until the project completion.
observations.reason:
LDRRM Fund was still utilized for programs/projects/ activities (PPAs) which are not among the PPAs allowed to be charged against the said fund.

Relief goods and supplies for pre-positioning and stock piling amounting P178,774.00 under check no. 777397 were still accounted as an outright expense.

BAC still failed to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System, strictly observe the prescribed timelines for GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely preparation and submission to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and the subsequent review and endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Person committed the prompt formulation, preparation and submission of the GPB and GAD AR.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report were reviewed and endorsed by the DILG Provincial Office but were still submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.managementAction:
The correct rate of discount was applied in collections for CY 2019. The Provincial Government of Palawan already issued an amendment to its Revenue Code, providing for the correct discount rate on prompt payment of RPT.

Other specific actions taken by Management could not yet be determined and validated due to lack of the AAPSI.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to collect the amount of undercollection or notify the Audit Team of any action taken relative thereto.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports on the same with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments; and

Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects to the LCE and the Audit Team for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Municipal Engineer submitted report on the status of implementation of projects, programs and activities.
observations.reason: Low physical delivery rate was still observed in CY 2019 thus, reiterated under Audit Observation No. 8 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
The MDRRMO submitted a copy of the LDRRMFIP for CY 2018.

Other specific actions taken by Management could not yet be determined and validated due to lack of the AAPSI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO - Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,092,611.26 to the respective Trust Fund bank account and make the necessary journal entries in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer transferred the unexpended balance of LDRRMF and the Municipal Accountant recorded the transfer in the Trust Fund books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

prepare a subsidiary ledger for each audit disallowance or charge that has become final and executory based on the NFD issued and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team; and

submit a detailed report on the monthly installment payments for settlement of ND No. 14-001-100-13 for CY 2018 using the format prescribed, complete with references to specific documents, and update the same every month thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The former ICO-Municipal Treasurer submitted reports which provided adequate information on the settlement of ND No. 14-001-100 in the total amount of ₱951,394.90.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still failed to prepare a subsidiary ledger for each audit disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee and the Municipal Accountant (a) cause the immediate submission of the GPB, GAD AR and other GAD reports and documents requested by the Audit Team for CY 2018; and (b) henceforth, ensure timely submission of GAD related reports and documents in accordance with Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Focal Person submitted GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018.

Other specific actions taken by Management could not yet be determined and validated due to lack of the AAPSI.
observations.reason: The GAD GFPS Executive Committee failed to submit a copy of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement particularly for the eleven bank accounts immediately and to submit a revise list of outstanding checks with complete details;

Direct the Local Chief Executive to augment the work force assigned in the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of reports.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all paid disbursement vouchers are included in the Report of Checks Issued and forwarded to the Accounting Department for the timely and proper recording in the books.  

Direct the Municipal Accountant to enroll the bank accounts to online/ we-access to be able to keep track of its balances at any given time.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 
CY 2018
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has submitted all the BRS for CY 2019 as of Feb. 14, 2020 but the submission of  2019 BRS were still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the purpose of cash advances are on the face of the disbursement vouchers and that are duly supported with complete Documents at the time of grant of cash advance including his/her certification that the previous cash advance of the employee/officer has been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare demand letter for each employee requiring them to liquidate or refund unused cash advances and cause the withholding of salaries for those who failed to liquidate within the time frame, if warranted; 

Advise the Local Chief Executive to impose a “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy and to impose administrative sanctions against accountable officers/employees who continue to neglect their obligations in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular 2012-001.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments for suppliers are made thru checks and consider establishing a petty cash fund to address petty expenditures of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct both offices to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards for properties owned and conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of the PPE accounts.  The differences per physical count and book balances must be investigated cleared and reconciled immediately.
observations.managementAction: Management to reconcile balances between the two funds through conduct of physical count and determination of serviceability of recorded inventory items
observations.reason: Management was unable to conduct physical count and determine serviceability of recorded items.  Instead, ownership records (Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment) was updated but item status was not updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 
• Formulate a disaster recovery plan to secure the agency’s data and information systems.
• Set policies in securing data which includes determining the frequency in which data and records are backed-up and the location to which these back-ups are stored.
observations.managementAction: Management to formulate policies regarding safeguard to backup agency data in cases of emergency.
observations.reason: Back-up of agency data done at employee level with no policies provided by top management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the contracts including all the necessary supporting documents for review and evaluation.  Implement strictly the provisions of Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. With strict adherence, immediate corrective measures can be done for all deficiencies noted in the course review, evaluation, and inspection.
observations.managementAction: Management to submit contracts and Purchase Orders with the deadline set.
observations.reason: Several procurement of goods were not submitted to COA within time period with the contracts determined to be submitted thereon with the related voucher.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the concerned contractor to rectify the defects noted by the COA Technical Audit Specialist.  Inform the Audit Team Leader if corrections were already made for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management to instruct the contractor to rectify the defects noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish close coordination with BFAR and fisherfolks to ensure success and continuity of the project and to ensure through close monitoring that the CFLC operates in accordance with the MOA and CFLC guidelines.  Require the contractor to deliver the proper items as stated in the purchase order.
observations.managementAction: Management to provide an Executive Order for the usage of the CFLC

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned departments and personnel to fast track the implementation of the project to avoid incurring additional cost due to delay.
observations.managementAction: Management to fast track implementation of project.
observations.reason: Project on-going as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Accountant prepare and submit the required monthly and annual statements of the auditor’s office and ensure that requirements set by the Financial Management Operations Guidelines for the LGU are followed.
observations.managementAction: Management to provide SRE and SSAF to COA and DA for purposes of liquidation and reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the corresponding elected officials to secure from the Philippines Councilor’s League, Incorporated, a government issued official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management to correspond with League of Councilors to demand Official Receipts for the registration thereon.
observations.reason: Ongoing deliberation with courts regarding status of the League and the requirement to issue Official Receipts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to prepare the LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness in the DRRM Investment Plan pursuant to 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommend that Management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and Utilization Report to the auditors’ office.
observations.reason: Monthly utilization report not submitted to auditor’s office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management enforce strictly the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, aggressively follow-up long outstanding cash advances and compel them to settle their accounts. And after due notice/demand, cause the withholding of the amount due the concerned officials. 

We also recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers giving additional cash advances unless the previous ones are settled.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand to liquidation of advances prior to release of new cash advances.
observations.reason: Balances from several transactions from prior periods still unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management discontinue the deduction from the salaries of the loans from thrift banks/lending institutions for those who have net take home pay of below P4,000.00.

We also recommend that the MOA entered into between the banks and the LGU for the collection arrangement should not be renewed.
observations.managementAction: Management to refrain deducting other than the mandatory contributions from the payroll.
observations.reason: Deductions still made for loans to private lenders in payroll with remittances made monthly

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the completed projects to the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
   
We also recommend that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant once constructed projects are completed, and furnish the latter with the documents for proper reclassification to the appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: Accountant and Engineer to facilitate count and inspection of listed projects to correct records.

observations.reason: The Management committee has conducted the physical count of all properties and submitted the RPCPPE, however, the same were not reconciled with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to henceforth perform loan aging and analysis and maintain a separate record thereof, aside from the output of the accounting system, to accurately identify and classify the Loans Payable account in accordance with the specific provisions in PPSAS and PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant immediatel y complied the recommenda tion given by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: 2. In coordination with the Offices of the Municipal Accountant, the General Services and the Rural Health Unit, to establish a procedural mechanism from the receipt of the delivery of the transferred assets from the Department of Health and Provincial Health Unit up to the recording of these assets in the LGU’s books; 3. Require the RHU Administrator to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with the Inventory Report of the Drugs, Medicines and Medical Supplies as at December 31, 2018 to facilitate the recording of the inventories on hand as of year-end; and 4. For items delivered by PHO, instruct the Accountant to coordinate with the PHO, PGSO and/or PAO to indicate in their deliveries the unit price of inventory items to facilitate recording the books.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials committed to adhere to the provisions and to follow the recommenda tion of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to file appropriate request for authority to write-off dormant account pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2017-010. 6. We also recommended to the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of all fund transfers including those granted by other LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is committed to strictly comply with the COA Memorandum.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Development Council to henceforth, specify the PPAs funded out of the 20% Development Fund, be it in Annual/Supple mental Investment Program, to maintain transparency and accountabilit y of the Municipality in its development undertakings.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already directed MPDC as the Secretaria t to specify the PPAs under 20% LGDF.

observations.recommendation: 8. Instruct the LDRRM Officer to submit the CY 2018 LDRRMFIP using the prescribed format, Annex A, of COA Circular No. 2012-002, to facilitate post-audit of transactions for LDRRMF for the calendar year within (ten) days upon receipt; 9. Direct the LDRRM Officer to ensure that for CY 2019 the LDRRMFIP used by the Municipality is in accordance with the format prescribed in the said Circular; and 10. Require the LDRRM Officer to attend trainings, briefing and/or orientation with offices such as DILG, to further enhance his understanding of LDRRM, particularly on the preparation of proper LDRRMFIP, and familiarity of some forms.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is committed to the provisions. (no further detail)

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to prioritize the creation of two mandatory positions in addition to the other positions already existing in the LDRRM Office to comply with the pertinent provisions in NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to comply with the recommenda tions.
observations.reason: Waiting for reorganizati on on CY 2020

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Health Officer to maximize the use of the funds intended for the implementatio n of the MLGP and the Filariasis program within the bounds of the guidelines provided in DOH RO XI Order No. 2014-0017-D and DOH RO XI Order No. 2018-0047. 13. Proper monitoring of the balances of the fund at the end of each period is enjoined to ensure optimal utilization of the funds for its purpose. Should the Filariasis fund cannot be utilized for its purpose, it is recommended to return such fund to DOH Region XI.
observations.managementAction: The Management already agreed to comply the recommenda tions.
observations.reason: The implementati on was interrupted due to the series of earthquakes. MHO personnel are busy responding to the health needs on different evacuation centers.

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the return of the unexpended balances of completed projects to the Source Agencies and/or request the transfer to the General Fund to be available for use and henceforth to strictly adhere to the provisions in COA Circular 94-013 and/or COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management already returned the unexpended amounts to NGAs.

observations.recommendation: 15. Efficient handling of segregated wastes confined in the MRF/RCA located at Brgy. Dalumay should be improved to ensure that wastes are placed in a sack/containe rs and piled in one zone of the facility to maximize the storage area; 16. Develop a comprehensive program/plan on the use of the crusher and shedders to avoid downtime on solid waste management operation whenever repairs are undertaken; 17. Provide labels on the MRF receptacles/com partments for proper waste identificatio n.
observations.managementAction: Segregated wastes were already placed in sack and piled in the designated zone of the facility. Measures were already undertaken re: cost of SLF level I and signed MOA of Winder Recycling Company at Davao City who shall receive plastic wastes within the landfill/MRF of Magsaysay, Davao del Sur to process and recycle aforesaid wastes. Printed tarpaulin to secure as label in the respective zones.

observations.recommendation: 18. Direct the Market Operations- Designate to cause the renewal of the lease contracts of all existing occupants and have them duly notarized and ensure that requirements for such renewal are complied with; 19. Direct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Market Operations- Designate to check the completeness and relevance of the provisions stipulated in the Contract of Lease and adopt changes when necessary; and 20. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Market Operations- Designate to effect sound management and internal control policies in the market.
observations.managementAction: The Management fully implemente d all recommenda tions.

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the MSWDO to resubmit the GAD Accomplishm ent Report for CY 2018 complete with all the needed information as required in Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 to facilitate thorough evaluation of the fund. And henceforth, strictly adhere to the deadlines set for the submission of GAD reports as specified in item V of COA Circular 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person already submitted the Accomplish ment Report for GAD with complete needed informat ion as required in the Memorandum Circular.

observations.recommendation: 22. We reiterated our recommendatio n to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the MSWDO to facilitate the allocation of at least 1% of its IRA solely for the strengthening and implementat ion of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC in compliance with the provisions mentioned in the RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012, in order that prevention, rehabilitatio n and reintegration of children at risk and/or in conflict with the law are ensured and given utmost importance.
observations.managementAction: Already provided on CY 2019 budget.

observations.recommendation: 23. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer to embark on measures to speed-up the implementatio n of the remaining portion of the project and in coordination with the contractor, maximize the available human and machine resources to finally complete the project.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied to the recommenda tions.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following: Henceforth, the programs/projects/ activities to be funded out of the 20% Local Government Developmen t Fund for the succeding years’ AIP shall be specific in order to enhance the transparency and accountabilit y in the municipality’ s implementatio n/undertaking of the local developmen t projects, programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Continuous coordinati on with concerned program coordinato rs.

observations.recommendation: 25. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council, to initiate the preparation of a separate Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan for the calendar year 2018 and onwards to give a clear direction to the council in implementing the Municipali ty’s programs, projects and activities related to local disaster risk reduction management.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied to the recommenda tions.

observations.recommendation: 26. We recommended to the Municipality to allocate at least 1% of its IRA solely for the strengthening and implementat ion of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC in compliance with the provisions mentioned in the RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012, in order that prevention, rehabilitatio n and reintegration of children at risk and/or in conflict with the law are ensured and given utmost importance.
observations.managementAction: Already included on CY 2020 budget. Coordinate with all concerned departments/ Local Finance Committee to properly implement the program. Conducted meeting among members of the Local Finance Committee to comply with RA 9344.

observations.recommendation: 27. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council, to initiate the preparation of the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan for the calendar year 2017 and onwards to give a clear direction to the council in implementing PPAs related to local disaster risk reduction management.
observations.managementAction: Committed to comply every year.

observations.recommendation: 28. Direct the personnel concerned to process the transfer of ownership of various sites from the sellers to the Municipality. 29. Direct the personnel concerned to cause the return of TCTs that are with the GSIS.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents to transfer the property. Partially Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management, through the:

General Services Officer        (i) include the actual/acquisition cost of all parcels of land in the RPCPPE to provide basis for disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; and (ii) initiate the disposal procedures by preparing the IIRUP for the eventual disposal of the unserviceable properties and dropping its amount from the books of accounts in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445; 

Municipal Accountant provide adequate disclosure in the Notes of Financial Statements pending disposal of unserviceable PPE totaling P13,503,830.69; and

Concerned accountable officer submit to the Auditor the required documents for the request for relief from property accountability pursuant to Section 378 of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: Continued disregard of the concerned Municipal Officials, hence, this finding is reiterated in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant: 

conduct a thorough review of long outstanding accounts payable to (i) facilitate proper reconciliation of reciprocal accounts/transactions to properly identify the same; and (ii) revert to the general fund all dormant and long outstanding payables/balances without valid claimants; and

ensure that no obligation is booked up unless the same is founded on a valid claim that is supported by sufficient evidence and documentation.
observations.reason:
Records pertaining to dormant Accounts Payable which are the reciprocal amount of long outstanding cash advances were lost due to fire that razed the Provincial Auditor’s Office in February 2010 hence, reversion thereof could not be facilitated. 

Meanwhile, no action on the part of the concerned Municipal Officials regarding revision of undocumented obligation pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, (a) exert more efforts in demanding immediate settlement of the unliquidated cash advances including those granted to officers and employees that are no longer in the government service;         (b) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted unless the previous one has been liquidated and duly accounted for; and       (c) adhere  strictly with the provisions of Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 regarding the full settlement and liquidation of cash advances at the end of each year.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) consider requiring the Municipal Treasurer to suspend and withhold the payment of salaries or any amount due the concerned accountable officers and employees who are still in service, considering the lapse of time that the aforementioned cash advances have been outstanding; and (b) institute or file appropriate administrative charges against erring officers and employees pursuant to Section 9.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-004, if warranted.
observations.reason: The account of Ms. Itumay could not be established since documents pertaining thereto were lost due to fire that razed the Provincial Auditor’s Office in February 2010. Similar deficiencies were noted on the grant and liquidation of cash advances which was discussed in the part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the          (a) Municipal Accountant          (i) analyze and break down the amount pertaining to the previous and current Municipal Treasurers to properly establish their accountabilities and transfer the amount pertaining to the previous Municipal Treasurers from Cash in Vault account to Other Receivable account for its fair presentation in the financial statements; and (ii) keep the accounts in such detail as is necessary by maintaining subsidiary records pursuant to Sections 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445; and (b) Municipal Treasurer reconcile the cashbooks with accounting records at least once a month and immediately examine any discrepancy to forestall accumulation of reconciling items. 

Also, after proper reconciliation, we strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor exert efforts to enforce its collection from the concerned Municipal Treasurers.
observations.reason: Documents needed for reconciliation were lost due to fire that razed the Provincial Auditor’s Office in February 2010.
Continued disregard of the concerned Municipal Officials, hence, this finding is reiterated in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, thru the GAD Focal Person, (a) synchronize the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget with the planning and budgeting calendar to enable its timely submission to the PPDO to check its alignment with the priorities of the Province and then to the DILG Regional Office for final review and approval;         (b) develop GAD PPAs that would address gender issues in the Municipality to strengthen its gender-responsive governance; and (c) ensure that GAD Fund is fully utilized in compliance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: Insufficient knowledge of the GAD Focal Point System on the timeline provided in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 hence, this finding is reiterated in Observation No. 10, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 31-34 | We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to require the Inventory Committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE to determine the actual existence and proper valuation of the same, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1. The Inventory Committee may coordinate and request the assistance of the department heads to conduct inventory of all properties under their respective offices and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should create, update and maintain the required property cards and ledger cards. Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results of the actual count with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled immediately.

Furthermore, we reiterated that the physical count of PPE should be undertaken annually by the Inventory Committee and the corresponding RPCPPE be submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year as required under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.reason: Still no Report on Physical Count of PPE submitted in CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 35-37 | We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make extra effort to reconstruct/trace back the composition of Infrastructure Assets and prepare the corresponding GLs and SLs.

We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.
observations.reason: Still no Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network submitted in CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 37-42 | We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to compel the Municipal Accounting Office to establish the breakdown and component of every major real account reflected in the submitted Statement of Financial Position as of December 31, 2017 and for this purpose, maintain subsidiary ledgers. Likewise, the General Ledger for CY 2017 should be reconstructed to prove the ending balances of the accounts. The schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger as of December 31, 2017 should be submitted immediately to the Audit Team as supporting schedule for the financial statements submitted.
