We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to review and be guided by the NGAS Manual for LGUs and other related COA issuances for the procedures and appropriate accounting formats to be used in recording the financial transactions of the Municipality. Likewise, the JEV should be duly approved before recording to the appropriate journals or books of original entry. 

In addition, we recommended for the Municipal Accountant to trace/review the accounts with negative balances and make the necessary adjusting/correcting entries with supporting documents.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 42-44 | We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to instruct/remind the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their respective books of accounts. Any discrepancy noted should be investigated in accordance with existing regulations and take appropriate action thereon.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 44-45 | We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements (all funds) for CY 2017 and previous years including the duly certified copies of bank statements. Henceforth, Bank Reconciliation Statements should be submitted within ten (10) days after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 45-48 | We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445. Likewise, require the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances, else suspension of salaries should be imposed until respective outstanding cash advances have been fully settled and liquidated.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain the GLs and SLs and properly present an accurate and reliable Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances (RUCA). 

Henceforth, we recommended for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 48-50 | We reiterated our recommendation that the Office of the Municipal Accountant periodically reconcile the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds, Due to Other Funds and other reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary adjustments should be effected in the books of accounts.
 
Likewise, we strongly recommended that Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) and General Ledgers (GLs) should be maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018; exactly the same amount of discrepancy between the two accounts still exist as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 59-61 | We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. We also recommend the immediate submission of the copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents which are due on the prescribed period to the Office of the Auditor.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 78-81 | We recommended the Municipality to faithfully comply with the preparation and submission of the documents as prescribed under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the said Joint Circular.

We also recommended that the disbursements to be made out of SEF must be within the budget and in accordance with Section 100(c) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 4 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. 

Lastly, we recommended the Municipality, through its Municipal Treasurer, to exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real property for the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: The same deficiency exist in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 84-85 | We reiterated our recommendation the management to initiate the immediate establishment of Municipal Solid Waste Management Facility. Henceforth, we recommended strict implementation of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 or Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 85-87 | We recommended that the Management should prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. In addition, we also recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF. 
Likewise, the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of ₱1,155,109.25 for CY 2017 should be transferred to the Trust Fund and be deposited to the respective bank account. 

We further recommended that the guidelines provided in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 for the allocation, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF be strictly observed.
observations.reason: The same deficiency occurred in CY 2018 and was reiterated in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 87-89 | We recommended for the Management to strictly comply with Section 48 of the General Provisions of R.A. No. 10964 known as the “General Appropriations Act of FY 2018” for the monthly net take home pay of the regular employees which is not lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00), after all authorized deductions based on the above order of preference presented.
observations.reason: The same deficiency still exist in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to -

Direct all Revenue Collecting Officers to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and to remit their collections on a daily basis to the Liquidating Officer for her to deposit collections daily to the depository bank or as soon as the collections reach P10,000.00.

Instruct the Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of collector and check the same against new balances on hand column.  Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I. 

Instruct the Accountable Officers to stop using the collections for payment of any expenses as this will be covered appropriately by issuance of checks or through the agency’s established petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction: Collections were still reported and deposited at a later date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to -

Establish and maintain the Petty Cash Fund under the imprest system. 

Establish PCF equivalent to an amount sufficient to cover only expenses that are difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by check, pursuant to COA-Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  

The Annual Procurement Plan (APP) should include requirements for common-use office supplies to avoid purchases of the same through cash advances. 

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission of official receipts and other supporting documents to support the liquidation of cash advances/PCF as proof of payments and refrain from using Reimbursement Expenses Receipts (RERs).

Maintain a PCF cashbook and record therein transactions daily.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash disbursements were made thru a reimbursement basis.
No inventory committee created to conduct the physical inventory taking although the Local Chief Executive has already designated a General Supply Officer.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management closely monitor the receivables and intensify its collection, otherwise, file the appropriate request for authority to write-off for the dormant accounts duly supported with the relevant documents pursuant to Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand liquidation from LGUs.
observations.reason: Changes in barangay personnel and difficulty in obtaining records on the barangay level.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management strictly require the recipient NGOs/POs to submit the duly certified final Utilization Report to support its liquidation pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001. Issue demand letter to the NGOs/POs. If no settlement is made, institute legal sanctions pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations applicable for violation of the provisions of the above COA Circular. 
  
We also recommend to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers releasing fund transfers to the same NGOs/POs unless the previous ones are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: Accountant to demand liquidation from NGOs/PO.  Pursue writeoff of long outstanding accounts.
observations.reason: Difficulty in serving demand letters to NGOs/POs involved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the completed projects to the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
  
We also recommend that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant once constructed projects are completed, and furnish the latter with the documents for proper reclassification to the appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: Accountant and Engineer to facilitate count and inspection of listed projects to correct records.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to conduct physical count and reconciliation.  Further, difficulty to determine prior year portion of the account.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to insure all new buildings including its equipment and other attachments thereon pursuant to the provisions of Section5 of R.A. 656 and Section489 of GAAM, Volume I.

Provide an appropriation for the insurance premium for the insurance of all its properties having insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).
observations.managementAction: For budget deliberation
observations.reason: Not yet implemented in CY 2018 as there were no funds appropriated during the year. But the Management disclosed that the properties will be insured in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to allocate at least 1% of its IRA solely for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC in compliance with the provisions mentioned in the RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012, in order that prevention, rehabilitation and reintegration of children at risk and/or in conflict with the law are ensured and given utmost importance.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with all concerned departments/ Local Finance Committee to properly implement the program. Conducted meeting among members of the Local Finance Committee to comply with RA 9344.
observations.reason: Insufficien t funds priority programs, projects and activities that need immediate resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Council, to initiate the preparation of the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2017 and onwards to give a clear direction to the council in implementing PPAs related to local disaster risk management.
observations.managementAction: Committed to prepare a detailed LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2018 and onwards.
observations.reason: Full implemen-ta tion will be on CY 2019 transaction s. Reiterated in this year’s audit findings.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) coordinate with the Punong Barangay of Barangay Caguray to identify the rightful owner of the Land costing P300,000.00. If it is proven that it will remain in the possession of the Municipality, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the actual acquisition cost of the land and fast track the transfer of the certificate of title under the name of the Municipality to avoid any third party claims and to recognized expense on the inventory items amounting to P262,550.00 that were already distributed to various Barangays; and (ii) require the concerned officials to locate the service vehicles amounting to P250,000.00 which was not found during the ocular inspection.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to provide full disclosure on the valuation of land acquired through non-exchanged transactions and other important information in the Notes to Financial Statement as required by PPSAS 17. Prepare the necessary adjustments on the error made in the recording of service vehicle amounting to P720,000.00 for fair and accurate presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position;
Inventory Committee to ensure that the RPCPPE are based on the results of actual physical count so that any unrecorded PPE found on location and/or losses discovered during the physical count could be immediately investigated and adjusted in the books of accounts; and
ICO Municipal Treasurer to prepare the IIRUP, together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items. While waiting for the proper disposal of PPE, the concerned officials may start the test for impairment of PPE totaling P23,141,507.95 for the proper valuation in the Statement of Financial Position. If the carrying amount exceeded the recoverable service amount, the Municipal Accountant shall recognize an impairment loss in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer all funds received from NGAs from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books and all disbursements and transactions pertaining thereto shall be duly recorded therein for proper accounting and monitoring of project implementation and reporting to the respective source agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) initiate the preparation and submission of the request for write-off, together with the required documents, for the unliquidated cash advances of the deceased Municipal Mayor totaling P1,539,241.54 to the Commission on Audit for proper evaluation and disposition pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 for fair presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements; (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of salaries and other emoluments due to Ms. Elizabeth Itumay to enforce the liquidation/settlement of her long outstanding cash accountabilities; and (c) henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: (i) issue demand letters requiring the immediate settlement of the remaining outstanding cash advances especially those who are no longer connected with the agency but are still alive; (ii) refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance given is first settled and proper accounting thereof is made in accordance with Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular N. 97-002; (iii) require full settlement of cash advances upon submission of liquidation reports. Any excess from the amount granted should be simultaneously refunded; and (iv) make the necessary adjustments on the reclassification of the Due from Officers and Employees account totaling P2,545,583.04 to a proper Receivable account depending on the nature of the cash advance for fair and accurate presentation of accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide sufficient appropriation for the completion of the Municipality’s 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan so that the programs/projects/activities embodied therein can be implemented accordingly pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Local Sanggunian, enact an ordinance that will restrict/halt the practice of open burning of wastes in compliance with Section 48 of RA No. 9003 to avoid various health problems acquired through inhalation of fine particles produced during incineration and to avoid long-term adverse effects on the environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board to observe the timely preparation, submission and approval of the Annual SEF Budget as prescribed by the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017. We also recommended that the LSB ensure that PPAs incorporated in the SEF Budget are only those expenses allowed to be charged against the fund as enumerated in the same Joint Circular to eliminate expenditures that are not among the primary purpose for which the fund was established.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR RA No. 9184 pursuant to Item 6.3 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. Requisitions for procurements with the same purpose and needed at or about the same time be consolidated into a single requisition and undergo competitive bidding, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Development Council, ensure that only expenditures/projects that directly contribute to capital development be included in the local development plan and refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures in accordance with Items 3 and 4 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that funds are utilized for the specific purpose for which they have been appropriated. In the event that appropriation for PPAs in the budget is determined to be deficient, augmentation thereof may be made within the same class of their respective appropriation pursuant to Section 336 of RA No. 7160. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
LDRRM Officer to utilize the LDRRM Fund solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities such as early warning device, search, rescue and retrieval equipment and other useful equipage for disaster preparedness and disaster response pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 10121;
Municipal Accountant to: (i) transfer the CY 2012 unutilized LDRRM Fund which remained unutilized for more than five years in the Trust Fund to General Fund in compliance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121; (ii) transfer the CY 2018 unexpended appropriation for Capital Outlay which was erroneously transferred to the Trust Fund to the General Fund and henceforth ensure that unappropriated balances for CO shall remain in the General Fund until the project funded therefore is completed; (iii) refrain from recording purchases of goods and supplies as outright expense instead such shall be accounted under the inventory accounts which shall be properly monitored; and (iv) coordinate with the LDRRM Office for the proper inventory and reconciliation of the relief goods on hand as of December 31, 2018 and prepare the necessary adjustments for the correct presentation of the related inventory and expense accounts; and
BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects; and
We also recommended that the Management ensure that only the information required by COA Circular No. 2013-004 is displayed on signages, tarpaulins and other printed materials to avoid unnecessary expenditures that are disallowable in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System, strictly observe the prescribed timelines for GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely preparation and submission to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and the subsequent review and endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to prepare the GL and SLs for CYs 2017 and 2018 in accordance with Sections 9 and 10 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.
The audit observation and recommendations were well taken by the Management.
The Municipal Accountant failed to prepare the (a) Consolidated Financial Statements for all funds, (b) Notes to Financial Statements, (c) Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts for General Fund and Special Education Fund, and (d) Statement of Cash Flows for the Trust Fund, contrary to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 and Section 76 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume I, thus limiting the scope of our audit.
Section 76 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I provides:
Year-end Financial Statements. – Local accountants shall prepare at the end of the year the following financial statements for each fund:
Balance Sheet;
Statement of Income and Expenses; and
Statement of Cash Flows.
The Chief Accountant shall likewise prepare the consolidated financial statements for all funds and the Notes to the Financial Statements. (Underscoring ours)
With the issuance of the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs in connection with the adoption of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS), the presentation of Financial Statements has been updated to include the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts, as prescribed under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016. The updated list of Financial Statements is as follows:
Statement of Financial Position
Statement of Financial Performance
Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity
Cash Flow Statement
Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual Amounts
Notes to Financial Statements
Relative thereto, Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 requires the Accountants to submit to the auditor the year-end financial statements and schedules on or before February 14 of each year. However, records of this Office showed that the Municipal Accountant has not yet submitted the following financial reports for CY 2018 as of this date:
Consolidated Financial Statements for all funds;
Notes to Financial Statements;
Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts for General Fund (GF) and Special Education Fund (SEF); and
Statement of Cash Flows for the Trust Fund (TF).
A summary of the financial statements submitted and not submitted by the Municipal Accountant for CY 2018 is provided on the following table.
The Accountant submitted a Statement of Cash Flows for TF but only for the Quarter ended March 31, 2018.
Inquiry with the Municipal Accountant revealed that she was not able to prepare the complete set of financial reports due to voluminous work load. According to her, the preparation of reports for the first quarter of CY 2019 had become a priority and she no longer had time to complete the CY 2018 financial statements.
The incomplete set of financial reports has limited the scope of our audit. We were not able to conduct a review on the cash flows and the comparison of budget and actual amounts of revenue and expenditure items due to lack of the corresponding Financial Statements therefor. Furthermore, lack of the Notes to Financial Statements resulted in non-disclosure of important information that are relevant in understanding the Statements of Financial Position, Financial Performance, Changes in Equity and Cash Flows. Pursuant to Section 80 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I, the Notes to Financial Statements are supposed to provide information on the following:
Summary of significant accounting policies adopted and followed by the reporting entity;
Narrative descriptions or detailed analyses of amounts shown on the face of the Statements of Financial Position, Financial Performance and Cash Flows;
Customary or routine disclosure – which are information about measurement bases of important assets, restrictions on assets, contingent assets/liabilities, important long term commitments not recognized in the body of the statements, etc.;
Disclosures of changes in accounting principles - changes in accounting principles, practices or methods of applying them; and
Disclosures of subsequent events – disclosure of events that affect the agency directly and that occur between the date of, or end of the period covered by, the financial statements and date of completion of the statements are necessary; if knowledge of the events might affect the interpretation of the statements, even though the events do not affect the propriety of the financial statements themselves.
Attention is invited to Sections 122 and 127 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 which provide:
Section 122. Submission of Reports – Whenever deemed necessary in the exigencies of the service, the Commission may under regulations issued by it require the agency heads, chief accountants, budget officers, cashiers, disbursing officers, administrative or personnel officers, and other responsible officials of the various agencies to submit trial balances, physical inventory reports, current plantilla of personnel, and such other reports as may be necessary for the exercise of its functions.
(2) Failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.
Section 127. Administrative disciplinary action. Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the President (Prime Minister), any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against the said public officer who, upon being found guilty thereof after hearing, shall be meted out such penalty as is commensurate with the degree of his guilt in accordance with the Civil Service Law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements imposed in this Code shall be conclusive proof that the public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete set of Financial Statements for CY 2018, including the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to Section 76 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004. Otherwise, cause the suspension of her salaries until such reports have been submitted to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
The audit observation and recommendation were well taken by the Management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2018 and submit the same along with duly certified copies of bank statements, paid checks and debit/credit memos to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
The Municipal Accountant said that she has started working on the preparation of BRS in CY 2018 but failed to keep them updated.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the general and subsidiary ledgers for all accounts pertaining to cash advances and submit a summary schedule of subsidiary ledger balances as of the end of each quarter for CY 2018;
impose strictly the provision requiring all accountable officers to fully and promptly liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served or at the end of each year, whichever comes first; and
cause the issuance of demand letters to all accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018.
The Management agreed to take action on the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, cause the immediate issuance of a demand letter to the Provincial Government, requiring the return of unexpended fund transfers.
The Municipal Mayor said that they have already communicated with the Provincial Government of Palawan to resolve this matter.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor, through the Inventory Committee, require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; and
ICO-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant prepare the Property Cards, and RPLC and PPELC, respectively, per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
The audit observation and recommendations were well taken by the Management. According to the Municipal Mayor, the Department Heads have already started the physical count of PPE in their offices.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of the documents and reports pertaining to revenue-generating projects for CY 2018.
The Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the said documents and reports.

We recommended that the (a) Local School Board submit a copy of the SEF Budget for CY 2018; and (b) Municipal Accountant submit a revised SEF Utilization Report for CY 2018 with supporting computations for each expenditure item and henceforth, ensure the correctness and timely submission of documents and reports pertaining to SEF.
The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson cause the immediate submission of the contracts and purchase orders for CY 2018 along with the complete supporting documents and henceforth, ensure the timely and regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
The BAC Chairperson agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the:
MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports on the same with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments; and
Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects to the LCE and the Audit Team for monitoring purposes.
The Municipal Mayor explained that one of the factors which caused the low physical accomplishement rate for CY 2018 was the insufficient manpower, considering that the projects were implemented by administration due to lack of interested bidders on straight-contracts. The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation in CY 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
through the MSWMB and in coordination with the PCSD, cause the safe closure of the open dumpsite and the creation of a solid waste disposal facility that is compliant to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; and
through the MPDC, intensify the monitoring on the implementation of the solid waste management program.
The Municipal Mayor said that they already have ongoing projects for CY 2019 in connection with the solid waste management program. The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
The Management agreed to submit the said investment plan.

We recommended that the ICO - Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,092,611.26 to the respective Trust Fund bank account and make the necessary journal entries in the books.
The ICO - Municipal Treasurer mentioned that the unexpended balance of LDRRMF was transferred to the Trust Fund in March 2019.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant cause the immediate recording of the receivable relative to audit disallowance under ND No. 12-001-101-(11) on the basis of the corresponding NFD dated December 27, 2012 in accordance with Section 60 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section 22.6 of the RRSA; and
Municipal Treasurer enforce settlement of the disallowance through withholding of salaries of persons liable in accordance with the instructions under the COE issued on May 26, 2014, otherwise she shall be held liable along with the Local Chief Executive.
The Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare a subsidiary ledger for each audit disallowance or charge that has become final and executory based on the NFD issued and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team; and
submit a detailed report on the monthly installment payments for settlement of ND No. 14-001-100-13 for CY 2018 using the format prescribed, complete with references to specific documents, and update the same every month thereafter.
The Municipal Accountant informed that she already started preparing the report.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the unremitted balance of the Due to PhilHealth account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, if any. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed under PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s. 2014 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.
The Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer both agreed to take action on the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Executive Committee and the Municipal Accountant (a) cause the immediate submission of the GPB, GAD AR and other GAD reports and documents requested by the Audit Team for CY 2018; and (b) henceforth, ensure timely submission of GAD related reports and documents in accordance with Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.
The Management agreed to submit GAD related reports and documents. According to the Municipal Budget Officer, they have prepared the GPB and GAD AR for CY 2018 but failed to provide a copy to the Audit Team due to miscommunication within the Management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (1) direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2017 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and (2) henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.
The Management agreed to take action on the audit recommendation.

We have reiterated our recommendations and management agreed to-
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement particularly for the eleven bank accounts immediately and to submit a revise list of outstanding checks with complete details;
Direct the Local Chief Executive to augment the work force assigned in the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of reports.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all paid disbursement vouchers are included in the Report of Checks Issued and forwarded to the Accounting Department for the timely and proper recording in the books.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to enroll the bank accounts to online/ i-access to be able to keep track of its balances at any given time.

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the purpose of cash advances are on the face of the disbursement vouchers and that are duly supported with complete documents at the time of grant of cash advance including his/her certification that the previous cash advance of the employee/officer has been liquidated and accounted for in the books.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare demand letter for each employee requiring them to liquidate or refund unused cash advances and cause the withholding of salaries for those who failed to liquidate within the time frame, if warranted;
Advise the Local Chief Executive to impose a “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy and to impose administrative sanctions against accountable officers/employees who continue to neglect their obligations in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular 2012-001.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments for suppliers are made thru checks and consider establishing a petty cash fund to address petty expenditures of the LGU.

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Direct the GSO to initiate the immediate disposal proceedings by preparing Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) showing the itemized list and complete description of the assets and Appraisal Report showing the appraised value of the assets.
Require the GSO to transfer the four unserviceable motor vehicles in one designated area pending the disposal of the items.

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit immediately disbursement vouchers together with its supporting documents and ensure to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to approve and process only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete and valid documentations and approvals.
Advise the Local Chief Executive to augment the working force under the Accounting Office with great consideration on the competency and qualification

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure strict compliance with the transfer of all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund books.
Also, require the Municipal Accountant with the assistance of the MDRRMO to prepare necessary adjusting entries in order to reclassify in the Disaster and Rescue Equipment account all those procured items or equipment relative to calamity fund.

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Require the BAC Secretariat to submit to COA the approved contracts together with its supporting papers for the said perfected projects for COA’s review immediately and to submit prospective contracts within 5 days upon its perfection in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.
Advise management headed by the Local Chief Executive to revisit the internal control policies over LGU procurement operations to identify and address these recurring lapses over submission of contracts.

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the above-mentioned lacking documents and to ensure that prospective payments are supported with complete documents to establish the propriety of the transactions.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a checklist for each type of transaction in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Cashier and Revenue Collectors to –
Maintain a cashbook for each accountable form per fund and for each type of cash advances per fund;
Record daily their transactions in the cashbook in ink and in the order they occur;
Balance, rule and close at the end of the day their cashbook and foot and close the books at the end of each month; and
Reconcile their cashbook with the accounting records regularly or at least once in every quarter to establish the correct balance of accountability of the AO at the earliest time possible. Any account found unrecorded can be easily traced and the necessary corrections/recording can be effected immediately.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to create a subsidiary ledger for each revenue collecting officer.

We have recommended and management agreed to direct all Revenue Collectors to prepare RAAF while the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF and submit to COA the said monthly reports in the prescribed form provided in the NGAS Manual not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2017 and to strictly comply with the submission of AAPSI in the ensuing annual audit reports.

We have recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the report and that the LDRRMO to submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to COA Auditor and to furnish the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) and the Department of the Interior and Local Government-Local Government Operations Office (DILG-LGOO).

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Direct the Local Chief Executive/Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board to initiate a written representation to follow up the approval of the agency’s Solid Waste Management Plan.
Direct management to adopt measures for the implementation of segregation of waste under all its jurisdiction.
Direct the Local Chief Executive to initiate the immediate implementation of the construction of MRF pursuant to Section 33 of RA 9003 in order to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage.

We have recommended and management agreed to direct the LCE to create a Municipal Council for Protection of Children (MCPC) and to ensure that programs, projects and activities are responsive to the needs of the children and pursuant to those identified in the Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC).

We have recommended and management agreed to-
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to ensure the timely completion of the programs, projects and activities.
Advise the Municipal Engineer to consider extending assistance to the barangays in preparing program of works.
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that funds for 20% development funds are transferred without delay to its bank account on a monthly basis.

We recommended that management instruct the Accountant to recognize and transfer the Local Road Networks from the Public Infrastructure Registry to the books of accounts.  Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to revisit the map of the municipality’s local road networks and determine the proper cost for each local road through thorough review of records.

We recommended that management instruct both offices to maintain a complete and updated property ledger cards and stock cards for properties owned and conduct the immediate reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the proper valuation of the PPE accounts.  The differences per physical count and book balances must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.
We recommended that management notify the Office of the Auditor of any deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours through submission of the aforementioned report in accordance with the said Circular.

We recommended that management:
Formulate a disaster recovery plan to secure the agency’s data and information systems.
Set policies in securing data which includes determining the frequency in which data and records are backed-up and the location to which these back-ups are stored.

We recommended that Management submit the contracts including all the necessary supporting documents for review and evaluation.  Implement strictly the provisions of Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. With strict adherence, immediate corrective measures can be done for all deficiencies noted in the course review, evaluation, and inspection.

We recommended that management instruct the concerned contractor to rectify the defects noted by the COA Technical Audit Specialist.  Inform the Audit Team Leader if corrections were already made for further evaluation.

We recommended that management establish close coordination with BFAR and fisherfolks to ensure success and continuity of the project and to ensure through close monitoring that the CFLC operates in accordance with the MOA and CFLC guidelines.  Require the contractor to deliver the proper items as stated in the purchase order.
We recommended that management require the concerned departments and personnel to fast track the implementation of the project to avoid incurring additional cost due to delay.

We recommended that management instruct the Accountant prepare and submit the required monthly and annual statements of the auditor’s office and ensure that requirements set by the Financial Management Operations Guidelines for the LGU are followed.

We recommended that management require the corresponding elected officials to secure from the Philippines Councilor’s League, Incorporated, a government issued official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to henceforth perform loan
aging and analysis and maintain a separate record thereof,  aside  from  the
output of the accounting system, to accurately  identify  and  classify  the
Loans Payable account in accordance with the specific  provisions  in  PPSAS
and PD 1445.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:


   1. In coordination with the Offices  of  the  Municipal  Accountant,  the
      General Services and the Rural Health Unit, to establish a  procedural
      mechanism from the receipt of the delivery of the  transferred  assets
      from the Department of Health and the Provincial Health Unit up to the
      recording of these assets in the LGU’s books;


   2. Require the RHU Administrator  to  furnish  the  Municipal  Accounting
      Office with the Inventory Report of the Drugs, Medicines  and  Medical
      Supplies as at December 31, 2018 to facilitate the  recording  of  the
      inventories on hand as of year-end; and


   3. For items delivered by PHO, instruct the Accountant to coordinate with
      the   PHO, PGSO and/or PAO to indicate in their  deliveries  the  unit
      price of inventory items to facilitate recording the books.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal  Accountant
to file appropriate request  for  authority  to  write-off  dormant  account
pursuant to COA  Memorandum  No.  2017-010  dated  May  15,  2017.  We  also
recommended to the Municipal Accountant  to  conduct  regular  and  periodic
verification,  analysis  and  validation  of  the  existence  of  all   fund
transfers including those granted by other LGUs.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Development
Council to henceforth, specify the PPAs funded out of  the  20%  Development
Fund,  be  it  in  Annual/Supplemental  Investment  Program,   to   maintain
transparency and accountability  of  the  Municipality  in  its  development
undertakings.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

     1. Instruct the LDRRM Officer to submit the CY 2018 LDRRMFIP using the
        prescribed format, Annex  A,  of  COA  Circular  No.  2012-002,  to
        facilitate post-audit of transactions for LDRRMF for  the  calendar
        year within (ten) days upon receipt;


     2. Direct the LDRRM Officer to ensure that for CY  2019  the  LDRRMFIP
        used  by  the  Municipality  is  in  accordance  with  the   format
        prescribed in the said Circular; and


     3. Require the LDRRM Officer  to  attend  trainings,  briefing  and/or
        orientation with offices such  as  DILG,  to  further  enhance  his
        understanding of LDRRM, particularly on the preparation  of  proper
        LDRRMFIP, and familiarity of some forms.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to prioritize the creation  of  two
mandatory positions in addition to the other positions already  existing  in
the LDRRM Office to comply with the pertinent provisions  in  NDRRMC,  DILG,
DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the  Municipal  Health
Officer to maximize the use of the funds intended for the implementation  of
the MLGP  and  Filariasis  program  within  the  bounds  of  the  guidelines
provided in DOH RO XI Order No. 2014-0017-D and DOH RO XI  Order  No.  2018-
0047.

      Proper monitoring of the balances of the  fund  at  the  end  of  each
period is enjoined to ensure  optimal  utilization  of  the  funds  for  its
purpose. Should the Filariasis fund cannot be utilized for its  purpose,  it
is recommended to return such fund to DOH Region XI.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal  Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the return of the  unexpended  balances
of completed projects to the Source Agencies and/or request the transfer  to
the General Fund to be available for use and henceforth to  strictly  adhere
to the provisions in COA Circular 94-013 and/or COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to facilitate, through  the  MENRO,
the following:

     1. Efficient handling of the segregated wastes confined in the MRF/RCA
        located at Brgy. Dalumay should be improved to ensure  that  wastes
        are placed in a sack/containers  and  piled  in  one  zone  of  the
        facility to maximize the storage area;



     2. Develop a  comprehensive program/plan on the use of the crusher and
        shedders to avoid downtime  on  solid  waste  management  operation
        whenever repairs are undertaken; and



     3. Provide labels on the MRF receptacles/compartments for proper waste
        identification.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

     1. Direct the Market Operations- Designate to cause the renewal of the
        lease contracts of  all  existing  occupants  and  have  them  duly
        notarized  and  ensure  that  requirements  for  such  renewal  are
        complied with;


     2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer  in  coordination  with  the  Market
        Operations- Designate to check the completeness  and  relevance  of
        the provisions stipulated  in  the  Contract  of  Lease  and  adopt
        changes when necessary; and


     3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Market Operations- Designate
        to effect sound management and internal  control  policies  in  the
        market.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the MSWDO to resubmit the
GAD  Accomplishment  Report  for  CY  2018  complete  with  all  the  needed
information as required in Annex  E  of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  JMC 2013-01  to
facilitate thorough evaluation of the fund. And henceforth, strictly  adhere
to the deadlines set for the submission of GAD reports as specified in  item
V of COA Circular 2014-001.

We reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Mayor to direct  the
Municipal Budget Officer and the MSWDO to facilitate the  allocation  of  at
least 1% of its IRA solely  for  the  strengthening  and  implementation  of
programs, projects and  activities  of  the  LCPC  in  compliance  with  the
provisions mentioned in the RA No. 9344 and  DILG  Memorandum  Circular  No.
2012-120 dated July 4, 2012, in order that  prevention,  rehabilitation  and
reintegration of children at risk  and/or  in  conflict  with  the  law  are
ensured and given utmost importance.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer
to embark on measures  to  speed-up  the  implementation  of  the  remaining
portion of the project and in coordination  with  the  contractor,  maximize
the available human and machine resources to finally complete the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) issue an executive order or the Sangguniang Bayan, thru a resolution or ordinance, creating the LCPC who will formulate a comprehensive plan on juvenile delinquency prevention and oversee its proper implementation to address the protection and development of children in the Municipality; and (b) provide the required at least 1% of the annual IRA for the implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated the amount of P1,278,931.81 for Child Protection Fund. Meanwhile, Executive Order No. 05-2016 dated July 8, 2016 was issued to create and establish the LCPC that will serve as the primary agency to coordinate with and assist the Municipality for the adoption of a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive    (a) direct the SWMB to prepare the Solid Waste Management Plan for ten year period for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste and submit it to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for review and monitoring;              (b) allocate appropriate funds for effective implementation of the solid waste management programs, projects and activities; (c) encourage waste segregation among constituents including composting, reuse of recyclable materials and to avoid burning of wastes; and (d) suggest to the Sangguniang Bayan to legislate a no plastic policy for the protection of the environment.
observations.managementAction: The SWMP were prepared and submitted by the SWMB to the National Solid Waste Commission (NSWC) for review and monitoring. On June 8, 2016 the SWMP of the Municipality was conditionally approved by the NSWC and require the submission of lacking requirements within 60 days from the date of its approval.
observations.reason: Lack of initiative on the part of the concerned Municipal Officials to appropriate funds to finalized the SWMP, and support projects and programs embodied in their 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the end-user units and the BAC to strictly comply with the guidelines set forth by RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR on the preparation of PPMPs and its consolidation into an APP;        (b) require the BAC to diligently subscribe to the forms, and manuals prescribed by the GPPB; and (c) ensure the competencies of the BAC members by authorizing them to attend seminars on updates on the procurement law, subject to availability of funds therefor.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials complied with the forms prescribed by the GPPB in the preparation of PPMP and APP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management, through the BAC, the following courses of action: (a) Full compliance with the mandates of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, particularly in the preparation of Bidding Documents, its forms and contents, timelines and other pertinent provisions such as the correct computation of NFCC, providing the required information in the ITB such as where the bidding documents may be acquired or where it may be downloaded, including the time and location of the bid opening, and the complete stipulations in the Omnibus Sworn Statement as prescribed by the GPPB; (b) Ensure that all projects are covered with duly accomplished PPMPs and included in the approved APP prior to commencement of any public bidding as prescribed under Section 7 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (c) Check the completeness of the legal, technical and financial documents during bid opening, bid evaluation and post-qualification to ascertain the regularity and propriety of the award of contract and to ensure submission of complete documents pursuant to Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; (d) See that a Supplemental/Bid Bulletin is issued at least seven (7) calendar days before the deadline for the submission and receipt of bids whenever the Municipality, as the procuring entity, deem it necessary to clarify or modify any provisions of the Bidding Documents to ensure transparency in the procurement process in compliance with Section 22.5.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (e) Submit copy of contracts and supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days after its perfection to allow sufficient time to evaluate the regularity, propriety and validity of contractual covenants in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001.

We also recommended that the Mayor assess the professionalism and competencies of the BAC and the Technical Working Group members on the development, changes, and updates in the procurement process and to require them to attend seminars, if necessary, subject to availability of funds therefor.
observations.managementAction:
Upon receipt of the Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) in CY 2018, the BAC of the Municipality uses the 5th Edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents as prescribed by the GPPB on the procurement of infrastructure projects. 

BAC Chairman and Members attended PHILGEPs Training in Pasig City on July 17 to 19, 2018.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies noted in the procurement process were discussed in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management, through the BAC, the following courses of action: (a) Full compliance with the mandates of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly Section 17.1 thereof regarding the preparation of Bidding Documents, its forms and contents, and timelines, among others; (b) Check the correctness of the computation of the NFCC following the formula prescribed under Section 23.4.1.4 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;   (c) Ensure that the Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts as well as the SLCC are included in the 1st envelope before proceeding with the opening of the 2nd envelope, otherwise the bidder who failed to submit these documents shall be declare ineligible; (d) Ensure the completeness of all documentary requirements for procurement of goods through public bidding as called for under Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; (e) Provide complete description of items to be bid for transparency purposes pursuant to Section 2.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to attract and promote wider competition among prospective bidders; (f) Ensure absolute compliance with Section 19 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 requiring that prior to their official release to prospective bidders, no aspect of the bidding documents shall be divulged or released  to any prospective bidder or person having direct or indirect interest in the project to be procured, or to any party, except those officially authorized in the handling of the documents; and (g) Submit to the Auditor all approved contracts and supporting documents within five working days after its perfection in compliance to COA Circular 2009-01 to allow sufficient period to evaluate the propriety and validity of contractual covenants entered into by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Upon receipt of the AOM in CY 2018, the Municipal BAC uses the 5th Edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents as prescribed by the GPPB on the procurement of goods.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies noted in the procurement process were discussed in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require concerned Municipal Social and Welfare Development (MSWD) officers/Day Care worker (DCW) to purchase commodities for supplemental feeding in accordance with the guidelines of the procurement of goods pursuant to RA No. 9184 and observed the procedures in proper disbursements of government funds including proper documentation. Henceforth, refrain from making personal procurements of food commodities for later reimbursement from the Municipality. A bonded officer may be granted petty cash fund for supplemental feeding subject to liquidation on a prompt and regular basis.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWD Officer obtained approval from DSWD Regional Office that procurement of commodities for Supplementary Feeding may be done through alternative mode of procurement such as Shopping and Small Value Procurement under Section 52 and 53.9 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. 

Upon receipt of AOM in CY 2018, payment through reimbursement basis was significantly reduced. Only two (2) out of 18 transactions were paid through reimbursement basis.
observations.reason: Out of 18 transactions amounting to P1,065,653.10, two (2) were paid through reimbursement basis amounting to P34,090.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the execution of Memorandum of Agreement with the concerned market stall lessors or the Sangguniang Bayan pass a revenue ordinance or other similar documents to serve as legal basis for the collection and disbursement of funds for the Construction of Market Stalls.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials has ceased collecting fees from market stall vendors for the Construction of Public Market Stall immediately after the receipt of the AOM in the preceding year. Further, no similar transaction occurred during the year that requires the execution of MOA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Executive Committee of the Local Development Council identify and prioritize the development projects that could be implemented during the year, taking into consideration the technical capability of the Municipality.  Consider changes in the annual budget for projects which cannot be implemented during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan. 

We also recommended that the (a) MPDC ensure that (i) the programs, projects and activities chargeable against the 20% Development Fund partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality; and (ii) ineligible expenditures such as wages, petroleum products and repair and maintenance of motor vehicles are not charged against the 20% Development Fund; and           (iii) specific projects, programs and activities are presented in the AIP with sufficient details in compliance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 70. 

Further, we recommended for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Projects and Programs embodied in the Annual Investment Plan was in line with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01.

The Municipality was able to reduce the amount of ineligible expenditures charged against the 20% Development fund from P3,060,781.13 to P1,623,714.00 in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies noted in the utilization of 20% Development Fund were discussed in the Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction:
The MDRRM Officer submitted the monthly utilization of LDRRM Fund through email prior to every 15th of the month. 



The Municipal Accountant recorded the full amount of unexpended balance of MDRRM Fund for CY 2018 amounting to P1,953,321.41 in the Trust Fund Books from the General Fund books under Journal Entry Voucher No. 12-04 and 12-10, respectively.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies noted in the utilization of MDRRM Funds were discussed in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the help of the Municipal Accountant, exert efforts to enforce the settlement of suspensions and disallowances by sending letters to the concerned persons responsible/liable requiring them to settle their accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: NS No. 13-005-401(13) dated November 19, 2013 amounting to P7,730,044.32 was settled on June 29, 2018 upon submission of lacking documents by the Accountable Officers. Meanwhile, ND No. 2011-003-101(11), 2011-002-101(11), 2011-004-101(11), 2011-001-101(11) dated October 24, 2011 were settled upon payment totaling P7,843.00 receipted under AF No. 51 Official Receipt No. 184048 dated May 23, 2018.
observations.reason: No explanation was provided by the Management on the remaining unsettled Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 50-51 | We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and/or the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going government projects/programs/ activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs. 
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted Reports on Government PPAs for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 52-53 | We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to appoint a Municipal Engineer as mandated under Article 7, Section 477 of Republic Act No. 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, for the benefit of the better services of the government to the community.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor has already appointed a Municipal Engineer in CY 2018

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 53-59 | We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor through its BAC to maintain the accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the GPPB and to this Office within 14 days at the end of each semester.

We recommended that the BAC of the Municipality should invite COA representative as Observer in its procurement process especially during eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination of bids, bid evaluation, opening of the bids, and post-qualification. Likewise, the BAC should see to it that at least one observer (1) must come from a duly recognized group in a sector and the other shall come from non-government organization (NGO). The observers shall be invited at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage. In the event that a procurement activity has to be postponed, the observers shall be notified immediately of the change in schedule.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should require their bidders to submit to the Audit Team the Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the HOPE or to the public officers or employees by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree and with the required documentary requirements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should procure its common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) under Procurement Service of DBM. If the BAC of the Municipality desires to procure its CSEs other than the PS depot, the procuring entity must secure first a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) from PS depot.

We recommended that the Municipality should immediately submit to us the requested documents to support its compliance with the pertinent law/s. Non-compliance may result to suspension of the procurement concerned until they have complied with the requirements.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with applicable laws and rules of R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted additional documents pertaining to CY 2017 transactions
observations.reason: The Management failed to provide sufficient information to allow the evaluation of their actions taken

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2017 Part II, Page 61-65 | We recommended the Municipality to enhance transparency in the procurement especially by posting its procurement opportunities to the conspicuous place and to the PhilGEPS. Likewise, Section 21.2.1 of IRR of R.A. No. 9184 must always be complied with.

We recommended that the payment must be made to the intended supplier to avoid possible conflict of interest that may arise.

We recommended that the documents that must be submitted to the Audit Team should be duly accomplished such as by proper dating, numbering and signing.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should properly withhold taxes. This should be properly computed and indicated in the face of the Disbursement Voucher with due regard to BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007. 

We also recommended the Municipality to submit the documentary requirements timely, accurately and completely. Non-submission would result to suspension or disallowance on our audit, as the case may be. 

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001, R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

In the determination of the gender issues, reference may be made to the PPGD for 1995-2025, the law on Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM), other laws on gender and development and programs and projects of the national government on the subject.

Establish and maintain a GAD Database in implementing the Magna Carta for Women which helps in formulating GAD-related activities based on information gathered.

Institute monitoring processes, particularly in the formulation, assessment and updating of the annual agency plan on the continuing basis so that GAD consciousness shall serve as foundation of its commitments to the program.   

Prepare the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed under DILG-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 and submit to the Provincial Government and the National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW), copy furnish the DBM and COA which is also a requirement for the conduct of GAD Audit.

observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following: 1. Also, ensure that appropriations of the Local Development Fund do not include expenditures that are considered not allowable to be charged against the fund; and 2. Henceforth, the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% Local Government Development Fund for the succeeding years’ AIP shall be specific in order to enhance the transparency and accountability in the municipality’s implementation/undertaking of development projects, programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: To comply with the recommendations: To itemize the programs, projects and activities identified under the 20% LGDF. Continuous coordinati on with concerned program coordinators.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Council, to initiate the preparation of a separate Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan for the calendar year 2018 and onwards to give a clear direction to the council in implementing programs, projects and activities related to local disaster risk reduction management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the head of the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources to provide, enforce, monitor and evaluate to enforce compliance on the wearing of the complete, basic safety gears and equipment to all personnel that are directly dealing with the collections of solid wastes of the Municipality. Also, we recommended that proper actions be taken to address the illegal open pit operation inside the Municipal Dump Site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the DepEd District Supervision strictly adhere to the priority structure in the utilization of the SEF which shall be the basis of the Local School Board, consideration as defined under RA No.7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.01m s. 2000 and 01-B, s.2001 in order to maximize the benefits that the public schools could avail.
observations.managementAction: To comply with the recommendation: Conducted meeting among concerned personnel and members of the Local School Board, this municipality for compliance and action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the head of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, ensure that there is proper and sufficient budget allocation relative to the MSWM’s 10-year plan so that projects and programs are implemented in the year that they are supposed to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: In order to enhance transparency and/or public awareness; monitor the procurement process; and check the implementation on of awarded and implemented contracts of the Municipality, we recommended the following: 1. Require the BAC to prepare a comprehensive/ complete PMR for all types of procurement at the end of each semester and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity; and 2. Submit the duly approved PMR to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester; copy furnished the Audit Team’s office.
observations.managementAction: Committed to follow the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: 1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to identify the causes of the delayed submission of reports with original copies of supporting documents, and enforce their submission within the prescribed period so as to avoid receipt of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; and to evaluate the existing accounting system and personnel complement if still responsive to the growing demands of the operations of the LGU; 2. Also, direct the Municipal Treasurer (since original copies of the reports like: Report of Checks Issued, Report of Cash Disbursements, Report of Collections and Deposits and other reports and supporting documents emanate from her Office) and the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the rules and regulations in the submission of financial accounts and reports to the COA.
observations.managementAction: Committed to follow the audit recommendations; to identify the causes of the delayed submission of reports with original copies of supporting documents and enforce their submission within the prescribed period; evaluate the existing accounting system and personnel complement; direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the rules and regulations in the submission of financial accounts and reports to the COA.

observations.recommendation: 1. The Municipality complies and submits the lacking documents required. 2. The BAC strictly adheres to the revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Committed to follow the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should: 1. Direct the personnel concerned to process the transfer of ownership of various sites from the sellers to the Municipality. 3. Direct the personnel concerned to cause the return of TCTs that are with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should: 1. Direct the personnel concerned to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9003 particularly Sections 32 and 37 thereof. 2. Direct the MENRO-Designate, the MPDO, and the Budget Officer to come up with a Comprehensive Solid Waste Management Program with sufficient budgetary requirements. 3. Revisit the penal provisions of the Ordinance relative to the burning of agricultural wastes and impose stiff penalties. 4. Direct the personnel concerned to expedite the procurement of a shredder so that the culvert molder could serve its intended purpose of reducing residual wastes through recycling.
observations.managementAction: Committed to follow the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality allocate 1% of their annual IRA for child protection program as required under RA No. 9344 and to create their LCPC and formulate and implement a comprehensive plan specifically to strengthen and develop the needs of the children in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended that the BAC properly evaluate the submitted eligibility documents of the bidder to ensure its conformity with the requirements of the procurement at hand.

We also recommended that Management utilize the LDRRM Fund solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities pursuant to RA No. 10121.
