observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management synchronize the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget with the planning and budgeting calendar to enable its timely submission to the PPDO to ensure its alignment with the priorities of the Province and to the DILG for review and approval before its incorporation in the annual budget.
observations.reason: Related discussion in Observation No. 15, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following:

• Instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their respective books to accounts. Any discrepancy noted should be investigated in accordance with existing regulations and take appropriate action thereon;

• The Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 59 of NGAS Manual on the procedures for the stale checks. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant should make the necessary adjustments in the books to account for the stale checks, provided they are supported with the original disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents.

• The Municipal Accountant should submit Bank Reconciliation Statements for all funds regularly as required under existing regulations.

• Likewise, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should accomplish certification in her Cashbooks every end of the month in compliance with Sections 26 and 27 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer commented that she will initiate in the reconciliation of their respective books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality should allocate at least 1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to ensure implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

Further, we recommended that the Municipality should establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC). Once established, the latter should prepare plans, such as but not limited to: (1) Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for the integration in the local development plan, (2) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and (3) other relevant plans for the allowable programs for the children under Section 15 of R.A. No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. In accordance with the said plans, the indicated programs, projects, and activities should be properly disbursed and implemented with due regard to the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that they will allocate 1% of their IRA for the programs, projects and activities for the protection of children on CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended toinstruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit liquidations or supporting documents of all unliquidated cash advances to COA, and to strictly observe the COA rules and regulations in the disbursement of public funds.  Otherwise, appropriate sanctions will be enforced by this Commission.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer committed to liquidate these cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to insure all new buildings including its equipment and other attachments thereon pursuant to the provisions of Section5 of R.A. 656 and Section489 of GAAM, Volume I.

Provide an appropriation for the insurance premium for the insurance of all its properties having insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).
observations.managementAction: To be included in CY 2018 budget.
observations.reason: Not yet implemented in CY 2017 as there were no funds appropriated during the year. But the Management disclosed that the properties will be insured in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management, through the:
General Services Officer (i) include the actual/acquisition cost of all parcels of land in the RPCPPE to provide basis for disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; and (ii) initiate the disposal procedures by preparing the IIRUP for the eventual disposal of the unserviceable properties and dropping its amount from the books of accounts in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445;
Municipal Accountant provide adequate disclosure in the Notes of Financial Statements pending disposal of unserviceable PPE totaling P13,503,830.69; and
Concerned accountable officer submit to the Auditor the required documents for the request for relief from property accountability pursuant to Section 378 of RA No. 7160.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:
conduct a thorough review of long outstanding accounts payable to (i) facilitate proper reconciliation of reciprocal accounts/transactions to properly identify the same; and (ii) revert to the general fund all dormant and long outstanding payables/balances without valid claimants; and
ensure that no obligation is booked up unless the same is founded on a valid claim that is supported by sufficient evidence and documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, (a) exert more efforts in demanding immediate settlement of the unliquidated cash advances including those granted to officers and employees that are no longer in the government service; (b) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no additional cash advance is granted unless the previous one has been liquidated and duly accounted for; and (c) adhere  strictly with the provisions of Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 regarding the full settlement and liquidation of cash advances at the end of each year.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) consider requiring the Municipal Treasurer to suspend and withhold the payment of salaries or any amount due the concerned accountable officers and employees who are still in service, considering the lapse of time that the aforementioned cash advances have been outstanding; and (b) institute or file appropriate administrative charges against erring officers and employees pursuant to Section 9.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-004, if warranted.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) analyze and break down the amount pertaining to the previous and current Municipal Treasurers to properly establish their accountabilities and transfer the amount pertaining to the previous Municipal Treasurers from Cash in Vault account to Other Receivable account for its fair presentation in the financial statements; and (ii) keep the accounts in such detail as is necessary by maintaining subsidiary records pursuant to Sections 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445; and (b) Municipal Treasurer reconcile the cashbooks with accounting records at least once a month and immediately examine any discrepancy to forestall accumulation of reconciling items.
Also, after proper reconciliation, we strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor exert efforts to enforce its collection from the concerned Municipal Treasurers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) issue an executive order or the Sangguniang Bayan, thru a resolution or ordinance, creating the LCPC who will formulate a comprehensive plan on juvenile delinquency prevention and oversee its proper implementation to address the protection and development of children in the Municipality; and (b) provide the required at least 1% of the annual IRA for the implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the SWMB to prepare the Solid Waste Management Plan for ten year period for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste and submit it to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for review and monitoring; (b) allocate appropriate funds for effective implementation of the solid waste management programs, projects and activities; (c) encourage waste segregation among constituents including composting, reuse of recyclable materials and to avoid burning of wastes; and (d) suggest to the Sangguniang Bayan to legislate a no plastic policy for the protection of the environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the execution of Memorandum of Agreement with the concerned market stall lessors or the Sangguniang Bayan pass a revenue ordinance or other similar documents to serve as legal basis for the collection and disbursement of funds for the Construction of Market Stalls.

We recommended that the Executive Committee of the Local Development Council identify and prioritize the development projects that could be implemented during the year, taking into consideration the technical capability of the Municipality.  Consider changes in the annual budget for projects which cannot be implemented during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.
We also recommended that the (a) MPDC ensure that (i) the programs, projects and activities chargeable against the 20% Development Fund partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality; and (ii) ineligible expenditures such as wages, petroleum products and repair and maintenance of motor vehicles are not charged against the 20% Development Fund; and (iii) specific projects, programs and activities are presented in the AIP with sufficient details in compliance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 70.
Further, we recommended for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management, thru the GAD Focal Person, (a) synchronize the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget with the planning and budgeting calendar to enable its timely submission to the PPDO to check its alignment with the priorities of the Province and then to the DILG Regional Office for final review and approval; (b) develop GAD PPAs that would address gender issues in the Municipality to strengthen its gender-responsive governance; and (c) ensure that GAD Fund is fully utilized in compliance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the help of the Municipal Accountant, exert efforts to enforce the settlement of suspensions and disallowances by sending letters to the concerned persons responsible/liable requiring them to settle their accountabilities.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to require the
Inventory Committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE to
determine the actual existence and proper valuation of the same, pursuant to
Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1. The Inventory
Committee may coordinate and request the assistance of the department heads to
conduct inventory of all properties under their respective offices and submit the
inventory report to the committee for validation and verification.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer
and the Municipal Accountant should create, update and maintain the required
property cards and ledger cards. Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results of
the actual count with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies
between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and
reconciled immediately.
Furthermore, we reiterated that the physical count of PPE should be
undertaken annually by the Inventory Committee and the corresponding RPCPPE
be submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year as required
under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to make extra effort to reconstruct/trace back the composition of
Infrastructure Assets and prepare the corresponding GLs and SLs.
We also recommended for the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory
Committee and enjoin the concerned department heads’ compliance with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and submit
the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to compel the Municipal
Accounting Office to establish the breakdown and component of every major real
account reflected in the submitted Statement of Financial Position as of December
31, 2017 and for this purpose, maintain subsidiary ledgers. Likewise, the General
Ledger for CY 2017 should be reconstructed to prove the ending balances of the
accounts. The schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in
the General Ledger as of December 31, 2017 should be submitted immediately to the
Audit Team as supporting schedule for the financial statements submitted.
We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to review and be guided
by the NGAS Manual for LGUs and other related COA issuances for the procedures
and appropriate accounting formats to be used in recording the financial
transactions of the Municipality. Likewise, the JEV should be duly approved before
recording to the appropriate journals or books of original entry.
In addition, we recommended for the Municipal Accountant to trace/review
the accounts with negative balances and make the necessary adjusting/correcting
entries with supporting documents.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to
instruct/remind the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
regularly reconcile their respective books of accounts. Any discrepancy noted
should be investigated in accordance with existing regulations and take appropriate
action thereon.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements
(all funds) for CY 2017 and previous years including the duly certified copies of
bank statements. Henceforth, Bank Reconciliation Statements should be submitted
within ten (10) days after the end of each month in compliance with COA Circular
No. 96-011.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to strictly
enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
Likewise, require the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances, else
suspension of salaries should be imposed until respective outstanding cash advances
have been fully settled and liquidated.
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to create and maintain the GLs and SLs and properly present an
accurate and reliable Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances (RUCA).
Henceforth, we recommended for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce
the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances
prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Office of the Municipal
Accountant periodically reconcile the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds,
Due to Other Funds and other reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these
accounts in the financial statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary
adjustments should be effected in the books of accounts.
Likewise, we strongly recommended that Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) and
General Ledgers (GLs) should be maintained by the Office of the Municipal
Accountant.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should
instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and/or the
Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on
implementation of PPAs.
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to appoint a Municipal
Engineer as mandated under Article 7, Section 477 of Republic Act No. 7160
otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, for the benefit of the better
services of the government to the community.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor through its
BAC to maintain the accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the
GPPB and to this Office within 14 days at the end of each semester.

We recommended that the BAC of the Municipality should invite COA
representative as Observer in its procurement process especially during
eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination
of bids, bid evaluation, opening of the bids, and post-qualification. Likewise,
the BAC should see to it that at least one observer (1) must come from a duly
recognized group in a sector and the other shall come from non-government
organization (NGO). The observers shall be invited at least five (5) calendar
days before the date of the procurement stage. In the event that a procurement
activity has to be postponed, the observers shall be notified immediately of the
change in schedule.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should
require their bidders to submit to the Audit Team the Sworn Affidavit that it is
not related to the HOPE or to the public officers or employees by
consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree and with the required
documentary requirements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality should procure
its common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) under
Procurement Service of DBM. If the BAC of the Municipality desires to
procure its CSEs other than the PS depot, the procuring entity must secure
first a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) from PS depot.

We recommended that the Municipality should immediately submit to us
the requested documents to support its compliance with the pertinent law/s.
Non-compliance may result to suspension of the procurement concerned until
they have complied with the requirements.
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with applicable laws and
rules of R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the the provisions of COA
Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. We also recommend the immediate
submission of the copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their
supporting documents which are due on the prescribed period to the Office of the
Auditor.
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA
Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended the Municipality to enhance transparency in the
procurement especially by posting its procurement opportunities to the
conspicuous place and to the PhilGEPS. Likewise, Section 21.2.1 of IRR of
R.A. No. 9184 must always be complied with.
We recommended that the payment must be made to the intended
supplier to avoid possible conflict of interest that may arise.
We recommended that the documents that must be submitted to the
Audit Team should be duly accomplished such as by proper dating, numbering
and signing.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should properly
withhold taxes. This should be properly computed and indicated in the face of
the Disbursement Voucher with due regard to BIR Revenue Memorandum
Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.
We also recommended the Municipality to submit the documentary
requirements timely, accurately and completely. Non-submission would result
65
to suspension or disallowance on our audit, as the case may be.
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with P.D. No. 1445,
COA Circular No. 2012-001, R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended the Municipality to enhance transparency in the
procurement especially by posting its procurement opportunities to the
conspicuous place and to the PhilGEPS. Likewise, Section 21.2.1 of IRR of
R.A. No. 9184 must always be complied with.
We recommended that the requests for price quotation (RFQs) should
be prepared and sent to at least (3) suppliers of known qualification.
We recommended that the documents that must be submitted to the
Audit Team should be duly accomplished such as by proper dating, numbering
and authorized signatory.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should properly
compute the taxes to be withheld in accordance with BIR Revenue
Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007. The computation
should be at the face of the DV.
We recommended the Municipality to submit the documentary
requirements timely, accurately and completely. Non-submission would result
to suspension or disallowance on our audit, as the case may be.

We recommended the Municipality to enhance transparency in the
procurement especially by posting its procurement opportunities to the
conspicuous place and to the PhilGEPS. Likewise, Section 21.2.1 of IRR of
R.A. No. 9184 must always be complied with.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to obligate the
appropriation and determine the completeness of the supporting documents.
This could be inferred by certifying in the Box “A” in the voucher.
We recommended that the documents that must be submitted to the
Audit Team should be duly accomplished such as by proper dating, numbering
and signing by all accountable officials concerned.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should properly
compute the taxes to be withheld in accordance with BIR Revenue
Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007. The computation
should be reflected at the face of the DV.

We recommended that the Municipality should allocate at least 1% of its IRA
for that purpose in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to ensure
implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.
Further, we recommended that the Municipality should establish the Local
Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC). Once established, the latter should
prepare plans, such as but not limited to: (1) Council’s Annual Work and Financial
Plan prioritized according to needs for the integration in the local development
plan, (2) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and (3) other relevant plans
for the allowable programs for the children under Section 15 of R.A. No. 9344 and
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. In accordance with
the said plans, the indicated programs, projects, and activities should be properly
disbursed and implemented with due regard to the existing accounting and auditing
rules and regulations.

We recommended the Municipality to faithfully comply with the
preparation and submission of the documents as prescribed under DEPED-
DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the said Joint
Circular.

We also recommended that the disbursements to be made out of SEF
must be within the budget and in accordance with Section 100(c) of R.A. No.
7160 and Section 4 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
Lastly, we recommended the Municipality, through its Municipal
Treasurer, to exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real
property for the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to fast track the
implementation of all the programmed projects and activities charged to 20% DF
during the year to achieve effectively and efficiently its socio-economic development
and environmental outcomes.

We reiterated our recommendation the management to initiate the immediate
establishment of Municipal Solid Waste Management Facility. Henceforth, we
recommended strict implementation of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of
2000 or Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended that the Management should prepare and submit the
LDRRMFIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. In addition, we also
recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statement the amount and details of the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF.
Likewise, the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of ₱1,155,109.25 for CY 2017
should be transferred to the Trust Fund and be deposited to the respective bank
account.
We further recommended that the guidelines provided in NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1, COA Circular No. 2012-002
and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 for the
allocation, utilization and reporting of LDRRMF be strictly observed.

We recommended for the Management to strictly comply with Section 48 of
the General Provisions of R.A. No. 10964 known as the “General Appropriations Act
of FY 2018” for the monthly net take home pay of the regular employees which
is not lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00), after all authorized deductions
based on the above order of preference presented.

We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw the
necessary accounting journal entry pertaining to the audit disallowance that became
final and executory amounting to ₱1,225,000.00 and submit a copy of the JEV and
its supporting documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement particularly for the eight bank accounts immediately.
Direct management to augment the work force assigned in the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of reports.
Submit the supporting documents necessary to substantiate the identified reconciling items.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all paid disbursement vouchers are included in the Report of Checks Issued and forwarded to the Accounting Department for the timely and proper recording in the books.
Make representations with the bank to include in the monthly bank statements the copies of bank debit/credit memoranda.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to online/ i-access to be able to keep track of its balances at any given time and for the Municipal Accountant to update regularly the reconciliation of the bank accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Direct allRevenue Collecting Officers to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and to remit their collections on a daily basis to the Liquidating Officer for her to deposit collections daily to the depository bank or as soon as the collections reach P10,000.00.
Instruct the Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of collector and check the same against new balances on hand column.  Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I.
Instruct the Accountable Officers to stop using the collections for payment of any expenses as this will be covered appropriately by issuance of checks or through the agencys established petty cash fund.

We recommended and management agreed to -
Establish and maintain the Petty Cash Fund under the imprest system.
Establish PCF equivalent to an amount sufficient to cover only expenses that are difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by check, pursuant to COA-Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
The Annual Procurement Plan (APP) should include requirements for common-use office supplies to avoid purchases of the same through cash advances.
Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission of official receipts and other supporting documents to support the liquidation of cash advances/PCF as proof of payments and refrain from using Reimbursement Expenses Receipts (RERs).
Maintain a PCF cashbook and record therein transactions daily.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Require the BAC Secretary –
	to submit to COA the approved contracts together with its supporting papers for the said perfected projects for COA’s review.
	to submit prospective contracts within 5 days upon its perfection in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.
Advise management headed by the Local Chief Executive to revisit the internal control policies over LGU procurement operations to identify and address these recurring lapses over submission of contracts.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly render accounting of all paid vouchers and other related reports on its due date as required under Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and refrain from its usual lump sum submission within the prescribed period for timely post-audit and verification of accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure timely preparation and submission of the monthly Trial Balance and JEVs and that the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the SAAOB under the prescribed period for proper monitoring and timely decision making.

We recommended and management agreed to –
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the projects and activities to be funded and a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
Likewise, require the LDRRMO to submit report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council.
We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive –
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer the November and December 20% development funds to its corresponding bank account.
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to ensure the timely completion of the programs, projects and activities.

We recommended and management agreed that the GAD Focal Person –
Formulate a well-defined programs, projects and activities that are gender related to conform with the purpose of establishing the GAD Plan, that is, to address gender issues and women empowerment.
In the determination of the gender issues, reference may be made to the PPGD for 1995-2025, the law on Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM), other laws on gender and development and programs and projects of the national government on the subject.
Establish and maintain a GAD Database in implementing the Magna Carta for Women which helps in formulating GAD-related activities based on information gathered.
Institute monitoring processes, particularly in the formulation, assessment and updating of the annual agency plan on the continuing basis so that GAD consciousness shall serve as foundation of its commitments to the program.
Prepare the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed under DILG-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 and submit to the Provincial Government and the National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW), copy furnish the DBM and COA which is also a requirement for the conduct of GAD Audit.

We recommended that Management enforce strictly the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, aggressively follow-up long outstanding cash advances and compel them to settle their accounts. And after due notice/demand, cause the withholding of the amount due the concerned officials.
We have also recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers giving additional cash advances unless the previous ones are settled.

We recommended that Management closely monitor the receivables and intensify its collection, otherwise, file the appropriate request for authority to write-off for the dormant accounts duly supported with the relevant documents pursuant to Item 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the completed projects to the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
We have also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant once constructed projects are completed and furnish the latter with the documents for proper reclassification to the appropriate accounts.

We recommended that Management strictly require the recipient NGOs/POs to submit the duly certified final Utilization Report to support its liquidation pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001. Issue demand letter to the NGOs/POs. If no settlement is made, institute legal sanctions pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations applicable for violation of the provisions of the above COA Circular.
We also recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from approving Disbursement Vouchers releasing fund transfers to the same NGOs/POs unless the previous ones are fully settled.

We recommended that Management submit the contracts including all the necessary supporting documents within five (5) working days after perfection, for its timely review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 
No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that in the procurement process, the Management should strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that submission of the required monthly reports be on time as prescribed under Section 122 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We have recommended that Management revoke the designation of 
Mr. Delima as the Acting MPDC-I in view of the pronouncement of the CSC that the designation is void and hire or appoint/employ somebody who qualifies the minimum qualification standards of a Municipal Planning and Development Officer.

We recommended that Management discontinue the deduction from the salaries of the loans from thrift banks/lending institutions for those who have net take home pay of below 4,000.
We also recommended that the MOA entered into between the banks and the LGU for the collection arrangement should not be renewed.

We recommended that the Accounting Office/Human Resource Management Office of the Municipality comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 8291 by deducting mandatory deductions for GSIS Premiums for Social Insurance Fund and Employees’ Compensation Insurance Fund and remit directly to the GSIS within the first ten (10) days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of the above subproject so that the provision of facilities for which the funds were intended could be afforded to the public.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal  Treasurer  to
properly coordinate with the Assessor’s and  Accountant’s  offices  so  that
the preparation of pertinent  reports  and  documents  are  coursed  through
proper offices, in order to come up with a prompt basis for  the  Tax  dues,
RPT and SET Receivables  during  the  year.  Likewise,  we  recommended  the
proper compliance to the above-mentioned guidelines.

We recommended to the  Municipal  Mayor  thru  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
facilitate a reevaluation and updating of the collection scheme employed  by
the Municipality and to extend extra effort to increase collection  rate  of
both the current and delinquent receivables.

We also recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to revisit the provisions  in
the RA No. 7160 regarding the collection  of  RPT  and  SET  Receivables  to
enable him more, and acquire more confidence from the Act,  to  implement  a
proper and high efficiency collection rate on the receivables.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

      1. Facilitate the proper compliance and strict adherence of the above-
        mentioned Joint Memorandum Circular as far as the utilization of the
        20% LDF is concerned. Also, ensure that appropriations of the  Local
        Development Fund do not include expenditures that are considered not
        allowable to be charged against the fund; and

      2. Henceforth, the programs/projects/activities to be  funded  out  of
        the 20% Local Government Development Fund for the succeeding  years’
        AIP shall be specific in  order  to  enhance  the  transparency  and
        accountability in the municipality’s  implementation/undertaking  of
        development projects funded therein.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of  the  Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council, to initiate the  preparation  of
a separate Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan for  the  calendar  year  2018  and
onwards to give a clear direction to the council in implementing programs,
projects  and  activities  related  to   local   disaster   risk   reduction
management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the head of the  Municipal
Environment and Natural Resources to provide, enforce, monitor and  evaluate
to enforce compliance on the wearing of the  complete,  basic  safety  gears
and  equipment  to  all  personnel  that  are  directly  dealing  with   the
collections of solid wastes of the Municipality. Also, we  recommended  that
proper actions be taken to address the illegal  open  pit  operation  inside
the Municipal Dump Site.

We recommended that the DepEd District Supervision strictly  adhere  to  the
priority structure in the utilization of the SEF which shall  be  the  basis
of the Local School Board consideration as defined under  RA  No.  7160  and
DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01m s. 2000  and  01-B,  s.  2001)  in
order to maximize the benefits that the public schools could avail.

We recommended to the Municipality to  allocate  at  least  1%  of  its  IRA
solely for the strengthening and implementation of  programs,  projects  and
activities of the LCPC in compliance with the provisions  mentioned  in  the
RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated  July  4,  2012,
in order that prevention, rehabilitation and reintegration  of  children  at
risk  and/or  in  conflict  with  the  law  are  ensured  and  given  utmost
importance.

We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Team to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and require the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain updated PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, to facilitate its reconciliation with the RPCPPE. Should there be any discrepancy between these records, the same should be properly examined to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances for its fair presentation in the Statement of Financial Position. We also recommended for the preparation of the IIRUP for unserviceable property to facilitate disposal and dropping thereof from the books.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical count and submitted inventory report on February 23, 2017.
observations.reason: The amount of land in the RPCPPE was reported in its current assessed value which was not reconciled with the carrying amount reflected in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

conduct a thorough review of long outstanding accounts payable and revert to the general fund all dormant and long outstanding payables/balances without valid claimants, if warranted;

ensure that no obligation is booked unless the same is founded on a valid claim that is supported by sufficient evidence and documentation; and

present the long outstanding accounts payables as non-current liabilities in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Long outstanding Accounts Payable was reduced from P3,093,409.66 to P3,047,981.66 during the year.
observations.reason: Doubts/insufficient knowledge on the legal basis on how to revert the outstanding balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Mayor enjoin compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and other existing rules and regulation on the proper grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and

observations.recommendation:
7.a Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP)

(7.a) We recommended the management thru its Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare PPMP, consolidate it with the APP that must be approved by the Municipal Mayor and submit it to COA in the prescribed latest format issued by the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB).

7.b Procurement Monitoring Report

(7.b) We recommended the Municipal Mayor through its BAC to maintain the accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the GPPB and to this Office within 14 days of each semester.

7.c Observers

(7.c) We recommended that the Municipality BAC should invite COA representative as Observer. Likewise, the BAC should see to it that at least one observer (1) must come from a duly recognized group in a sector and the other shall come from non-government organization (NGO). The observers shall be invited at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage.

7.d Disclosure of Relations

(7.d) We recommended the Municipality to require their contractors/ suppliers to submit to us Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the HOPE or to the persons mentioned above by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree and the same should be submitted with the required documentary requirements.

7.e Procurement Service (PS)

(7.e) We recommended that the Municipality should procure its common-use supplies, materials and equipment under Procurement Service of DBM. On the grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of stocks in such depot, the procuring entity must secure a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS) from such depot. Those CSEs that the LGU already procured in violation of the provisions mentioned above, the management should send us the written explanation that would justify their action. Otherwise, the persons responsible would suffer the corresponding penalty prescribed by law.

Overall Recommendations:

• We recommended the Municipality to uphold and guide themselves with the applicable laws and rules such as R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR, respectively.

• Section 4(6) of PD 1445 states that “Claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation.” In line with this, we recommend the Municipality to submit accurately, timely and completely the documentary requirements as prescribed by law, or rules and regulations.

• Lastly, no public officials or employees should be pecuniary interested, directly or indirectly, in any contract or transaction of the government in violation of the laws such as Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees (R.A. 6713) and Anti-Graft and Corrupt Practices Act (R.A. 3019).

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the inventory
committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE to determine
the actual existence and proper valuation of the same, pursuant to Section 124
of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1. The inventory committee may
coordinate and request the assistance of the department heads to conduct
inventory of all properties under their respective offices and submit the
inventory report to the committee for validation and verification.

We also recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant should create, update and maintain the required
property cards and ledger cards. Moreover, cause the reconciliation of results
of the actual count with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies
between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and
reconciled immediately.

Lastly, ensure that the physical inventory be undertaken annually and
submit the RPCPPE to the COA Auditor concerned on or before January 31 of
each year.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following:
 Instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
regularly reconcile their respective books to accounts. Any discrepancy
noted should be investigated in accordance with existing regulations and
take appropriate action thereon;
 The Municipal Accountant ICO-Municipal Treasurer to comply with
Section 59 of NGAS Manual on the procedures for the stale checks.
Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant should make the necessary
adjustments in the books to account for the stale checks, provided they
are supported with the original disbursement vouchers and other
supporting documents.
 The Municipal Accountant should submit Bank Reconciliation
Statements for all funds regularly as required under existing regulations.
 Likewise, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer should accomplish certification
in her Cashbooks every end of the month in compliance with Sections 26
and 27 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the rules
and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances
prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
Likewise, require the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances,
else suspension of salaries should be imposed until respective outstanding cash
advances have been fully settled and liquidated.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should
reclassify immediately the affected accounts and be guided by Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant should include other cash advances
accounts in the presentation of RUCA.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant periodically reconcile
the balances of accounts Due from Other Funds, Due to Other Funds and other
reciprocal account in all Funds to fairly present these accounts in the financial
statements of the municipality. Likewise, the necessary adjustments should be
effected in the books of accounts.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to compel the Municipal
Accountant to submit immediately the complete financial reports and other
documents required to be submitted. Otherwise, suspension of the payment of
salaries of the Municipal Accountant is recommended until she has complied
with the submission of required reports and documents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management particularly
the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the aforecited
regulations. We also recommended the immediate submission of the CY 2016
copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting
documents to the Office of the Auditor.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of
COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended the management thru its Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to prepare PPMP, consolidate it with the APP that must be approved by
the Municipal Mayor and submit it to COA office timely, accurately and
completely in the prescribed latest format issued by the Government
Procurement Policy Board (GPPB).

We recommended the Municipal Mayor through its BAC to maintain the
accuracy and completeness of the PMR and submit it to the GPPB and to this
Office within 14 days of each semester.

We recommended that the Municipality BAC should invite COA
representative as Observer. Likewise, the BAC should see to it that at least one
observer (1) must come from a duly recognized group in a sector and the other
shall come from non-government organization (NGO). The observers shall be
invited at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage.

Thus, we recommended the Municipality to require their contractors/
suppliers to submit to us Sworn Affidavit that it is not related to the HOPE or
to the persons mentioned above by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil
49
degree and the same should be submitted with the required documentary
requirements.

We recommended that the Municipality should procure its common-use
supplies, materials and equipment under Procurement Service of DBM. On the
grounds of efficiency, practicality, economic viability and/or non-availability of
stocks in such depot, the procuring entity must secure a Certificate of Non-
Availability of Stocks (CNAS) from such depot. Those CSEs that the LGU
already procured in violation of the provisions mentioned above, the
management should send us the written explanation that would justify their
action. Otherwise, the persons responsible would suffer the corresponding
penalty prescribed by law.

Overall Recommendations
We recommended the Municipality to uphold and guide themselves with
the applicable laws and rules such as R.A. No. 9184 and its R-IRR, respectively.
Section 4(6) of PD 1445 states that “Claims against government funds
shall be supported with complete documentation.” In line with this, we
recommend the Municipality to submit accurately, timely and completely the
documentary requirements as prescribed by law, or rules and regulations.
Section 39(3) of said decree provides “It shall be the duty of the officials or
employees concerned xxx to comply promptly with these requirements. Failure or
refusal to do so without justifiable cause shall constitute a ground for
administrative disciplinary action xxx.”

Lastly, no public officials or employees should be pecuniary interested,
directly or indirectly, in any contract or transaction of the government in
violation of the laws such as Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public
Officials and Employees (R.A. 6713) and Anti-Graft and Corrupt Practices Act
(R.A. 3019). Even if the public officials and employees of the Municipality don’t
know the law, they are still liable because Art. 3 of the Civil Code provides
“Ignorance of the law excuses no one from compliance therewith.”

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator / Acting Municipal
Engineer to submit immediately to this Office the list of all on-going
government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented for the year together with the monthly monitoring report on
implementation of PPAs.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator together with the Local Development Council should coordinate,
monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development
programs, projects and activities of the Municipality in accordance with the
approved development plan and properly render a report to the Mayor the
status of every project, problems encountered and of any recommended
measures to address such problem.

We recommended the management to:
 include in the municipal programs an information campaign on proper
waste disposal to avoid hazardous effect of improper waste disposal to
the community and environment; and
 initiate the immediate establishment of Municipal Solid Waste
Management Facility and Barangay Materials Recovery Facility.

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and allocate at
least 1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344
and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in order to
ensure implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of
children.
We recommended and management agreed to require the municipal accountant to prepare and submit promptly the said bank reconciliation statements to COA.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to supervise the creation of a separate special trust fund bank account for any unexpended balance of LDRRM fund in accordance with Republic Act 101211.  Also, it was recommended to deposit any unexpended balance of DRRMF in its special depository account.

Management Comment:
The management committed that it will facilitate to open a separate bank depository account exclusive for the DRRM fund.

We recommended the management to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in all stages of the procurement of goods and services and implementation of projects to achieve transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement process.  Also, we recommended the management to submit all the required documents for projects implemented by administration to proceed with the auditorial and technical review.

We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and submit the abstract of all of its collections remitted by the accountable officers; and direct the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain the subsidiary ledger of the Accountable Officers on the basis of the abstract submitted.
We have recommended that Management require the Collecting Officers to remit all collections intact in accordance with the aforementioned local government code provision.

We have recommended and Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties of the LGU and submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the books with the inventory report and property cards.

We have recommended and Management agreed to notify the Office of the Auditor of any deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours through submission of the aforementioned report in accordance with the said Circular.
We have recommended that Management prepare the Reports in accordance with the COA rules so that verification will be facilitated.

We have recommended that Management prepare and timely submit the reports on the LDRRMF. LDRRM Officers must strictly comply with the accounting and reporting requirements stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated 12 September 2012, to ensure that the financial information are appropriately taken up in the books and properly presented in the financial statements with adequate disclosures. These reports should be posted on the LGU’s website and conspicuous places within its premises.

We have recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of the above-cited Joint Memorandum Circular. Evaluate further the components included in its 20% Local Development Fund in order to avoid charging of expenditures that are not related to and/or not connected with the implementation of development projects, programs and activities. Otherwise, incurrence of these expenses shall be disallowed in audit.

We have also recommended to utilize the General Fund Proper account for programs, projects and activities not chargeable against the 20% Local Development Fund.

Recommendations:  We recommended the following:

   1. Submit the list of specific PPAs funded out of the 20% DF appearing in
      the CY 2016’s AIP as loan amortization, barangay development  programs
      and counterpart to the national government projects; and


   2.       Henceforth, the PPAs to be funded out of  the  20%  DF  for  the
      coming  years’  AIP  shall  be  specific  in  order  to  enhance   the
      transparency    and    accountability    in     the     Municipality’s
      implementation/undertaking of development projects funded therein.

Recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor,  as  the  Chairperson
of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction  Management  Council,  to  initiate
the preparation of the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan for the  calendar  year
2017 and onwards to give a clear direction to the  council  in  implementing
PPAs related to local disaster risk reduction management.

Recommendations:  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the
Municipal Solid  Waste  Personnel,  especially  the  MSWMO,  to  immediately
implement the proper segregation from barangay level up to the  disposal  of
solid wastes in the landfill including its strict control and monitoring  in
accordance with RA No. 9003.

We also recommended that the Municipal  Mayor,  as  head  of  the  Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board, ensure that there  is  proper  and  sufficient
budget allocation relative to the MSWM’s 10-year plan so that  projects  and
programs  are  implemented  in  the  year  that  they  are  supposed  to  be
implemented.

Recommendations:  We  recommended  that  the  DepEd   District   Supervision
strictly adheres to the priority structure in the  utilization  of  the  SEF
which shall be the basis  of  the  Local  School  Board’s  consideration  as
defined under RA No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.  01,  s.
1998 dated April 14, 1998 with supplemental provisions under Joint  Circular
No. 01-A, s. 2000 and 01-B, s. 2001 dated March 14, 2000 and June 25,  2001,
respectively in order to maximize  the  benefits  that  the  public  schools
could avail.


We  also  recommended  that  any  disbursement  shall  be  covered   by   an
appropriate ordinance in order to comply with Section 305 of RA No. 7160.

Recommendations: In order to enhance transparency and/or  public  awareness;
monitor the procurement process; and check  the  implementation  of  awarded
and  implemented  contracts  of  the  Municipality,   we   recommended   the
following:



   1. Require the BAC to prepare a comprehensive/complete PMR for all  types
      of procurement at the end of each semester and have it approved by the
      Head of the Procuring Entity; and


   2. Submit the duly-approved PMR to the GPPB  in  printed  and  electronic
      format within 14 calendar days after the end of  each  semester;  copy
      furnished the Audit Team’s office.

Recommendations:   We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the
Municipal Accountant to:

   1. Demand the immediate settlement/liquidation of  the  outstanding  cash
      advances.  Refrain  from  granting  additional  cash  advances  unless
      previous ones are properly liquidated and  accounted  for.  Cause  the
      withholding of salaries for those who failed to liquidate  within  the
      time frame set by regulations; and


   2. Adhere  strictly  to  the  rules  and  regulations  on  the  granting,
      utilization and  liquidation  of  cash  advances  provided  for  under
      Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, COA  Circular  No.
      2003-003 and other pertinent rules and regulations.

Recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Accountant  to  compute  and
provide for the Impairment of the Accounts Receivables and Loans  Receivable
- Others accounts pursuant to the guidelines presented in PPSAS 29.

We also recommended to the Municipal Mayor to provide mechanism  to  enforce
the proper collection, if  possible  of  the  receivables  especially  those
which have been long outstanding.

Recommendation:  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor   direct   the
Municipal Agriculture Officer in coordination with the Municipal  Accountant
to conduct a  physical  count  of  the  Biological  Assets  and  submit  the
inventory report  to  the  Municipal  Mayor  copy  furnished  the  Municipal
Accountant and the Audit Team Leader of COA.

We recommended the following:
 We reiterate that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should create an Inventory Committee, consisting of a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office.
 Require each department head to fast track inventory of all properties under their office and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification of the correctness and existence of the property.
 The inventory committee should check the condition of the property, any cast damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable and Waste Material Report and immediately request for disposal from the Commission.
 Compel the inventory committee to fully accomplish the RPCPPE, indicating the actual count and complete details, and the results of the actual count shall be reconciled with the property and accounting records. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled immediately. From thereon, perform periodic inventory of PPE and prepare report on the results thereof, in accordance with existing regulations.
 The LCE should require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain ledger cards for each class and category of property and equipment. Work back on the previous years, based on the delivery receipt, acceptance and inspection report and other source documents prepare property ledger card indicating acquisition date, cost and the brief descriptions which serves as the subsidiary ledgers of fixed assets per classs and category. For the purpose of reconciling the accounting records the reported balance of PPE per Balance Sheet is the based amount of property per category. Based on ledger cards prepare lapsing schedule for the computation and monitoring of depreciation charges.
 The Treasurer Office to maintain Property Cards per class and category of property, plant and equipment indicating acquisition date, the brief descriptions work back on the previous years and reconcile with the records of the accounting office to determine the correctness of the property records. Update the property issue slip/PAR to check custodianship of the property.
 Drop the unserviceable and obsolete properties items from the books following the process prescribed by the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Management in Local Government as prescribed by COA Circular No. 92-386
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee was created.
Some reports were submitted timely.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 regarding the mandated functions of the Accountant in the disbursement of government funds. The Municipal Accountant should be personally involved in the accounting and internal audit functions of the municipality. By the very nature of her duties and responsibilities should exercise extra diligence in performing internal audit functions and be vigilant in the review of the required documents needed for the municipality’s transactions.
observations.managementAction: Some DVs were duly signed by the Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following corrective measures for the aforesaid deficiencies:
 Strict compliance on the documentary requirements under COA Circular 2012-001.
 Strictly comply Sec. 22, only employees with accumulated 15 days VL should be allowed to monetize and make sure that at least five (5) days balance is retained after monetization. In case employees wish to monetize 50% or more of their accumulated leave credits (VL & SL) require them to submit justifiable reasons and additional requirements as provided for under Sec. 23.
 Acting HRMO and Municipal Accountant should closely monitor leave credit balances of the employees, maintain and up-date leave cards of each employee.
 Stop the practice of allowing employees to monetize more than once during the year.
 Re-compute the leave credit balance of Ms. Nonita Sayang.
observations.managementAction: The management said that they only implemented subject criteria upon the receipt of the said findings from the COA.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our last year’s recommendation that:
 The management to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to a special trust fund pursuant to Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121 to ensure that funds are available for disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and maintain a separate bank account.
 The Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant should be submitted to this Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF and disclose the details of the unexpended balance in the Notes to the Financial Statements in the Year-End financial reports.
 Lastly, the Municipality should be judicious in the utilization of the LDRRMF, taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the said fund.
observations.managementAction: Separate bank account for LDRRMF was established.
observations.reason: However, funds were not yet transferred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to PCW-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. The Municipal Mayor and the GAD Focal Person should identify gender issues in accordance with the aforementioned guidelines to ensure that GAD Fund allocation shall be utilized for PPAs that directly address gender disparities and their total disbursement for identified PPAs should be equivalent to the required fund allocation for GAD. Submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report in the prescribed format and within the deadline so that timely review and evaluation of the same is facilitated.
observations.managementAction:
allocated the required allocation for GAD Fund.
They also address certain gender issues according to GAD Plan and Budget for 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality of Magsaysay to undertake the following remedial measures to regulate the granting of financial assistance:
 All cases should be referred to the MSWDO for eligibility evaluation and the case study report and should be prepared and be made part of the supporting documents of the claim.
 All disbursements for financial assistance should be supported with appropriate and complete documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
 Include in the guidelines of the grant of financial assistance the frequency of the grants to each individual client.
 The local Sanggunian should formulate guidelines in the granting of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The management has had observed the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of on-going government PPAs and those to be implemented during the year. The LCE should regularly submit the monitoring report and to submit at the beginning of the year a list of on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year.
observations.managementAction: Report on PPAs was made, however, the report was not submitted monthly.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant or Bookkeeper to submit to COA the disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents promptly or within the prescribed period, otherwise the transactions will be subjected for suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: Delayed submission was attributed to lack of signatures on supporting documents.
observations.reason: It was still attributed to the lack of signatures on DVs and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SLs by year for the Development Fund, Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and the collections and remittances of the collectors to facilitate verification and monitoring of the said accounts’ balances
observations.reason: The Accounting Office was still deficient in complying the appropriate form of SLs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:

Implement strictly and properly the provisions stipulated in COA Circular No.97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Monitoring of cash advances should be made to ensure settlement thereof at year-end. Likewise, management should cause the withholding of salaries and institution of other sanctions as provided in the circular incase the accountable officer failed to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a demand letter and to inform the claimants of the effect if not claimed within the scheduled period/time so that the liquidation reports will be submitted within the reglementary period.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to employees/ officials concerned and mostly had liquidated their cash advance.
observations.reason: The balance of the Cash Advance pertains travel of personnel in the later months of the year and Confidential Fund of the Municipal Mayor, the liquidation of which were already submitted to the Central Office but no Credit Notice were issued yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to stop granting honorarium to regular employees receiving regular salaries in adherence to existing laws and regulations pertaining to claims of honorarium.

We also recommended to refund the amount by the officers and employees receiving the honorarium.
We recommended management to attach supporting documents such as approved Program of Work (POW) and accomplishment reports.

We recommended management to submit copies of MOAs/Contracts and Purchase Orders within five working days upon approval. For MOAs get copies from the Source Agencies and furnish the office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended management to adjust/correct such inventories to each proper account basing on the required documentary reports as provided under existing accounting rules and regulations for a fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended management to ensure that the required financial reports and statements are submitted within the period prescribed by the Commission to facilitate timely review and prompt correction of errors and deficiencies. Otherwise, administrative disciplinary action must be initiated against officials and employees who shall continuously fail to comply with the requirements.

We recommended management to instruct the office/department/end-user to prepare PPMP for the BAC Secretariat to consolidate it into APP.  We also recommend management follow the procedure and forms in the preparation of PPMP and APP according to RA 9184.
We recommended management instruct the Focal Person to gather the data prior to its deadline so that the report be complied on or before its due date.

We recommended Management to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher 	(JEV) to correct the affected accounts in conformity with Section 04 (p), Chapter 2 of the New Government Accounting system (NGAS) for LGUs.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of the Section 79 of P.D 1445 on the disposal of the unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: The IIRUP has already been prepared but still no adjustment made on the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor for the proper titling of all the lands in the name of the Municipality. Likewise, follow-up from the Court the delay in issuing its decision for the Municipality’s Application for Registration of Title.

Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare the subsidiary ledgers/inventory of all lands to facilitate the verification/ reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Implementation on-going. Already filed application for titling of said lots in the court.

observations.recommendation:
1. The Municipality complies and submits the lacking documents required.
2. The BAC strictly adheres to the revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Although Management has been diligent in its submission of reports & supporting documents, however, it was noted that there were still lacking attachments

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should strictly comply with the provisions of the Circular mentioned above. Adjusting entries should also be made to reclassify the said equipment into their proper accounts including its respective Accumulated Depreciation accounts.

The Implementing Office and other personnel concerned to expedite the implementation and accomplishment of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund so that these projects could immediately serve its purposes to the intended beneficiaries.

1. Submit proof of duly served invitations to mandated observers in all stages of procurements aside from the pre-bid conference and opening of bids. In the event none were made, written justification may be submitted.
2. Henceforth, strictly comply with RA 9184 and its IRR and make part of contract documents proof of duly served written invitations to mandated observers in all stages of procurements that will be undertaken by the Municipality.

1. Maintain the requied Work, Other Animals and Breding Stocks Property Cards detailing the acquisition, addition and disposition of these animals;
2. Reconcile the property cards with the accounting records and the inventory report; Submit a report to the Municipal Mayor copy furnished the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team Leader (COA) to confirm the existence and accuracy of these accounts as of December 31, 2014 and to enable the offices concerned to take necessary action if these accounts are already non-existent.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the following: Prior Years Adjustment 311,000.00 Land 311,000.00 To reclassify barangay sites from Land account to Prior Year’s Adjustments due to erroneous recording of previous transactions.
2. Direct the personnel concerned to process the transfer of ownership of various sites from the sellers to the Municipality.
3. Direct the personnel concerned to cause the return of TCTs that are with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
1. Ensure that the essential elements in GAD planning and budgeting are present or strengthened in the Municipality’s GPB; 
2. Direct the GAD Focal Point System and other personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2012-01 so that the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring, evaluation and accounting of results of the Municipality’s annual GPB and GAD Accomplishment Reports will be in order.
observations.reason: Due to non-consideration in the 2016 AIP. However, for CY 2017, the GAD mandatory obligation was already implemented.

1. Direct the personnel concerned to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9003 particularly Sections 32 and 37 thereof.
2. Direct the MENRO-Designate, the MPDO, and the Budget Officer to come up with a Comprehensive Solid Waste Management Program with sufficient budgetary requirements.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Agriculture Officer to conduct a physical count of the Work/Other Animals and the Breeding Stocks accounts and submit the inventory report to the Municipal Mayor copy furnished the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team Leader (COA) to confirm the existence and accuracy of these accounts as of December 31, 2013 and to enable the offices concerned to take necessary action if these accounts are already non-existent.
